29.38: "This is what you are to offer on the altar continually： two one year old lambs each day.
29.39: "You are to offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight,
29.40: and there shall be a tenth measure of choice flour mixed with one quart of oil extracted by hand, and one quart of wine as a drink offering for one lamb.
29.41: You are to offer the other lamb at twilight with the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning. You are to offer it as a soothing aroma, an offering by fire to the Lord.
29.42: It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the doorway to the Tent of Meeting in the Lord's presence, where I'll meet with you to speak to you there.
29.43: "I'll meet there with the Israelites, and it shall be consecrated bymyglory.
29.44: I'll consecrate the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and I'll consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve as my priests.
29.45: I'll dwell among the Israelites, and I'll be their God.
29.46: They shall know that I am the Lord their God who brought them out of Egypt so I might dwell among them. I am the Lord your God." 2944

30.1: "You are to make an altar for burning incense. You are to make it of acacia wood.
30.2: It shall be a square, one and a half feet long and one and a half feet wide, and it shall be three feet high, with its horns of one piece with it.
30.3: You are to overlay it with pure gold, its top, its sides all around, and its horns, and you are to make a molding of gold all around it.
30.4: "You are to make two gold rings for it under its molding; you are to make them on its two opposite sides, and they are to be holders for poles by which to carry it.
30.5: You are to make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
30.6: You are to put the altar in front of the curtain that is over the Ark of the Testimony, in front of the Mercy Seat that is over the Testimony where I'll meet with you.
30.7: Aaron shall offer fragrant incense on it. Every morning when he trims the lamps he shall offer it,
30.8: and when Aaron sets up the lamps at twilight, he shall offer it as a continual incense offering in the Lord's presence throughout your generations.
30.9: You are not to offer strange incense, a burnt offering, or a grain offering on it, nor shall you pour out a libation on it.
30.10: Each year Aaron shall make atonement on its horns with the blood of the sin offering of atonement. He shall make atonement on it each year throughout your generations. It is most holy to the Lord.
30.12: "When you take a census of the Israelites to register them, each shall give a ransom for himself to the Lord when they're registered so there won't be a plague among them when they're registered.
30.13: This is what everyone who is registered shall give： half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs), half a shekel as a contribution to the Lord.
30.14: All who are registered, twenty years of age and older, shall give a contribution to the Lord.
30.15: The rich shall not give more nor shall the poor give less than the half shekel, when you give a contribution to the Lord to make atonement for yourselves.
30.16: You are to take the atonement money from the Israelites and give it for the service of the Tent of Meeting, and it shall be a memorial for the Israelites in the Lord's presence to make atonement for yourselves."13
30.18: "You are to make a bronze basin with a bronze base for washing. You are to put it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar and put water in it,
30.19: and Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet from it.
30.20: When they enter the Tent of Meeting or when they approach the altar to minister to make an offering by fire to the Lord, they are to wash with water so they don't die.
30.21: They shall wash their hands and their feet so that they don't die, and it shall be for them a perpetual ordinance for Aaron and his seed from generation to generation."
30.23: "You are to take for yourself the finest spices： 500 shekels of liquid myrrh, half as much fragrant cinnamon (250 shekels), 250 shekels of fragrant reeds,
30.24: 500 shekels of cassia - all according to the shekel of the sanctuary - and a gallon of olive oil.
30.25: You are to make them into a holy anointing oil, a perfume mixture made by a perfumer. It shall be a holy anointing oil.
30.26: "With it you are to anoint the Tent of Meeting, the Ark of Testimony,
30.27: the table and all its utensils, the lamp stand and its utensils, the altar of incense,
30.28: the altar for burnt offerings and all its utensils, and the basin and its base.
30.29: You are to consecrate them and they are to be most holy. Whatever touches them shall be holy.
30.30: You are to anoint Aaron and his sons, and you are to consecrate them to serve as my priests.
30.31: You are to speak to the Israelites saying, 'This shall be holy anointing oil for me from generation to generation.
30.32: It is not to be poured out on a person's body, nor shall you make anything like it with similar formulations. It is holy, and it shall be holy to you.
30.33: Anyone who mixes anything like it or who puts any of it on an unqualified person shall be cut off from his people.'"
30.34: The Lord said to Moses, "Take for yourself spices： stacte, onycha, galbanum, and spices with pure frankincense, all in equal amounts. 3
30.35: You are to make it into a fragrant incense, expertly blended, pure, and holy.
30.36: You are to grind some of it fine, and put some before the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting where I shall meet with you. It shall be most holy to you.
30.37: You are not to make the incense that you make in this formulation for your own use. It shall be holy to the Lord for you.
30.38: Anyone who makes anything like it to use it as perfume shall be cut off from his people."

31.2: "Look, I've called Uri's son Bezalel, grandson of Hur from Judah's tribe
31.3: and I've filled him with the Spirit of God, with wisdom, understanding, knowledge, and all kinds of craftsmanship
31.4: to create plans for work in gold, silver, and bronze,
31.5: and for cutting stones to set them, for carving wood, and for doing all kinds of craftsmanship.
31.6: "I'm now appointing with him Ahisamach's son Oholiab from the tribe of Dan, and I've given wisdom to all who are skilled so they can make everything that I've commanded you,
31.7: including the Tent of Meeting, the Ark of Testimony, the Mercy Seat that is on it, all the furnishings of the tent -
31.8: the table and its furnishings, the lamp stand of pure gold, all its furnishings, the altar of incense,
31.9: the altar for burnt offerings, its furnishings, the basin, its base,
31.10: the woven garments, the holy garments of Aaron the priest, the garments of his sons as they serve as priests,
31.11: the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense for the Holy Place. They are to make them in accordance with everything that I commanded you."31
31.13: "You are to speak to the Israelites： 'You are to surely observe my Sabbaths because it's a sign between me and you from generation to generation, so you may know that I am the Lord who sanctifies you.
31.14: You are to observe the Sabbath, because it's holy for you. Whoever profanes it shall surely die; indeed, whoever does work on it shall be cut off from among his people.
31.15: Work may be done for six days, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to the Lord. Whoever does work on the Sabbath shall surely die.
31.16: The Israelites shall keep the Sabbath to make the Sabbath observance a perpetual covenant from generation to generation.
31.17: It is a sign forever between me and the Israelites, for in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, but on the seventh day he rested and was refreshed.'"3113
31.18: When he finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of Testimony, tablets of stone written by the finger of God.

32.1: When the people saw that Moses took a long time to come down the mountain, they gathered around Aaron and told him, "Come here and make us a god who will go before us, because, as for this fellow Moses who led us out of the land of Egypt, we don't know what has become of him."
32.2: Aaron said to them, "Tear off the gold rings which are in the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters and bring them to me."
32.3: All the people tore off the gold rings which were in their ears and brought them to him.
32.4: He took them from them and, using a tool, fashioned them into a molten calf. The people said, "This, Israel, is your god who brought you out of the land of Egypt."
32.5: When Aaron saw this, he built an altar in front of it, and then he proclaimed, "Tomorrow shall be a festival to the Lord."
32.6: They got up early the next day and offered burnt offerings and brought peace offerings. Then the people sat down to eat and drink, and then they got up to play.
32.7: The Lord said to Moses, "Go down immediately, for your people whom you led out of Egypt have behaved corruptly.
32.8: They have been quick to turn aside from the way I commanded them, and they have made for themselves a molten calf. They have bowed down to it in worship, they have offered sacrifices to it, and they have said, 'This, Israel, is your god who brought you out of the land of Egypt.'"
32.9: Then the Lord said to Moses, "I've seen these people and indeed they're obstinate.
32.10: Now let me alone so that my anger may burn against them and that I may consume them, but I'll make a great nation of you."
32.11: But Moses implored the Lord his God： "Lord, why are you angry with your people whom you brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and a show of force -
32.12: Why should the Egyptians say, 'He brought them out with an evil intention to kill them in the mountains and to destroy them from the face of the earth' -  Turn from your anger and change your mind about the calamity against your people.
32.13: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants to whom you swore by yourself as you said to them, 'I'll increase the number of your descendants like the stars of the heavens, I'll give your descendants all of this land about which I have spoken, and they are to possess it forever.'"
32.14: So the Lord changed his mind about the calamity he had said he would bring on his people.
32.15: Then Moses turned and went down the mountain with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hand, tablets which were written on both sides. They were written on one side and the other.
32.16: The tablets were the work of God and the writing was God's writing, inscribed on the tablets.
32.17: When Joshua heard the sound of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, "The sound of war is coming from the camp."16
32.18: Moses said, "It is not the sound of a victory shout, and it's not the sound of a shout of defeat, but it's the sound of singing that I hear."
32.19: As Moses approached the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, he became angry. He threw the tablets from his hands and shattered them at the base of the mountain.
32.20: He took the calf that they had made, burned it with fire, and ground it into powder. He scattered it on the water and made the Israelites drink it.
32.21: Then Moses said to Aaron, "What did this people do to you that you brought such great sin upon them?"
32.22: Aaron said, "Sir, don't be angry. You know the people - that they're intent on evil. 23They told me, 'Make a god for us who will go before us because, as for this fellow Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we don't know what has become of him.'
32.24: So I said to them, 'Whoever has gold ornaments, tear them off.' When they gave it to me, I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf."
32.25: When Moses saw that the people were out of control - for Aaron had let them get out of control, something that brought ridicule from their enemies -
32.26: he stood in the gate of the camp and called out： "Whoever is for the Lord come over to me," and all the sons of Levi gathered around him.
32.27: He said to them, "This is what the Lord, the God of Israel, says, 'Every man put his sword on his thigh, and go back and forth from gate to gate in the camp, and each of you kill his brother and friend and neighbor.'"32
32.28: The Levites did just as Moses told them, and about 3,000 people died that day.
32.29: Moses said, "You have been ordained to serve the Lord today, and you have brought a blessing on yourselves today because every man opposed his son or brother."
32.30: The next day Moses said to the people, "You committed a great sin, and now I'll go up to the Lord, and perhaps I can make atonement for your sin."
32.31: Moses returned to the Lord and said, "Please, Lord, this people committed a great sin by making a god of gold for themselves.
32.32: Now, if you will, forgive their sin - but if not, blot me out of your book which you have written."
32.33: The Lord said to Moses, "Whoever sins against me, I'll blot him out of my book.
32.34: Now, go, and lead the people where I told you, and now my angel will go before you, but on the day when I do punish, I'll punish them for their sin."
32.35: Then the Lord sent a plague on the people because they made the calf (the one Aaron made).

33.1: The Lord said to Moses, "Go up from here, you and the people whom you brought out of Egypt, to the land about which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob saying, 'I'll give it to your descendants.'
33.2: I'll send an angel in front of you and I'll drive out the Canaanites, the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
33.3: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey, but I won't go up among you, because you are an obstinate people, and otherwise I might consume you along the way."
33.4: When the people heard this troubling word, they mourned, and no one put on his ornaments.
33.5: The Lord had said to Moses, "Say to the Israelites, 'You are an obstinate people, and if for one moment I went up among you, I would put an end to you. Now take off your ornaments so I may decide what to do with you.'"
33.6: So the Israelites did not wear their ornaments from Mount Horeb onward.5
33.7: Moses used to take the tent and set it up outside the camp at a distance from the camp, and he called it the Tent of Meeting. When anyone sought the Lord, he would go out to the Tent of Meeting which was outside the camp.
33.8: When Moses would go out to the tent, all the people would get up, and each would stand in the doorway of his tent, watching Moses until he entered the tent.
33.9: When Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would come down and stand at the doorway of the tent while God spoke with Moses.
33.10: When all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the doorway of the tent, all of them would get up and prostrate themselves in worship, each one at the doorway of his tent.
33.11: The Lord would speak to Moses face to face just as a man speaks with his friend. When Moses returned to the camp, Nun's son Joshua, his young servant, would not leave the tent.
33.12: Moses said to the Lord, "Look, you have told me, 'Bring up this people,' but you haven't let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, 'I know you by name,' and also, 'You have found favor in my sight.'
33.13: Now, if I've found favor in your sight, please show me your ways so I may know you in order to find favor in your sight. And remember, this nation is your people."
33.14: He said, "My presence will go with you, and I'll give you rest."
33.15: Then Moses said to the Lord, "If your presence does not go with us, don't bring us up from here.
33.16: For how shall it be known that your people and I have received favor from you, unless you go with us and that we, your people and I, are distinguished from all the people on the face of the earth?"
33.17: The Lord said to Moses, "I'll do the very thing that you have said, because you have found favor in my sight and I know you by name."
33.18: Then Moses said, "Please show me your glory."
33.19: God said, "I'll cause all my goodness to pass before you, and I'll proclaim the name 'the Lord' before you. I'll be gracious to whom I'll be gracious, and I'll show compassion on whom I'll show compassion.
33.20: But," he said, "You cannot see my face, because a man cannot see me and live.
33.21: The Lord said, "Look, there is a place near me where you can stand on the rock;
33.22: and as my glory passes by, I'll put you in a crevice in the rock, and cover you with my hand until I've passed by.
33.23: Then I'll remove my hand so you may see my back, but my face must not be seen."33

34.1: The Lord said to Moses, "Cut out for yourself two stone tablets like the first ones, and I'll write on the tablets the words which were on the first tablets which you broke.
34.2: Be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning on Mount Sinai, where you are to present yourself to me there on the top of the mountain.
34.3: No one shall come up with you, nor shall anyone be seen anywhere on the mountain. Also, the sheep and cattle shall not graze in front of that mountain."
34.4: Moses cut out the two stone tablets like the first ones, got up early in the morning, and climbed Mount Sinai, just as the Lord had commanded him. He took with him the two stone tablets.
34.5: The Lord came down in a cloud and stood there with him and proclaimed the name of the Lord.
34.6: The Lord passed in front of him and proclaimed, "The Lord, the Lord God  is compassionate and gracious. He is slow to anger, and filled with gracious love and truth.
34.7: "He graciously loves thousands, and forgives iniquity, transgression, and sin. But he does not leave the guilty unpunished, visiting the iniquity of the ancestors on their children, and on their children's children to the third and fourth generation."
34.8: Moses quickly bowed to the ground and prostrated himself in worship.
34.9: He said, "If I've found favor in your sight, Lord, please, Lord, walk among us. Certainly this is an obstinate people, but pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your own inheritance."
34.10: Then the Lord said, "Now I'm going to make a covenant. Before all your people I'll do miraculous deeds that haven't been done in all the earth or in any nation. All the people among whom you live will see the work of the Lord, for it's an awesome thing that I'll do with you.
34.11: Obey what I am commanding you today. I'll drive out from before you the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
34.12: Be very careful not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land to which you are going, so they won't be a snare among you.
34.13: Rather, you are to tear down their altars, you are to smash their sacred pillars, and you are to cut down their sacred poles -
34.14: indeed, you are not to bow down in worship to any other god, because the Lord's name is Jealous - he's a jealous God -
34.15: Otherwise, you may make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land and when they prostitute themselves with their gods and offer sacrifices to their gods, someone may invite you and then you may eat some of their sacrifices.
34.16: You are not to take any of their daughters for your sons. Otherwise, when their daughters prostitute themselves with their gods, they may cause your sons to prostitute themselves with their gods."
34.17: "You are not to make molten gods for yourselves.
34.18: "You are to observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days, at the appointed time in the month Abib, you are to eat unleavened bread as I commanded you, for in the month Abib you came out of Egypt.
34.19: "Everything firstborn belongs to me： all the males of your herds, the firstborn of both cattle and sheep.
34.20: You are to redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a sheep, and if you don't redeem it, you are to break its neck. You are to redeem every firstborn of your sons, and no one shall appear before me empty-handed.19
34.21: "For six days you are to work, but on the seventh day you are to rest; even during plowing time and harvest you are to rest.
34.22: "You are to observe the Festival of Weeks, the first fruits of the wheat harvest, and the Festival of Ingathering at the turn of the year.
34.23: Three times during the year all your males shall appear in the presence of the Lord God of Israel.
34.24: For I'll drive out nations before you, and enlarge your borders, and no one will covet your land when you go up to appear in the presence of the Lord your God three times a year.
34.25: "You are not to offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened, nor shall you allow the sacrifice of the Festival of Passover to remain until morning.
34.26: "You are to bring the best of the first fruits of the ground to the house of the Lord your God. "You are not to boil a young goat in its mother's milk."
34.27: Then the Lord said to Moses, "Write down these words, for I'm making a covenant with you and with Israel according to these words."
34.28: While Moses was there with the Lord for 40 days and 40 nights, he did not eat or drink. He wrote the Ten Commandments, the words of the covenant, on the tablets.
34.29: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai, he had the two tablets in his hand, and he did not know that the skin of his face was ablaze with light because he had been speaking with God.
34.30: Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses and immediately noticed that the skin of his face was shining, and they were afraid to come near him.
34.31: When Moses called to them, Aaron and the leaders of the congregation returned to him, and he spoke to them.
34.32: Afterwards all the Israelites came near and he gave them everything the Lord told him on Mount Sinai as commandments.3430
34.33: When Moses finished speaking with them he put a veil over his face,
34.34: and then whenever Moses would come in the Lord's presence to speak with him, he would remove the veil until he left the Lord's presence. When he went out, he would tell the Israelites what he had been commanded.
34.35: The Israelites would see the face of Moses and that the skin of his face shone; then Moses would put the veil back over his face until he went in to speak with God.

35.1: M oses assembled the entire congregation of the Israelites and said to them, "These are the things that the Lord has commanded you to do：
35.2: For six days work shall be done, but on the seventh day you are to have a holy day, a Sabbath of complete rest in dedication to the Lord. Anyone who does work on that day shall be executed.
35.3: You are not to light a fire in any of your dwellings on the Sabbath."
35.4: Then Moses said to the entire congregation of the Israelites, "This is what the Lord has commanded,
35.5: 'Take from among yourselves an offering for the Lord. Everyone whose heart is willing shall bring it as an offering for the Lord： gold, silver, and bronze;
35.6: blue, purple, and scarlet material; fine linen and goat hair;
35.7: ram skins dyed red, dolphin skins, acacia wood,
35.8: oil for lighting, spices for the anointing oil and for aromatic incense,
35.9: onyx stones, and stones for setting in the ephod and the breast piece.
35.10: 'Let everyone who is skilled among you come and make everything that the Lord has commanded：
35.11: the tabernacle, its tent, its covering, its clasps, its boards, its bars, its pillars, and its sockets,
35.12: the ark, its poles, the Mercy Seat, the curtain,
35.13: the table, its poles, all its furnishings, and the bread of the presence,
35.14: the lamp stand for light, its furnishings, its lamps, and oil for the light,
35.15: the altar of incense, its poles, the anointing oil, the aromatic incense, and the screen for the doorway at the entrance to the tabernacle,
35.16: the altar for burnt offerings, the bronze lattice for it, its poles, and all its furnishings, the basin and its base,
35.17: the hangings for the court, its pillars, its sockets, the screen for the gate of the court,
35.18: the pegs for the tabernacle, the pegs for the court, and their cords,
35.19: the woven garments for ministering in the Holy Place, the holy garments of Aaron the priest and the garments of his sons for serving as priests.'"
35.20: Then the entire congregation of the Israelites withdrew from Moses' presence,
35.21: and every person whose heart moved him and all whose spirits prompted them, brought an offering to the Lord for constructing the Tent of Meeting, for all its service, and for the holy garments.
35.22: Both the men and women came, all whose hearts prompted them, and brought brooches, earrings, rings, pendants, and all kinds of gold jewelry. Every person presented a wave offering of gold to the Lord.
35.23: Everyone who had blue, purple, and scarlet material, fine linen, goat hair, ram skins dyed red, and dolphin skins brought them.
35.24: Everyone who could give an offering of silver and bronze brought it as a contribution for the Lord. Also all who had acacia wood for any use in the work brought it.22
35.25: Every skilled woman spun with her hands, and brought what she spun： blue, purple, and scarlet material, and fine linen.
35.26: All the women who were skilled artisans spun the goat hair.
35.27: The leaders brought onyx stones and stones to be set in the ephod and the breast piece,
35.28: spices and oil for the light and for the anointing oil and the aromatic incense.
35.29: Each Israelite man and woman whose heart was prompted brought something as a freewill offering to the Lord for all the work which the Lord had commanded them to do through Moses.
35.30: Moses said to the Israelites, "Look, the Lord has called Uri's son Bezalel, grandson of Hur, from the tribe of Judah,
35.31: and he has filled him with the Spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, and with knowledge of all kinds of work,
35.32: to make artistic designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,
35.33: to cut stones for setting, to carve wood, and to engage in all kinds of artistic work.
35.34: And he has given both him and Ahisamach's son Oholiab from the tribe of Dan the ability to teach.
35.35: He has equipped them to do all kinds of work done by an engraver, designer, embroider in blue, purple and scarlet material and in fine linen, or as a weaver. They were able to do all kinds of work and were skilled designers.

36.1: Bezalel and Oholiab and all the skilled craftsmen to whom the Lord gave wisdom and understanding to know how to do all the work in constructing the sanctuary shall do everything that the Lord has commanded."3
36.2: Then Moses summoned Bezalel, Oholiab, and all the skilled people to whom the Lord had given ability, including everyone whose hearts stirred them to come forward to do the work.
36.3: They received from Moses all the offerings that the Israelites had brought for doing the work of constructing the sanctuary, and the people continued to bring freewill offerings every morning.
36.4: All the craftsmen who were doing all the work on the sanctuary left the work they were doing
36.5: and said to Moses, "The people are bringing much more than enough for the work that the Lord has commanded us to do."
36.6: Then Moses issued an order, and the message was taken throughout the camp, "Men and women, don't bring more offerings for the sanctuary." The people were restrained from bringing any more,
36.7: since the material was more than sufficient for doing all the work.
36.8: All the skilled craftsmen among the workers made the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine woven linen, blue, purple, and scarlet material. He made them with cherubim skillfully worked into them.
36.9: The length of each curtain was 42 feet, and the width of each curtain six feet. All the curtains had the same measurements.
36.10: He joined five of the curtains together, and the other five curtains he joined together.
36.11: He made loops of blue material along the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set, and likewise, he made loops along the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
36.12: He made 50 loops in the one curtain, and he made 50 loops along the edge of the curtain that is in the second set, with the loops opposite each other.
36.13: Then he made 50 gold clasps, and joined the curtains to each other with the clasps so the tabernacle was one piece.
36.14: He made curtains of goat hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made 11 curtains.
36.15: The length of each curtain was 45 feet, and the width of each curtain six feet; the measurements of each of the eleven curtains was the same.
36.16: He joined five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.
36.17: He made 50 loops along the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set, and 50 loops along the edge of the curtain of the other set.
36.18: He made 50 bronze clasps to join the tent together so it would be one piece.
36.19: Then he made a cover for the tent of ram skins dyed red and a covering of dolphin skins above that.
36.20: Then he made upright boards of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
36.21: Each board was fifteen feet long, and 27 inches wide.
36.22: Each board had two pegs, joined to one another, and he did this for all the boards of the tabernacle.
36.23: He made the boards for the tabernacle： twenty boards for the south side.
36.24: He made 40 silver sockets under the twenty boards： two sockets under one board for its two pegs and two sockets under the next board for its two pegs.
36.25: For the second side of the tabernacle to the north he made twenty boards,
36.26: and 40 silver sockets for them, two sockets under one board and two sockets under the next board.
36.27: For the rear of the tabernacle on the west he made six boards,
36.28: and he made two boards for the rear corners of the tabernacle.
36.29: They were joined together at the bottom and they were connected on top, by one ring. He did this for the two of them, and they were the two corners.
36.30: There were eight boards with their sixteen silver sockets, two sockets under each board.22
36.31: Then he made bars of acacia wood, five for the boards on one side of the tabernacle,
36.32: five bars for the boards on the second side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards on the back side of the tabernacle to the west.
36.33: He made the middle bar in the center of the boards pass through from end to end.
36.34: He overlaid the boards with gold, and made gold rings for them as holders for the bars, and he overlaid the bars with gold.36
36.35: He made a curtain of blue, purple, and scarlet material, and fine woven linen. He made it with cherubim skillfully worked into it.
36.36: He made four pillars of acacia for it and overlaid them with gold, along with their gold hooks, and he cast four silver sockets for them.
36.37: For the doorway of the tent, he made a screen of blue, purple, and scarlet material and fine woven linen, the work of an embroiderer,
36.38: and five pillars of acacia along with their hooks. He overlaid their tops and their bands with gold. Their five sockets were of bronze.

37.1: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood 45 inches long, 27 inches wide, and 27 inches high.
37.2: He overlaid it with pure gold, inside and outside, and made a gold molding around it.
37.3: He cast four rings for it on its four feet, two rings on one side of it and two rings on its other side.
37.4: He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
37.5: He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry the ark.
37.6: He made a Mercy Seat of pure gold 45 inches long and 27 inches wide.
37.7: He made two cherubim of gold; he made them of hammered work at the two ends of the Mercy Seat.
37.8: One cherub was at one end and one cherub at the other end. He made the cherubim at the two ends of the Mercy Seat and of one piece with it.
37.9: The cherubim had their wings spread upward, covering the Mercy Seat with their wings and facing each other. The faces of the cherubim were turned toward the Mercy Seat.
37.10: Then he made a table of acacia wood three feet long, one and a half feet wide, and 27 inches high.
37.11: He overlaid it with pure gold and put a gold molding around it.
37.12: He made a three-inch wide rim around it, and made a gold molding around the rim.
37.13: He cast four gold rings for it and put the rings on the four corners where its four feet were.
37.14: The rings were close to the rim as holders for the poles to carry the table.
37.15: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold to carry the table.
37.16: He made the utensils which were on the table, its plates, dishes, bowls, and jars out of which libations are poured. He made them of pure gold.
37.17: He made the lamp stand of pure gold. He made the lamp stand, its base, and stem of hammered work and its cups, calyxes, and flowers were of one piece with it.
37.18: Six branches extended from its sides, three branches of the lamp stand from one side of it, and three branches of the lamp stand from its other side.
37.19: Three cups shaped like almond blossoms with calyxes and flowers were on one branch and three cups shaped like almond blossoms with calyxes and flowers were on the other branch, and so on for the six branches extending from the lamp stand.
37.20: On the lamp stand itself there were four cups shaped like almond blossoms each with their calyxes and flowers.
37.21: A calyx was under the two branches that extended out of the stem; a calyx was under the next pair of branches that extended out of the stem; and a calyx was under the last pair of branches that extended out of the stem, and so on for the six branches extending from the lamp stand.
37.22: Their calyxes and their branches were of one piece with it, all of it was of one piece of hammered work of pure gold.
37.23: He made its seven lamps, its tongs and its trays of pure gold.
37.24: He made it and all of its furnishings from a talent of pure gold.23
37.25: He made the altar for burning incense of acacia wood, a square, one and a half feet long, one and a half feet wide, and three feet high, with its horns of one piece with it.
37.26: He overlaid it with pure gold, its top, its sides all around, and its horns, and he made a gold molding around it.
37.27: He made two gold rings for it under its molding, on its two opposite sides as holders for poles by which to carry it.
37.28: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
37.29: And he made the holy anointing oil and the pure aromatic incense, the work of a perfumer.37

38.1: Then he made the altar for burnt offerings of acacia wood. It was a square, seven and a half feet long and seven and a half feet wide, and it was four and a half feet high.
38.2: He made horns on its four corners. Its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
38.3: He made all the utensils for the altar, the pans, the shovels, the bowls, the forks, and the fire-pans, and he made all its utensils of bronze.
38.4: He made a lattice, a netting of bronze, for the altar. It was under its ledge, extending halfway up.
38.5: He cast four rings on the four ends of the bronze lattice as holders for the poles.
38.6: He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
38.7: And he put the poles through rings on the sides of the altar to carry it. He made it hollow, out of boards.
38.8: He made the bronze basin and its bronze base from mirrors contributed by the women who served in the doorway of the Tent of Meeting.
38.9: Then hemadethecourt.Onthesouth side the hangings for the court were of fine woven linen, 150 feet long.
38.10: He made their twenty pillars and their twenty sockets of bronze, while the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
38.11: The north side was 150 feet long, and its 20 pillars and 20 sockets were of bronze, and the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
38.12: For the west side there were hangings 75 feet long with their ten pillars and ten sockets. The hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
38.13: The east side was 75 feet long.
38.14: The hangings for one section were 22 and a half feet with their three pillars and three sockets,
38.15: and also for the second section. On either side of the gate of the court were hangings of 22 and a half feet with their three pillars and three sockets.
38.16: All the hangings around the court were of fine woven linen.
38.17: The sockets for the pillars were of bronze and the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver. Their tops were overlaid with silver, and all the pillars of the court were banded with silver.
38.18: The screen of the gate of the court was the work of an embroiderer of blue, purple and scarlet material, and fine woven linen. The length was 30 feet and it was seven and a half feet high along its width, corresponding to the hangings of the court.
38.19: Their four pillars and their four sockets were of bronze, and their hooks were of silver. Their tops were overlaid with silver and their bands were of silver.
38.20: All the pegs for the tabernacle and for all around the court were of bronze.17
38.21: Here is a summary of materials for the tabernacle of the Testimony that was compiled at Moses's direction, the work of the Levites under the direction of Aaron the priest's son Ithamar.
38.22: Now Uri's son Bezalel, grandson of Hur from the tribe of Judah, made everything that the Lord had ordered Moses to build.
38.23: With him was Ahisamach's son Oholiab from the tribe of Dan, an engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, and scarlet material, and of fine linen.21
38.24: All the gold that was used in the work, in all the work on the sanctuary, including the gold from the wave offering, totaled 29 talents, 730 shekels, according to the standard used in the sanctuary.
38.25: The silver from those of the congregation who were recorded totaled 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, according to the standard used in the sanctuary;
38.26: a beka a head (a beka is half a shekel, according to the standard used in the sanctuary) for everyone who went through the registration process from 20 years old and older. The total numbered 603,550 bekas.38
38.27: One hundred talents of silver were used to cast the sockets for the sanctuary and the sockets for the curtain, 100 sockets for 100 talents, a talent per socket.
38.28: And with 1,775 talents he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their tops, and made bands for them.
38.29: The bronze from the wave offering totaled 70 talents and 2,400 shekels.
38.30: With it he made the sockets for the doorway to the Tent of Meeting, the bronze altar, the bronze lattice for it, all the furnishings for the altar,
38.31: the sockets for all around the court, the sockets for the gate to the court, all the pegs for the sanctuary, and all the pegs for all around the court.

39.1: From the blue, purple, and scarlet material they made finely woven garments for ministering in the Holy Place, and they made the holy garments for Aaron, just as the Lord commanded Moses.
39.2: He made the ephod out of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet material and fine woven linen.
39.3: They hammered out gold sheets and cut off threads to work into the blue, purple, and scarlet material and into the fine linen, a work of skillful design.
39.4: They made connecting shoulder pieces for the ephod and attached them to its two edges.
39.5: The skillfully woven band that was on it was made like it, of one piece with it： of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet material, and fine woven linen, just as the Lord commanded Moses.
39.6: They prepared the onyx stones, engraved with the names of the sons of Israel like the engraving on a signet, and mounted them in settings of gold filigree.
39.7: He put them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel, just as the Lord commanded Moses.4
39.8: He made a breast piece, skillfully worked, like the work of the ephod： of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet material, and fine woven linen.
39.9: They made the breast piece square when folded double： nine inches in length and nine inches in width when folded double.8
39.10: They mounted on it four rows of stones. The first row was a row of carnelian, topaz, and emerald;
39.11: the second row ruby, sapphire, and crystal;
39.12: the third row jacinth, agate, and amethyst;
39.13: the fourth row beryl, onyx, and jasper. They were set in gold filigree when they were mounted.
39.14: The stones corresponded to the names of the sons of Israel, twelve stones corresponding to their names, with the engraving of a signet, each with the name of one of the twelve tribes.
39.15: They made chains of pure gold twisted like cords for the breast piece.
39.16: They made two settings of gold filigree and two gold rings, and they put the two rings on the two edges of the breast piece.
39.17: They put the two gold cords on the two gold rings at the edges of the breast piece,
39.18: and they attached the other two ends of the two cords to the filigree settings, and then attached them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod in front.
39.19: They made two gold rings and attached them to the two edges of the breast piece, on the side of it which is toward the inner side of the ephod.
39.20: They made two gold rings and attached them in front on the lower part of the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod close to the place where it's joined, above the skillfully woven band of the ephod.
39.21: They tied the breast piece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord so it would rest on the skillfully woven band of the ephod and so the breast piece would not come loose from the ephod.16
39.22: He made the robe of the ephod of woven work, entirely of blue.
39.23: The opening of the robe was in the middle, like the opening of a coat of mail, with a binding around the opening so it would not be torn.
39.24: On the hem of the robe they made pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet material and woven linen.
39.25: They made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates on the hem of the robe; all around between the pomegranates.
39.26: There was a bell and a pomegranate, then a bell and a pomegranate, all around the hem of the robe for when the High Priest ministered, just as the Lord commanded Moses.
39.27: For Aaron and his sons, they made tunics, woven of fine linen,
39.28: the turban of fine linen, decorated head coverings of fine linen, linen undergarments of fine woven linen,
39.29: the sash of fine woven linen, woven of blue, purple, and scarlet material, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.39
39.30: They made the medallion for the holy crown of pure gold, and they wrote on it an inscription like the engraving on a seal： "Holy to the Lord."
39.31: They fastened a blue cord to it in order to fasten it on the turban above, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.32: All the work on the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting was completed, and the Israelites had crafted it according to everything that the Lord had commanded Moses, as they should have.
39.33: They brought to Moses the tabernacle, the tent, all its furnishings, its clasps, its boards, its bars, its pillars, its sockets,
39.34: the covering of ram skins dyed red, the covering of dolphin skins, the curtain,
39.35: the Ark of the Testimony and its poles, the Mercy Seat,
39.36: the table and all its utensils, the bread of the presence,
39.37: the lamp stand of pure gold, its lamps (with the lamps in order), its furnishings, its oil for lighting,
39.38: the altar of gold, anointing oil, aromatic incense, the screen for the doorway to the tent,
39.39: the bronze altar and the bronze lattice for it, its poles, all its furnishings, the basin and its base,
39.40: the hangings for the court, its pillars, its sockets, the screen for the gate of the court, its cords, its pegs, all the furnishings for the service of the tabernacle, for the Tent of Meeting,
39.41: the woven garments for Aaron the priest for ministering in the Holy Place, and the garments for his sons for serving as priests.
39.42: The Israelites had done all the work according to all that the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.43: Moses blessed them when he saw all the work, and that they had done it. They had done it just as the Lord had commanded

40.2: "On the first day of the first month you are to set up the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.
40.3: You are to put the Ark of the Testimony there, and screen off the ark with the curtain.
40.4: You are to bring in the table and properly arrange what goes on it. Then you are to bring in the lamp stand and set up its lamps.
40.5: "You are to put the golden altar for incense in front of the Ark of the Testimony and then set up the screen for the doorway to the tabernacle.
40.6: You are to put the altar for burnt offerings in front of the doorway of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.
40.7: You are to put the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar and put water in it.
40.8: You are to set up the court all around, and hang up the screen for the gate of the court.
40.9: You are to take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it. You are to consecrate it and all its furnishings and it will be holy.8
40.10: "You are to anoint the altar for burnt offerings and all its utensils. You are to consecrate the altar and the altar will be most holy.
40.11: You are to anoint the basin and its base and consecrate it.
40.12: Then you are to bring Aaron and his sons to the doorway of the Tent of Meeting, and wash them with water.
40.13: You are to clothe Aaron with the holy garments, you are to anoint him, and consecrate him so he may serve me as priest.
40.14: You are to bring his sons and clothe them with tunics.
40.15: You are to anoint them just as you anointed their father so they may serve me as priests. Their anointing shall qualify them to belong to a perpetual priesthood from generation to generation."
40.16: Moses did everything that the Lord had commanded him, so he did.
40.17: And so in the first month of the second year, on the first day of the month, the tabernacle was set up.
40.18: Moses set up the tabernacle. He installed its sockets and set its boards in place. He inserted its bars and set up its pillars.
40.19: He spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent on top of it, just as the Lord had commanded him.
40.20: Then he took the Testimony, put it into the ark, and placed the poles on the ark. He then put the Mercy Seat on top of the ark.
40.21: He brought the ark into the tabernacle, set up the curtain, and screened off the Ark of the Testimony, just as the Lord had commanded him.
40.22: He put the table in the Tent of Meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the curtain,
40.23: and properly arranged the bread on it in the Lord's presence, just as the Lord had commanded him.
40.24: Then he put the lamp stand in the Tent of Meeting, opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle,
40.25: and set up the lamps in the Lord's presence, just as the Lord had commanded him.
40.26: He put the golden altar in the Tent of Meeting in front of the curtain
40.27: and burned aromatic incense on it, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.28: He set up the screen for the doorway of the tabernacle.
40.29: He put the altar for burnt offerings at the doorway of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting, and offered the burnt offering and the grain offering on it, just as the Lord had commanded him.
40.30: He put the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water in it for washing.
40.31: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and feet from it.
40.32: When they entered the Tent of Meeting and approached the altar, they washed, just as the Lord had commanded him.
40.33: He set up the court all around the tabernacle and the altar, and hung up the screen for the gate of the court. And so Moses finished the work.32
40.34: The cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
40.35: Moses was not able to enter the Tent of Meeting because the cloud had settled on it, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
40.36: Whenever the cloud was lifted up from the tabernacle, the Israelites would set out on their journey,
40.37: but if the cloud was not lifted up, they would not set out until it was lifted up.
40.38: For the cloud of the Lord was over the tabernacle by day, and the fire was in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel in all their journeys.

1.1: The Lord spoke to Moses from the midst of the Tent of Meeting：
1.2: "Speak to the Israelites and tell them, When any person brings an offering to the Lord from among you, whether he brings on offering of animals from either cattle or flock,
1.3: if his offering is a burnt offering from the herd, he is to bring a male without any defect. He is to present it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. At the appointed time it is to be presented in the presence of the Lord so that he may be accepted.
1.4: He is to lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering, and it will be accepted for him as an atonement on his behalf.
1.5: Then he is to slaughter the young bull in the Lord's presence." "Aaron's sons, the priests, are to bring the blood and sprinkle it around the altar that stands at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
1.6: He is to skin the burnt offering and cut it into pieces.
1.7: Aaron's sons, the priests, are to build a fire on the altar and arrange the wood over the fire.
1.8: They are to arrange the pieces of meat - including the head and the fat - on the wood over the fire that burns on the altar.
1.9: Then he is to wash its entrails and legs with water. After this, the priest is to offer all of it on the altar - a burnt offering by fire, an aroma that will be pleasing to the Lord."
1.10: "If his offering is a burnt offering from the flock, whether lamb or goat, he is to bring a male without any defect
1.11: and slaughter it at the north side of the altar in the Lord's presence. Then Aaron's sons, the priests, are to sprinkle its blood around the altar.
1.12: He is to cut up its head and fat into separate pieces. Then the priest is to arrange them in rows on the wood over the fire that burns on the altar.
1.13: Then he is to wash its entrails and legs with water. After this, the priest is to offer all of it on the altar - a burnt offering by fire, an aroma that will be pleasing to the Lord."
1.14: "If his offering is a burnt offering of birds to the Lord, he is to bring turtledoves or young doves.
1.15: The priest is to bring it to the altar to offer it up in smoke. He is to decapitate it, drain its blood on the side of the altar,
1.16: and then he is to eviscerate it, and throw the viscera and the feathers to the east side of the altar, where the fatty ashes are located.
1.17: He is then to tear it open by its wings, but not dividing it completely into two parts. The priest is then to offer all of it on the wood over the fire as a burnt offering by fire, an aroma pleasing to the Lord."1

2.1: "When a person brings an offering - that is, a grain offering to the Lord, his offering is to consist of fine flour. He is to pour olive oil mixed with frankincense over it.
2.2: Then he is to bring it to Aaron's sons, the priests. He is to take a handful of fine flour, the olive oil, and all of the frankincense. Then the priest is to offer a memorial offering by fire, an aroma pleasing to the Lord.
2.3: The remnants from the grain offering is for Aaron and his sons - the holiest of the offerings made by fire to the Lord."
2.4: "When you bring an offering - that is, a grain offering baked in an oven, it is to consist of fine flour baked into unleavened bread mixed with olive oil or of wafers made of unleavened bread and smeared with olive oil.
2.5: "If your grain offering has been prepared on a griddle, then it is to consist of fine flour mixed with olive oil.
2.6: Crumble it into morsels of bread and then pour olive oil on it. It's a grain offering.
2.7: "When your grain offering has been prepared in a stew pan, it is to consist of fine flour mixed with olive oil.
2.8: Bring the grain offering that you prepared from these ingredients to the Lord. Present it to the priest, who will bring it to the altar.
2.9: Then the priest will dedicate some of the grain offering as a memorial offering and offer it in smoke on the altar, an offering by fire that will be a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
2.10: The remainder from the memorial offering is for Aaron and his sons - the holiest of the offerings made by fire to the Lord."
2.11: "Any grain offering that you bring to the Lord is not to be prepared with yeast, because anything with leaven and honey may not be offered in smoke as an offering by fire to the Lord.
2.12: You may bring them to the Lord as an offering of first-fruits, but they are not to be offered on the altar for a pleasing aroma."
2.13: "Also, be sure to rub every offering from your grain offering with salt. You are not to ever remove the salt of the covenant of your God from your grain offering. Present all your offerings with salt."
2.14: "Whenever you bring a grain offering of first-fruits to the Lord, bring fresh barley, roasted in fire, young kernels crushed into bits. Bring the grain offering with your first-fruits
2.15: and then pour olive oil and frankincense over it as a grain offering.
2.16: The priest is to offer the memorial offering in smoke - its crushed bits, olive oil, and frankincense - as an offering by fire to the Lord."

3.1: "If someone's offering is a peace offering from the cattle, the presenter is to offer it without defects, whether the animal is male or female. They are to be brought to the Lord.
3.2: Then the presenter is to lay his hand on the head of the offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. After this, Aaron's sons, the priests, are to sprinkle the blood on and around the altar.
3.3: "The presenter is then to bring a gift from the peace offering, an offering made by fire to the Lord. He is to remove the fat that covers the internal organs, all of the fat that is inside the internal organs,
3.4: the two kidneys with the fat on them by the loins, and the fatty mass that surrounds the liver and kidneys.
3.5: Then Aaron's sons are to burn them on the altar, over the burnt offering that has been placed on the wood, over the fire, as an offering made by fire, an aroma pleasing to the Lord.
3.6: "If his offering to the Lord is a peace offering from the flock, whether male or female, he is to bring them without defect.
3.7: If the offering that he is bringing is a lamb, then he is to bring it to the Lord.
3.8: He is to lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron's sons are to sprinkle the blood on and around the altar.
3.9: "The presenter is then to bring a gift from the peace offering as an offering made by fire to the Lord. He is to remove the fat, the entire fat tail near the spine, the fat that covers the internal organs, all of the fat that is inside the internal organs,
3.10: the two kidneys with the fat on them by the loins, and the fatty mass that surrounds the liver and kidneys.
3.11: Then the priest is to burn them on the altar as a food offering made by fire to the Lord.
3.12: "If his offering is a goat, then he is to bring it to the Lord,
3.13: lay his hand over its head, then slaughter it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. After this, Aaron's sons are to sprinkle the blood on and around the altar.
3.14: "The presenter is then to present the gift as an offering made by fire to the Lord, that is, the fat that covers the internal organs, all the fat that is inside the internal organs,
3.15: the two kidneys with the fat on them by the loins, and the fatty mass that surrounds the liver and kidneys.
3.16: The priest is to burn them on the altar, a food offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma. All the fat belongs to the Lord.314
3.17: "This is to be be a lasting statute for all your generations, wherever you live. You are not to eat any fat or blood. "

4.1: The Lord spoke to Moses：
4.2: "Speak to the Israelites and tell them, 'If a person inadvertently sins with respect to any of the Lord's commands that should not be violated, but nevertheless he disobeys one of them,
4.3: or if the anointed priest sins, thereby bringing guilt on the people, let him bring a young bull without defect as a sin offering to the Lord for his sin that he had committed.
4.4: "'He is to bring the bull to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, into the Lord's presence, where he is to lay his hand on the head of the bull and slaughter it in the Lord's presence.
4.5: The anointed priest is to take blood from the bull to the Tent of Meeting.
4.6: The priest is to dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times in the Lord's presence in front of the curtain of the sanctuary.
4.7: "'The priest is then to put some blood on the horn of the altar that is near the Tent of Meeting as an incense of pleasing aroma in the Lord's presence. He is to pour the rest of the bull's blood for a burnt offering at the base of the altar that is at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
4.8: Then he is to remove all the fat from the bull for a sin offering -  that is, the fat that covers the internal organs, all of the fat that is inside the internal organs,
4.9: the two kidneys with the fat on them by the loins, and the fatty mass the surrounds the liver and kidneys,
4.10: just as they are taken from the bull for a peace offering. Then the priest is to burn them on the altar for burnt offerings.
4.11: "'Now as for the bull's hide, its flesh, its head, its legs, its internal organs, its dung,
4.12: along with the rest of the bull, he is to bring it outside the camp to a clean place, where fat ashes are to be poured over it and then it is to be thoroughly burned over wood with fire. It is to be burned where the fat ashes are poured out.'"
4.13: "If the whole congregation of Israel goes astray, and if the sin is hidden from the eyes of the assembly, and if they will have gone astray from one of the Lord's commands that should not be violated, then they will stand guilty.
4.14: When the sin that they have committed becomes known, the entire congregation is to bring a young bull as a sin offering to the Tent of Meeting,
4.15: where the elders of the community are to lay their hands on the head of the bull in the Lord's presence and slaughter it.
4.16: The anointed priest is to take blood from the bull and bring it to the Tent of Meeting.
4.17: Then the priest is to dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times in front of the curtain in the Lord's presence.
4.18: "Then the priest is to put blood on the horn of the altar near the Tent of Meeting in the Lord's presence. He is to pour the rest of the blood as a burnt offering at the base of the altar that is at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
4.19: Then he is to remove all the fat from the bull for a sin offering and burn it on the altar.
4.20: "He is to do to the bull what he did to the bull for sin offering. He is to do it this way so that the priest will make atonement for them and they will be forgiven.
4.21: Then he is to bring the rest of the bull outside the camp and burn it just as he had burned the first bull. This is the sin offering for the congregation."
4.22: "When a ruler inadvertently sins, disobeying any one of the commands of the Lord his God that should not be violated, he will be guilty.
4.23: When the sin that he had committed is disclosed to him, he is to bring his offering： a male goat without defect.
4.24: He is then to lay his hand on the head of the goat and slaughter it at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered - in the Lord's presence - as a sin offering.
4.25: Then the priest is to take blood from the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horn of the altar that is used for burnt offerings. He is to pour the rest of the blood at the base of the altar that is used for burnt offerings.
4.26: But he is to burn all the fat on the altar as is done for the fat for the sacrifice of a peace offering. This is how the priest will make atonement for him concerning his sin. It will be forgiven him."
4.27: "If any of the common people of the land inadvertently sins by disobeying one of the Lord's commands that should not be violated, he will be guilty.
4.28: When the sin that he committed is disclosed to him, he is to bring his offering for his sin that he had committed： a female goat without defect.
4.29: He is to lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it at the place for burnt offering.
4.30: Then the priest is to take blood with his finger, put it on the horn of the altar that is used for burnt offerings, and then pour the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
4.31: He is to remove all the fat, just as the fat was removed from the sacrifice for peace offering. Then the priest is to burn it on the altar as a pleasing aroma to the Lord. This is how the priest will make atonement for him. It will be forgiven him.
4.32: "If he brings a lamb for his offering, he is to bring a female without defect.
4.33: He is to lay his hand on the head of the offering and slaughter it for a sin offering at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
4.34: Then the priest is to take blood with his finger and put it on the horn of the altar for burnt offering. Then he is to pour the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
4.35: Then the presenter is to remove all its fat, just as the fat was removed from the sacrifice of peace offering. The priest is to burn it on the altar over the offerings made by fire to the Lord. This is how the priest will make atonement for him concerning the sin that he had committed. It will be forgiven him."434

5.1: "If someone sins because he has received notice that he is to testify as a witness regarding what he has observed or learned, but then he fails to testify, he will be guilty.
5.2: When a person has touched a ceremonially unclean thing inadvertently, such as the carcass of an unclean animal, or some unclean creeping thing, he will be unclean and guilty nevertheless.
5.3: "When he inadvertently touches the uncleanness of a human being, whatever his uncleanness that made him unclean may be, when he himself comes to know about it, he will be guilty.
5.4: When a person has sworn inadvertently by what he has said, whether for evil or good, whatever it was that the person spoke, when he comes to understand what he said, he will incur guilt by of one of these things.
5.5: "When a person is guilty of one of these things, then he is to confess whatever sin it was
5.6: and bring compensation to the Lord for the guilt that he committed： a female from the flock - whether a lamb or goat - for a sin offering. Then the priest is to make atonement for him.
5.7: "If he can't afford a goat, then he is to bring for his sin offering two turtledoves or two young doves to the Lord： one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.
5.8: He is to bring them to the priest, who will offer a sin offering first. He is to wring off its head without separating it.
5.9: Then he is to spatter some of the blood from the sin offering on the sidewall of the altar. Now as to the remainder of the blood, he is to pour it out at the base of the altar for a sin offering.
5.10: "Now as to the second, he is to prepare it as a burnt offering according to the approved procedure. The priest is to make atonement for him on account of his sin that he had committed. Then it will be forgiven him.
5.11: "If he can't afford two turtledoves or two young doves, then he is to bring as his offering a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a sin offering for what he has committed. He is to put no olive oil or frankincense on it, since it's a sin offering.
5.12: He is to bring it to the priest. The priest is to take a handful as a memorial and burn it on the altar as an offering made by fire to the Lord. It's a sin offering.
5.13: The priest will make atonement for him, on account of the sin that he had committed in any of these things and it will forgiven him. As far as the priest is concerned, it will be a meal offering."
5.14: The Lord spoke to Moses：
5.15: "When a person commits a truly treacherous act and sins inadvertently concerning the sacred things of the Lord, then he is to bring a trespass offering from the flock to the Lord as compensation for his guilt. It is to be a ram without defect, estimated as to its value in silver shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
5.16: He is to compensate for whatever sin he had committed concerning the sacred things of the Lord, add a fifth part to it, and give it to the priest. The priest is to make atonement for him with the ram as a sin offering and he'll be forgiven.
5.17: "If a person sins and does what the Lord commanded is not to be done, and if he didn't know that he had sinned, then he will be guilty nevertheless.
5.18: He is to bring to the priest from the flock a ram without defect, estimated as to its value in silver shekels, as a guilt offering. Then the priest is to make atonement for him concerning his inadvertent act that he committed through ignorance, and it will be forgiven him. 19It's a sin offering for his guilt in the Lord's presence."

6.1: The Lord spoke to Moses：
6.2: "A person sins against the Lord by acting treacherously toward his neighbor regarding something entrusted to his care, security for a loan, robbery, or if he has oppressed his neighbor,
6.3: found something that had been lost and then lied about it, or if he makes a false oath about any of these things, thus commiting a sin with respect to these things.
6.4: "If that person has sinned and has been found guilty, then he is to return the stolen thing that he took or obtained by oppression, or the security that had been entrusted to him, or the lost thing that he had found,
6.5: or the thing about which he had given a false oath. He is to restore it in full, add a fifth to it, then give to whom it belongs the very day he's found guilty.
6.6: "Now as to his guilt offering, he is to bring to the Lord a ram without defect from the flock, estimated as to its value, to the priest.
6.7: Then the priest is to make atonement for him in the Lord's presence, and it will be forgiven him regarding whatever he did.
6.8: The Lord spoke to Moses：
6.9: "Deliver these orders to Aaron and his sons concerning the regulations for burnt offerings. The burnt offering is to remain on the hearth of the altar throughout the entire night until morning, and the fire on the altar is to be kept burning along with it.
6.10: "The priest is to clothe himself with linen robe and underclothes. Then he is to take the ashes of the burnt offering on the altar that had been consumed by the fire and set them beside the altar.
6.11: Then he is to change his clothes, dressing himself with a different set of clothes, and take the ashes to a clean place outside the camp.
6.12: "The fire on the altar is to be kept burning continuously without being extinguished. The priest is to burn wood on it every morning, arrange burnt offerings over it, and then burn the fat contained in the peace offerings over it.
6.13: The fire is to continue to burn on the altar and is never to be extinguished."
6.14: "This is the law concerning grain offerings. Aaron's sons are to offer it in the Lord's presence, in front of the altar.
6.15: He is to take a handful of fine flour for a grain offering, some olive oil, all of the frankincense on the grain offering, and make a sacrifice of smoke on the altar as a memorial portion, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
6.16: "Aaron and his sons are to eat what remains of the unleavened offering at this sacred place： the court of the tent of meeting.
6.17: It is not to be baked with leaven. I've given it as their portion out of my offerings made by fire. It's a most holy thing, like the sin and guilt offerings.
6.18: Every male of Aaron's sons is to eat it as a portion continuously allotted for your generations from the offerings made by fire to the Lord. Anyone who touches them is to be holy."
6.19: Then the Lord told Moses,
6.20: "This is the offering that Aaron and his sons are to offer to the Lord the day he is anointed： a tenth of an ephah of flour is to be offered throughout the day, half in the morning and half in the evening.
6.21: It is to be prepared with olive oil on a griddle. Once is has been mixed thoroughly, bake it, bring it in pieces, and offer it like a grain offering of broken pieces, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
6.22: The anointed priest who succeeds him from among his sons is to offer it. As a permanent statute, it is to be offered whole and made to smoke in the Lord's presence.
6.23: Every grain offering from a priest is to be burned whole. It is not to be eaten."
6.25: "Speak to Aaron and his sons. This is the regulation concerning sin offerings. Slaughter the sin offering in the same place where the whole burnt offering is slaughtered - in the Lord's presence. It's a most holy thing.
6.26: The priest who offers it as a sin offering is to eat it at a sacred place in the court of the tent of meeting.
6.27: Whoever touches its meat will be holy. "If some of its blood spatters on a garment, wash where it was spattered in a sacred place.
6.28: The earthen vessel in which it was boiled is to be broken, unless it was boiled in a bronze vessel, in which case it is to be polished very well and rinsed in water.
6.29: "Every male among the priests is to eat it. It's a most sacred thing.
6.30: Any sin offering from which its blood was brought to the tent of meeting to make atonement in the sacred place is not to be eaten. Instead, it is to be incinerated."

7.1: "This is the regulation concerning guilt offerings. It's most holy.
7.2: The guilt offering is to be offered in the same place where the burnt offering is slaughtered. The priest is to spatter some of its blood on the altar and around it.
7.3: As to all its fat, that is, the fat on the tail and the fat covering the internal organs, the one presenting the sacrifice is to offer it.
7.4: But the two kidneys, the fat over them by the loins, and the appendage on the liver is to be taken away, along with the kidneys.
7.5: Then the priest is to offer them on the altar, incinerating them with fire as a guilt offering to the Lord.
7.6: Any male among the priests may eat it, provided that it is eaten at a sacred place as a most holy thing.
7.7: The law for the sin offering is the same as the guilt offering. It belongs to the priest who made atonement with it.
7.8: "The hide from the burnt offering brought by the offeror is to belong to the priest.
7.9: Every grain offering that's baked on the oven and every thing that's prepared on a stew pan or on the frying pan belongs to the priest who offered it.
7.10: Furthermore, every grain offering that's mixed with olive oil or that's dry will be for Aaron's sons, each one like the other.
7.11: "This is the law concerning the sacrifice for peace offerings that are to be brought to the Lord.
7.12: If someone brings it to demonstrate thanksgiving, then he is to present along with the thanksgiving offering unleavened cakes mixed with olive oil, unleavened wafers spread with olive oil, and cakes of mixed fine flour with olive oil.
7.13: Along with the cakes of unleavened bread, he is to bring his thanksgiving offering with his peace offerings.
7.14: He is to present one from each grain offering, a separate offering to the Lord. It will belong to the priest who spatters the blood of the peace offering.
7.15: As to the meat contained in his peace offerings, it is to be eaten on the day it is offered. Nothing of it is to remain until morning.13
7.16: "If his sacrifice accompanies a fulfilled vow or is a voluntary offering, it is to be eaten on the day the offeror brings the sacrifice. Anything left over is to be eaten the next day,
7.17: but whatever remains uneaten from the meat of the sacrifice by the third day is to be incinerated.
7.18: "If any of the meat of his sacrifice of peace offerings is eaten on the third day, it won't be accepted for the one who brought it. It is to be considered as refuse, and whoever eats it will bear the punishment of his iniquity.
7.19: "Meat that comes in contact with a ceremonially unclean thing is not to be eaten. Incinerate it instead. As for ceremonially clean meat, any one who is clean may eat it.
7.20: But the person who eats meat from the sacrifice that belongs to the Lord, while still affected by his uncleanness is to be eliminated from contact with his people.
7.21: Any person who touches a ceremonially unclean thing - whether the uncleanness pertains to human beings, animals, or to creeping things - and then eats from the meat of peace offerings that belongs to the Lord is to be eliminated from contact with his people."
7.22: The Lord spoke to Moses：
7.23: "Tell the Israelites： You are not to eat the fat of an ox, a lamb, or a goat.
7.24: "The carcass of an animal that died of its own and an animal torn by wild beast may be used for any purpose except for eating.
7.25: "Anyone who eats the fat of an animal that has been offered by fire to the Lord is to be eliminated from contact with his people.
7.26: You are not to eat any form of blood in any of your dwellings, whether it's from birds or animals.
7.27: Any person who eats any form of blood is to be eliminated from contact with his people."
7.29: Tell the Israelites： "Whoever brings a peace offering sacrifice to the Lord is to bring his offering to the Lord from the sacrifice of his peace offerings.
7.30: He is to bring the offering made by fire with his own hands to the Lord. He is to bring the fat with the breast, since the breast is to be waved as a raised offering to the Lord.
7.31: The priest will burn the fat on the altar, but the breast belongs to Aaron and his sons.
7.32: From the sacrifices of your peace offerings give the right thigh to the priest as a raised offering to the Lord.
7.33: "The descendant of Aaron's sons who brings the blood from the peace offering and the fat will have the right thigh for his own portion,
7.34: since I've taken the breast and the thigh as raised offerings from the sacrifices of peace offerings of the Israelites and have given them to Aaron the priest and his sons as their perpetual portion from the Israelites."
7.35: This is the consecrated portion for Aaron and his descendants from the offerings made by fire to the Lord, the day they were presented to be priests to the Lord.
7.36: This is what the Lord had commanded to give them the day he anointed them from among the Israelites - a perpetual portion for their generations.
7.37: This is the regulation concerning burnt, grain, sin, guilt, and installation offerings, along with the sacrifice for peace offerings.
7.38: This is what the Lord had commanded Moses on Mount Sinai on the day when he commanded the Israelites to bring their offerings to the Lord in the Sinai wilderness.

8.1: The Lord spoke to Moses：
8.2: "Take Aaron, his sons with him, the clothing, the anointing oil, the bull for sin offering, two rams, and a basket of unleavened bread
8.3: and then assemble the entire congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
8.4: So Moses did just as the Lord had commanded him. He assembled the congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
8.5: Moses said to the congregation, "This is what the Lord commanded to be done."
8.6: Moses brought Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
8.7: Then he clothed Aaron with the tunic, girded him with the band for priests, clothed him with the robe, placed the ephod on him, girded him with the skillfully weaved band of the ephod, and bound it on him.
8.8: He set the breastplate on him and placed the urim and thumin on top of the breastplate.
8.9: Then he set the turban on his head, and on the turban at the front he set the golden plate, the sacred crown that the Lord had commanded.
8.10: After this, Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle, consecrating everything that was in it.
8.11: He spattered some on the altar seven times, then anointed the altar, all its vessels, the laver, and its base to consecrate them.
8.12: He then poured the oil of anointing on Aaron's head to anoint and consecrate him.
8.13: Then Moses brought Aaron's sons, clothed them with the tunic, girded them with the bands, and bound turbans on them just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
8.14: Next, he brought the bull for sin offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the bull's head for a sin offering.
8.15: So Moses slaughtered it, took the blood, and poured some of it at the horns of the altar and around it with his fingers, thus purifying the altar. Then he poured the blood at the base of the altar, thereby sanctifying it as a means to make atonement with it.
8.16: Moses burned on the altar all the fat on the internal organs, the appendage on the liver, the two kidneys, and the fat.
8.17: As to the bull and its fat, skin and offal, he incinerated them outside the camp, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
8.18: Next he brought the ram for whole burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.
8.19: Moses slaughtered it and poured its blood over the altar and around it.
8.20: As to the ram, he has cut it into parts at the joints. Moses burned the head, internal organs, and the fat.
8.21: Moses washed the internal organs and the thigh with water and then burned the entire ram on the altar as a whole burnt offering, a pleasing aroma of an offering made by fire to the Lord, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
8.22: Moses brought the ram, that is, the second of the rams for consecration. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.
8.23: Moses then slaughtered it, took some of its blood, and put it on Aaron's right earlobe, right thumb, and on his right great toe.
8.24: Then Moses brought Aaron's sons, took some of its blood, and put it on their right earlobes, on their right thumbs, and on their right great toes. Moses poured the blood on the altar and all around it.
8.25: Then he took the fat - from the tail, all the fat on the internal organs, the appendage of the liver, the two kidneys with the fat, and the right thigh.
8.26: From the basket of unleavened bread, which is in the Lord's presence, he took one piece of unleavened bread, one cake spread with olive oil, and one wafer, which he placed over the fat and the right thigh.
8.27: He put all of these things in the hands of Aaron and his sons, and they all waved them in a raised offering to the Lord.
8.28: After this, Moses took those things from their hands and burned them on the altar over the whole burnt offering for consecration. They served as a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the Lord.
8.29: Moses took the breast and waved it as a raised offering in the Lord's presence as the portion that belonged to Moses from the ram of consecration, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
8.30: Moses took some anointing oil and blood that was on the altar and spattered it on Aaron, on his clothes, on his sons, and on their clothes, consecrating Aaron, his clothes, his sons, and their clothes.
8.31: Then he said to Aaron and his sons, "Boil the meat at the entrance of the tent of meeting. There you may eat it along with the bread that is in the basket for consecration, just as I've commanded when I told him, 'Aaron and his sons may eat of it,
8.32: but the left over meat and bread is to be incinerated.'
