25.29: "If a person sells a residential house in a walled city, then he is to redeem it within the year in which it was sold. He may have right to its redemption for a full year.
25.30: But if it's not redeemed by the end of a full year, then the house next to which is a wall is to belong to the one who bought it, in perpetuity throughout his generations. It is not to be returned in the jubilee.
25.31: "However, the houses in the villages that don't have walls around them are to be categorized along with the fields of the land. It may be redeemed, so it is to be returned in the jubilee.
25.32: "Nevertheless, the cities that belong to the Levites - that is, the houses in the cities that belong to them - are to belong to the Levites perpetually as part of their right of redemption.
25.33: If someone from the Levites redeems the houses in the cities that they own, they are to be returned in the jubilee, because the houses of the cities of the Levites are to remain their property among the Israelites.
25.34: Also, the open land of their cities is not to be sold, because it is to remain their perpetual inheritance."
25.35: If your relative becomes poor so that he is indebted to you, then you are to support him. You are to let him live with you just like the resident alien and the traveler.
25.36: You are not to take interest or profit from him. Instead, you are to fear your God and let your relative live with you.
25.37: "You are not to loan him money with interest or sell him your food at a profit.
25.38: I'm the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, and to be your God.
25.39: "If your brother with you becomes poor, so that he sells himself to you, you are not to make him serve like a bond slave.
25.40: Instead, he is to serve with you like a hired servant or traveler who lives with you until the year of jubilee.
25.41: Then he and his children with him may leave to return to his family and his ancestor's inheritance.
25.42: Since they're my servants whom I've brought out from the land of Egypt, they are not to be sold as slaves.
25.43: You are not to rule over them with harshness. You are to fear your God." 39
25.44: "As for your male and maid slaves who will be with you, you may buy male and female slaves from among the nations.
25.45: You may also buy from resident aliens who live among you and their families who are with you, whom they fathered in your land. They may become your property.
25.46: "You may give them as inherited property to your children after you, to own as properties in perpetuity. You may make bond slaves of them, but no one is to rule over his fellow Israelite with harshness.
25.47: "If a resident alien or traveler becomes rich, but your relative who lives next to him is so poor that he sells himself to that resident alien or traveler among you or to a member of the resident alien's family,
25.48: he has the right to be redeemed after he sells himself. One of his brothers may redeem him.
25.49: His uncle or his uncle's son may redeem him or any blood relative from his clan may redeem him. If he becomes rich, then he may redeem himself.
25.50: "He is to bring an accounting to the one who bought him, starting from the year he had sold himself until the year of jubilee. The price of his sale is to correspond to the number of years comparable to the time a hired servant stays with him.
25.51: "If there are still many years left, he is to refund the cost of his redemption.
25.52: But if only a few years are left until the year of jubilee, he is to bring an accounting of the years that he is to refund for his redemption.
25.53: Like a hired servant, he is to remain with him year after year, but he is not to rule over him with what you see as severity.
25.54: "If he isn't redeemed by these, then he is to be set free in the year of jubilee - he and his children with him
25.55: because the Israelites are my servants. They're my servants, since I brought them out of the land of Egypt. I'm the Lord your God."

26.1: "You are not to make worthless idols, images, or pillars for yourselves, nor set up for yourselves carved images to bow down to them in the land, because I'm the Lord your God.
26.2: "You are to keep my Sabbath and fear my sanctuary. I'm the Lord.
26.3: "If you live by my statutes, obey my commands, and observe them,
26.4: then I'll send your rain in its season so that the land will yield its produce and the trees of the field will yield their fruit.
26.5: Threshing will extend to the time of vintage and the vintage will extend to the time of sowing so that you'll eat your bread to your satisfaction and live securely in your land.
26.6: "I'll give peace in the land so that you'll lie down without fear. I'll remove wild beasts from the land, not even war will come to your land.
26.7: Instead you'll pursue your enemies and they'll die by the sword before you.
26.8: Five of you will chase a hundred, a hundred of you will chase ten thousand, and your enemies will fall by the sword before you.
26.9: "I'll look after you, ensuring that you'll be fruitful. I'll increase your number and keep my covenant with you.
26.10: When you have consumed what was stored of the old, then you'll take out the old and replace it with what's new.
26.11: "I'll set up my tabernacle in your midst and I won't loathe you.
26.12: I'll walk among you. I will be your God, and you'll be my people.
26.13: I'm the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt so that you will no longer be their slaves, since I've broken their oppressive yoke upon you to make you walk upright."
26.14: "But if you won't listen to me and obey all these commands,
26.15: and if you refuse my statutes, loathe my ordinances, fail to do all my commands, thereby breaching my covenant,
26.16: then I will certainly do this to you： I'll appoint sudden terror to infect you like tuberculosis and fever. Your the eyes will fail and your life will waste away. You'll plant in vain, because your enemies will consume what you plant.
26.17: "I'll set my face against you so that you'll be defeated before your enemies. Those who hate you will have dominion over you and you'll keep fleeing even when no one is pursuing you.
26.18: "If despite all of this you still don't listen to me, then I'll punish you seven times more on account of your sins.
26.19: I'll break your mighty pride. I'll make the heavens to be like iron and the ground like bronze.
26.20: Your strength will be spent in vain, because your land won't yield its produce and the trees of the land won't yield its fruit.
26.21: "If you live life contrary to me and remain unwilling to listen to me, then I'll add to your wounds seven times more on account of your sins.
26.22: I'll send the wild beasts against you from the open country to deprive you of your children, destroy your cattle, and decrease your number so that your roads become desolate.
26.23: If despite these things you will don't return to me, but live life contrary to me,
26.24: then I'll certainly oppose you. I'll take vengeance against you seven fold on account of your sins.
26.25: I'll bring the sword against you to execute the vengeance of my covenant. When you gather in your cities, I'll send a pestilence. As a result, you'll be delivered into the control of your enemies.
26.26: "When I destroy the source of your bread, ten women will bake bread in one oven. Then they'll return back your bread by weight. You'll eat but won't be satisfied.
26.27: "If after all of this time you don't listen to me, but instead live life contrary to me,
26.28: I'll oppose you with vicious rage. Indeed, I myself will punish you seven fold on account of your sins.
26.29: At that time you'll eat the flesh of your sons and you'll eat the flesh of your daughters.
26.30: "I'll destroy your high places and cut down your sun-pillars. Then I'll cast your dead bodies on top of the bodies of your idols. "I'll loathe you.
26.31: I'll lay your cities to waste and destroy your sanctuaries so I don't have to smell the scent of your soothing odors.
26.32: "I'll make the land so desolate that your enemies who live in it will be astonished."
26.33: "I'll scatter you among the nations and draw the sword after you so that your land becomes desolate and your towns become ruins.
26.34: "Then the land will finally be pleased with its Sabbaths as long as it lies desolate while you are in the land of your enemies. At that time the land will rest and take its Sabbaths.
26.35: As long as it lies desolate, it will have rest, that it will not have had during your Sabbaths when you were living in it.
26.36: "As for the remnants among you, I'll bring despair in their hearts in the land of their enemies so that even the sound of a blown leaf will chase them and they flee as though pursued by the sword and fall when no one is pursuing.
26.37: They'll stumble over each other as though fleeing before the sword, even though no one is pursuing. "You won't have power to resist your enemies.
26.38: You'll perish among the nations and the land of your enemies will consume you.
26.39: The remnants among you will waste away on account of their iniquity in the land of your enemies. Indeed, they'll also waste away on account of the iniquities of their ancestors with them.
26.40: "Nevertheless, when they confess their iniquity, the iniquity of their ancestors, and their unfaithfulness by which they acted unfaithfully against me by living life contrary to me,
26.41: causing me to oppose them and take them to the land of their enemies so that the uncircumcised foreskin of their hearts can be humbled and so that they accept the punishment of their iniquity,
26.42: then I'll remember my covenant with Jacob, my covenant with Isaac, and my covenant with Abraham. I'll also remember the land.
26.43: "They will leave the land so it can rest while it lies desolate without them. That's when they'll receive the punishment of their iniquity because indeed they will have rejected my ordinances and despised my statutes.
26.44: "Yet despite all of these things, when they're in the land of their enemies, I won't reject or despise them so as to completely destroy them and by doing so violate my covenant with them, because I'm the Lord their God.
26.45: Instead, on account of them, I'll remember my covenant with their ancestors when I brought them out from the land of Egypt right before the eyes of the nations, so that I could be their God. I'm the Lord."
26.46: These are the statutes, ordinances, and laws that the Lord made between himself and the Israelites on Mount Sinai, as recorded by the hand of Moses.

27.2: "Tell the Israelites that when a person makes a special vow based on the appropriate value of people who belong to the Lord,
27.3: if your valuation of the vow is for a male from 20 to 60 sixty years old, the valuation is to be fifty shekels of silver, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
27.4: "If she is a female from 20 to 60 years old, then your valuation is to be 30 shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
27.5: "If the a person is from five to 20 twenty years, then your valuation for a male is to be 20 shekels and for a female ten shekels.
27.6: "If a person is from one month to five years old, then your valuation for a male is to be five shekels of silver, and for a female your valuation is to be three shekels of silver.
27.7: "If a person is 60 or more years old, then your valuation for a male is to be fifteen shekels and for a female ten shekels.
27.8: "But if he be too poor to be valuated, then cause him to stand before the priest and let the priest set a value on him according to the ability of the one making the vow.
27.9: "If it's an animal from which they make an offering to the Lord, everything that he gives to the Lord from it will be holy.
27.10: He is not to substitute it or exchange it - the good with the bad or the bad with the good. "If he ever makes an exchange of an animal for an animal, then it and what's being exchanged is holy.
27.11: "If any animal is unclean, which cannot be brought to the Lord as an offering, make the animal stand in the presence of the priest,
27.12: then the priest will evaluate it as to whether it is good or bad. According to your - that is, the priest's - valuation, so it is to be.
27.13: If a kinsman redeemer decides to redeem it, then he is to add a fifth to your valuation."
27.14: "If a person consecrates his house to be holy to the Lord, then the priest is to set a value for it as to its worth, whether good or bad. As the priest sets value on it, so it will stand.
27.15: "And if he that consecrated it wishes to redeem his house, he is to add one fifth to your valuation, after which it is to belong to him.
27.16: "If a person consecrates a portion of the field from his inheritance to the Lord, then your valuation is to be based on its capacity for yielding a harvest. Each homer of barley is to be valued at 50 shekels of silver.
27.17: "If he consecrates his field in the year of jubilee, it is to be based on your valuation.
27.18: "If he consecrates his field after the jubilee, then the priest is to account to him the silver according to the years that remain until the year of jubilee, with a deduction corresponding to your valuation.
27.19: "If the one who consecrated the field intends to redeem it, then he is to add one fifth of your valuation to it in silver, then it is to be established as his.
27.20: "But if he won't redeem the field, but instead sells it to another person. then it is not to be redeemed anymore.
27.21: When the field is released in the jubilee, it will be holy to the Lord. As a field that's devoted, it is to belong to the priest as his inheritance.
27.22: "If he consecrates a field that he had bought and that isn't part of his inheritance,
27.23: then the priest is to account to him the evaluated worth until the year of jubilee. Then he is to give the amount of valuation on that day as a holy gift to the Lord.
27.24: "During the year of jubilee, the field is to be returned by the one who originally sold it, that is, to the owner of the land.
27.25: Every valuation is to be according to the shekel of the sanctuary, evaluated at 20 gerahs to the shekel.
27.26: "No person is to consecrate the first born, because the first born of the animals already belongs to the Lord. Whether ox or goat, it belongs to the Lord.
27.27: If it's an unclean animal, then he is to ransom it according to your valuation, adding a fifth to it. If it's not redeemed then it is to be sold according to your valuation.
27.28: "However, any devoted thing, that a person consecrates to the Lord from what he owns, whether man, animals, or inherited fields, is not to be sold or redeemed. Any devoted thing is most sacred. It belongs to the Lord.
27.29: But anyone who is completely devoted from among human beings is not to be ransomed. He is certainly to be put to death.
27.30: "Any tithes of the land from grain grown on the land or from fruit grown on the trees belong to the Lord. It's sacred to the Lord.
27.31: But if a person wishes to redeem his tithe, he is to add a fifth to it.
27.32: "All the tithes from cattle and flocks that pass under the measuring rod are sacred to the Lord.
27.33: He is not to examine it to see if it's good or bad or even exchange it. If he does exchange it, what has been exchanged as well as its substitute is sacred. It is not to be redeemed."
27.34: These are the commands, that the Lord commanded Moses to deliver to the Israelites on Mount Sinai.

1.1: In the Sinai desert, the Lord spoke to Moses inside the Tent of Meeting on the first day of the second month of the second year after they had left the land of Egypt. He said,
1.2: "Take a census of the entire Israelite community, numbering them by their tribes and by ancestral houses. List the names of every male one-by-one,
1.3: from 20 years and upward. You and Aaron are to register everyone in Israel who is able to go to war, company by company.
1.4: One man from each tribe is to accompany you, each man being the leader of his ancestral house.
1.5: "Here is a list of names of the men who are to assist you： "From Reuben： Shedeur's son Elizur.
1.6: From Simeon： Zurishaddai's son Shelumiel.
1.7: From Judah： Amminadab's son Nahshon.
1.8: From Issachar： Zuar's son Nethanel.
1.9: From Zebulun： Helon's son Eliab.
1.10: "From Joseph's descendants through Ephraim： Ammihud's son Elishama. From Manasseh： Pedahzur's son Gamaliel.
1.11: From Benjamin： Gideoni's son Abidan.
1.12: From Dan： Ammishaddai's son Ahiezer.
1.13: From Asher： Ochran's son Pagiel.
1.14: From Gad： Deuel's son Eliasaph.
1.15: From Naphtali： Enan's son Ahira."
1.16: These men were appointed from within their communities, since they were leaders of their ancestral houses and heads of the tribes of Israel.
1.17: Moses and Aaron gathered these men who had been mentioned by name.
1.18: They assembled the entire community together during the second month. Then they recorded their ancestries according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as well as the names of the men 20 years old and above individually,
1.19: just as the Lord had commanded Moses. He numbered them in the Sinai desert.
1.20: The genealogies of the descendants of Reuben, the first born of Israel, were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.21: Those registered with the tribe of Reuben numbered 46,500.
1.22: The genealogies of Simeon's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.23: Those registered with the tribe of Simeon numbered 59,300.1
1.24: The genealogies of Gad's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.25: Those registered with the tribe of Gad numbered 45,650.
1.26: The genealogies of Judah's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.27: Those registered with the tribe of Judah numbered 74,600.
1.28: The genealogies of Issachar's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.29: Those registered with the tribe of Issachar numbered 54,400.
1.30: The genealogies of Zebulun's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.31: Those registered with the tribe of Zebulun numbered 57,400.
1.32: The genealogies of Joseph's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.33: Those registered with the tribe of Joseph numbered 40,500.
1.34: The genealogies of Manasseh's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.35: Those registered with the tribe of Manasseh numbered 32,200.
1.36: The genealogies of Benjamin's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.37: Those registered with the tribe of Benjamin numbered 35,400.
1.38: The genealogies of Dan's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.39: Those registered with the tribe of Dan numbered 62,700.
1.40: The genealogies of Asher's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.41: Those registered with the tribe of Asher numbered 41,500.
1.42: The genealogies of Naphtali's descendants were recorded individually according to their tribes and ancestral houses, as were the names of all the men 20 years and above who could serve in the army.
1.43: Those registered with the family of Naphtali numbered 53,400.
1.44: Moses and Aaron registered these individuals were the ones whom Moses and Aaron registered from the twelve leaders of Israel, each person from his ancestral house.
1.45: Everyone was numbered from the descendants of Israel, from their ancestral houses, from all the men who were 20 years and above and who could serve in the army.
1.46: The total of all those who were numbered was 603,550.
1.47: The Levites were not counted according to their ancestral houses
1.48: because the Lord had ordered Moses：
1.49: "Be sure not to number or count the tribe of Levi with the rest of the Israelites.
1.50: Instead, appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony, all the vessels, and everything in it. They are to carry the tabernacle and all the vessels in it. They are to attend to it and camp around it.
1.51: Whenever the tabernacle is ready for travel, the Levites are to take it down. "When it's time to encamp, the Levites are to set it up. Any unauthorized person who approaches it is to be executed.
1.52: Then the Israelites are to encamp around the tabernacle, arranged according to their company and the standard of their army.
1.53: But the Levites are to encamp on all sides of the tabernacle of the testimony so that divine wrath won't fall on the congregation of Israel. The Levites are to take care of the tabernacle of the testimony."1
1.54: The Israelites observed everything that the Lord had commanded Moses, doing exactly what they were told.

2.1: Later, the Lord told Moses and Aaron,
2.2: "Every single Israelite is to encamp beneath his standard with the emblem of his ancestral house. The Israelites are to encamp from a distance, but surrounding the tent of meeting."
2.3: "The encampment of Judah is to settle east toward the sunrise under their standard. The leader of Judah is to be Amminadab's son Nahshon.
2.4: Those in his division number 74,600.
2.5: "The tribe of Issachar is to encamp beside Judah. The leader of Issachar is to be Zuar's son Nathaniel.
2.6: Those in his division number 54,400.
2.7: "Next is to be the tribe of Zebulun. The leader of Zebulun is to be Helon's son Eliab.
2.8: Those in his division number 57,400.
2.9: All those numbered by division in the camp of Judah total 186,400. They are to be the first to travel."
2.10: "Toward the south is to be the division of the camp of Reuben under their standard. The leader of Reuben is to be Shedeur's son Elizur.
2.11: Those in his division number 46,500.
2.12: "The tribe of Simeon is to camp beside Reuben. The leader of Simeon is to be Zurishaddai's son Shelumiel.
2.13: Those in his division number 59,300.
2.14: "Next is to be the tribe of Gad. The leader of Gad is to be Deuel's son Eliasaph.
2.15: Those in his division number 45,650.
2.16: All those numbered by division in the camp of Reuben total 151,450. They are to be the second to travel."
2.17: "Then the tent of meeting is to travel with the camp of the Levites in the middle of the camps. They are to travel just as they have camped, each as designated under his standard."
2.18: "Toward the west is to be the division of the camp of Ephraim under their standard. The leader of Ephraim is to be Ammihud's son Elishama.
2.19: Those in his division number 40,500.
2.20: "The tribe of Manasseh is to encamp beside them. The leader of Manasseh is to be Pedahzur's son Gamaliel.
2.21: Those in his division number 32,200.
2.22: "Next is to be the tribe of Benjamin. The leader of Benjamin is to be Gideoni's son Abidan.
2.23: Those in his division number 35,400.
2.24: All those numbered by division in the camp Ephraim total 108,100. They are to be the third to travel."
2.25: "Toward the north is to be the division of the camp of Dan under their standard. The leader of Dan is to be Ammisshadai's son Ahiezer.
2.26: Those in his division number 62,700.
2.27: "The tribe of Asher is to encamp beside them. The leader of Asher is to be Ocran's son Pagiel.
2.29: "Next is to be the tribe of Naphtali. The leader of Naphtali is to be Enan's son Ahira.
2.31: All those numbered by division in the camp of Dan total 157,600. They are to be the last to travel under their standards."
2.32: Here is a summary of the census of the Israelites according to the tribes of their ancestral houses： All the divisions in the camps numbered 603,550,
2.33: but the Levites were not numbered along with the other Israelites, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
2.34: So the Israelites did everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses; that is, they encamped under their standard as each person traveled with his own tribe and ancestral house.

3.1: This is a record of the genealogies of Aaron and Moses current as of the day on which the Lord addressed Moses on Mount Sinai.
3.2: The sons of Aaron were Nadab the first-born, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar
3.3: who were anointed priests and whom he consecrated as priests.
3.4: Nadab and Abihu died in the Lord's presence when they offered unauthorized fire before him in the Sinai wilderness. Since they didn't have their own children, Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priest under the authority of Aaron their father.2
3.5: The Lord told Moses,
3.6: "Bring the tribe of Levi near and present them to Aaron the priest so they may serve him.
3.7: They are to take care of his needs and the needs of the whole congregation at the tent of meeting by performing duties at the tabernacle.
3.8: They are to take charge of the utensils at the tent of meeting and meet the needs of the Israelites by performing duties on behalf of the tabernacle.
3.9: Assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites.
3.10: Appoint Aaron and his sons so that they are to take responsibility for their priesthood. Any unauthorized person who approaches it is to be put to death."
3.11: Later, the Lord told Moses,
3.12: "I'm taking the Levites for myself from among the Israelites in place of every first-born who opens the womb. The Levites belong to me
3.13: because all the first-born belong to me. When I destroyed all the first born in the land of Egypt, I consecrated all the first-born in Israel for myself - from human beings to livestock. They belong to me, since I am the Lord."
3.14: The Lord also told Moses in the Sinai wilderness,
3.15: "Number the Levites according to their ancestral houses and tribes, numbering every male from a month old and above."
3.16: So Moses numbered them according to the instruction of the Lord, as he had been commanded.
3.17: These are Levi's descendant by name： Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
3.18: These are names of Gerson's descendants according to their families： Libni and Shimei.
3.19: These are the names of Kohath's descendants according to their families： Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uziel.
3.20: Merari's descendants according to their families were Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their ancestral house.19
3.21: The families of Libni and Shimei were descendants of Gershon. As families of the Gershonitess,
3.22: all the males a month old and above numbered 7,500.
3.23: The families of the Gershonites encamped behind the tabernacle toward the west.
3.24: The leader of the tribe and family of Gershon was Lael's son Eliasaph.
3.25: The duties of the Gershonites at the tent of meeting pertained to the tabernacle, the tent covering, the curtain to the entrance of the tent of meeting,
3.26: the hangings at the courtyard, the curtain to the entrance of the courtyard that surrounded the tabernacle, the altar, and all of the tent cords in use.
3.27: The families of Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel were descendants of Kohath. As families of the Kohathites,
3.28: all the males a month old and above numbered 8,600. They were tasked to care of the sanctuary.
3.29: The Kohathites encamped beside the tabernacle toward the south.
3.30: The leader of the tribe and family of Kohath was Uzziel's son Elizaphan.
3.31: Their duties pertained to the ark, the table, the lamp stand, the altars, the utensils of the sanctuary with which they ministered, and all the curtains in use.
3.32: The chief of all the leaders of the Levites was Aaron the priest's son Eleazar. He was assigned to oversee those who were in charge of the services of the sanctuary.
3.33: The families of Mahli and Mushi were descendants of Merari. As families of Merari,
3.34: all the males a month old and above numbered 6,200.
3.35: The leader of the tribe and family of Merari was Abihail's son Zuriel. The Merarites encamped beside the tabernacle toward the north.
3.36: The duties of the caretakers from the Merarites included the boards of the tabernacle, its bars, crossbars, socket, all its utensils for their services,
3.37: the pillars around the courtyard, their sockets, pegs, and tent cords.
3.38: In front of the tabernacle and east of the tent of meeting, Moses, Aaron, and Aaron's sons encamped facing the east. They were tasked to perform the duties of the sanctuary and care for the needs of the Israelites. Any unauthorized person who approached was to be executed.
3.39: As the Lord had instructed, everyone counted by Moss and Aaron from the Levites, according to their tribe, all males from a month old and above numbered 22,000.
3.40: Later the Lord instructed Moses： "Number all the first-born males of Israel from a month old and above and list their names.
3.41: Separate the Levites for me - since I am the Lord - in place of all the first-born sons of Israel. Also separate the livestock of the Levites in place of all the first born of the livestock of Israel."
3.42: So Moses numbered all the first born from the sons of Israel just as the Lord commanded him.
3.43: All of the first-born male according to the list of their names from a month old and above numbered 22,273.
3.45: "Separate the Levites in place of all the first born sons of Israel and the livestock of the Levites in place of their livestock. The Levites belong to me, since I am the Lord.
3.46: You are to pay a ransom of the 273 first-born sons of Israel who exceed the census number of the Levites,
3.47: so collect five shekels for each individual, denominated in shekels of the sanctuary, that is, the shekel that weighs 20 gerahs.
3.48: Then give the money meant for ransom of their excess to Aaron and his sons."
3.49: So Moses took the ransom money to account for the difference in the total number of those redeemed by the Levites.
3.50: From the first born of the Israelites, Moses took money amounting to 1,365 shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
3.51: Moses gave the ransom money to Aaron and his sons according to the Lord's instructions, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.

4.1: The Lord told Moses and Aaron,
4.2: "Take a census of the Kohathites from among the Levites according to their tribes and ancestral houses
4.3: from 30 years and older through the age of 50 years, from everyone who can enter the service to perform work at the tent of meeting.
4.4: "Here's what the Kohathites are to do regarding the tent of meeting and what's inside the most holy place：
4.5: When the camp is about to travel, Aaron and his sons are to come and take down the veil of the curtain and cover the ark of testimony with it.
4.6: They are to set a leather-dyed skin covering over it, cover it with a pure blue cloth, and then insert its poles.
4.7: "They are to spread a blue cloth over the table of the bread of the Presence and on top of it the dishes, pans, bowls, pitchers for drink offerings, and the bread of presence are to be on it continuously.
4.8: They are to spread over them a scarlet cloth and a leather-dyed skin covering and then insert its poles.7
4.9: "They are to take a blue cloth and cover the lamp stand for the light with its lamp, lamp-snuffers, censer, and all the utensils for its oil with which they minister.
4.10: Then they are to put them with all the other utensils on the leather-dyed skin covering and set them on the beams for transport.
4.11: "On the golden altar, they are to spread a blue cloth and cover it with a leather-dyed skin covering and then insert its poles.
4.12: Then they are to take all the utensils for service with which they minister at the sanctuary; set them on the blue cloth; cover them with the leather-dyed skin covering and then set them on the beams for transport.
4.13: They are to also remove the ashes on the altar and spread over it a purple cloth.
4.14: Then they are to put all the instruments with which they minister there - trays, forks, shovels, bowls, and all the utensils of the altar. They are to spread over it a leather-dyed skin covering and then insert its poles.
4.15: "When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the utensils of the sanctuary, and the camp is about to travel, then the Kohathites are to come and carry them, but they are not to touch the most sacred objects, so they won't die. These are the duties of the Kohathites at the tent of meeting."
4.16: "Now the duty of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest is to maintain the oil for the light, the spiced incense, the daily offerings, the oil for anointing, and to carry out all the duties of the tabernacle and the sanctuary, and to maintain its utensils."
4.17: Then the Lord told Moses and Aaron,
4.18: "You are not to eliminate the tribe of the families of the Kohathites from the Levites.
4.19: But do this for them so that they may live and not die when they approach the most sacred objects： Aaron and his sons are to go in and set specific responsilities for each of them to carry out.
4.20: But they are not to go in to see the sanctuary as it is being covered so they won't die."
4.21: Then the Lord told Moses,
4.22: Take a census of the Gershonites according to their ancestral house and tribes.
4.23: Count their number from between 30 to 50 years old, including everyone who can enter the service to perform work at the tent of meeting.
4.24: "These are the responsibilities that the Gershonites are to have：
4.25: They are to carry the curtain of the tabernacle, the covering of the tent of meeting, the dyed leather covering that goes over it, the curtain for the entrance to the tent of meeting,
4.26: the hangings for the courtyard, the curtain for the entrance to the gate of the courtyard that surrounds the tabernacle, the altar, the ropes, all the service utensils, and everything made for them. This is to be their service area.
4.27: "The Gershonites are carry out the instructions of Aaron and his sons. You are to assign them their responsibilities to carry out.
4.28: This is the work of the tribes of Gershon at the tent of meeting - their duties under the supervision of Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest.
4.29: "For the Merarites, number them according to their tribes and ancestral houses
4.30: from 30 to 50 years old as you count them, including everyone who can enter service and perform work at the tent of meeting.
4.31: This is to be their area of responsibility to carry out with respect to their service at the tent of meeting： the board of the tabernacle, its bars, its crossbars, its sockets,
4.32: the pillars around the courtyard, their sockets, their pegs, their ropes, and all the utensils for all their services.  "Assign the utensils by name to each person whose responsibility it will be to carry them.
4.33: This is the work of the tribes of the Merarites with reference to their service at the tent of meeting under the supervision of he priest's son Ithamar."
4.34: Moses and Aaron and the congregational leaders numbered the Kohathites according to their tribes and ancestral houses
4.35: from 30 to 50 fifty years old; that is, everyone who entered the service to perform work at the tent of meeting.
4.36: The total according to their tribe numbered 2,750
4.37: from the tribe of the Kohathites, everyone who were to serve at the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to what the Lord had said, under the supervision of Moses.
4.38: The tribes and the ancestral houses of the Gershonites were numbered

5.1: The Lord told Moses,
5.2: "Command the Israelites to send outside the encampment every leper, everyone who has a discharge, and whoever is ritually defiled by contact with a corpse.
5.3: Whether male or female, send them outside the camp so that they won't defile their camp, because I live among them."
5.4: So the Israelites sent them outside the camp. The Israelites did just what the Lord had told Moses.2
5.5: The Lord told Moses,
5.6: "Instruct the Israelites that whenever a man or woman does something contained in the list of the sins of man, thereby acting treacherously against the Lord, then that person stands guilty.
5.7: He is to confess the sin that he had committed, pay its full compensation, add one fifth to it, and give the compensation to whomever he offended.
5.8: But if the person has no related redeemer to whom compensation may be made, the payment is to be brought to the Lord and given to the priest, in addition to a ram for atonement with which he is to be atoned.
5.9: Every offering from all the most sacred things of the Israelites that they bring to the priest is to belong to him.
5.10: Furthermore, everyone's sacred things belong to him, and as well as regarding whatever a person gives to the priest.
5.11: Then the Lord told Moses,
5.12: "Instruct the Israelites what to do if a man's wife turns astray so that she unfaithfully acts against him,
5.13: a man has sexual relations with her and she conceals it from her husband, keeping it secret although she has defiled herself with there being no witnesses against her, but she was caught anyway.
5.14: If an attitude of jealousy overcomes him so that he becomes jealous at his wife when she is defiled, or if an attitude of jealousy overcomes him and he becomes jealous of his wife eventhough she isn't defiled,
5.15: then that man is to bring his wife to the priest along with an offering for her consisting of a tenth of an ephah of barley flour. "He is not to pour oil or set frankincense over it, because it's to be a jealousy offering, a memorial offering that will serve as a reminder of iniquity.
5.16: Then the priest is to bring it and make her stand in the Lord's presence.
5.17: The priest is to put some holy water into an earthen vessel, take some dust from the floor of the tabernacle, and put it into the water.
5.18: "The priest is to have the woman stand in the Lord's presence, uncover her head, and put the grain offering as a memorial, a reminder of jealousy, into her hands. The priest is also to have in his hand the contaminated water that carry a curse.
5.19: "The priest is to administer this oath to the woman： 'If indeed another man didn't have sexual relations with you and you didn't become unfaithful to your husband, then may you be free from these waters that bring a curse.
5.20: But if you have become unfaithful to your husband and have become defiled because a man who isn't your husband has had sexual relations with you…'
5.21: then the priest is to have the woman commit to an oath by saying to the woman, 'May the Lord make you a curse and a curse among your people. When the Lord makes your thigh waste away and your abdomen swell
5.22: and this water that brings a curse enters your abdomen, making it swell and your thigh waste away.' "Then the woman is to say 'Amen.'
5.23: "Then the priest is to write all of these words in a document and wipe it off with the contaminated water.
5.24: The woman is to drink the bitter water that brings a curse and the water that brings a curse is to be considered contaminated.
5.25: Then priest is to take the offering of jealousy from the woman's hand, wave the offering in the Lord's presence, and have her approach the altar.
5.26: "The priest is to take a handful of grain from the memorial and offer a sacrifice on the altar, after which he is to have the woman drink the water.
5.27: When he has had her drink the water, if she was defiled and had acted unfaithfully toward her husband, then the contaminated water that brings a curse will enter her and infect her, causing her abdomen to swell and her thigh to waste away. Then she is to be a cursed woman among her people.
5.28: "But if the woman isn't defiled, then she is to be freed and will be able to bear children.
5.29: This is the law in cases of jealousy when a woman defiles herself while under her husband's authority.
5.30: The husband will be free from guilt, but the wife is to bear the punishment of her iniquity."529

6.1: Then the Lord told Moses,
6.2: "Tell the Israelites that a man or woman who commits to the vow of the Nazarite, is to be separated to the Lord,
6.3: then is to separate from wine and strong drink. He is not to drink vinegar or strong drink made from wine. He is not to drink grape juice or eat grapes, whether fresh or dried.2
6.4: "During the entire time of his dedication, he is not to eat any product from the grapevine, from seed to the skin.
6.5: "During the entire time of his dedication, he is not to allow a razor to pass over his head until the days of his holy consecration to the Lord have been fulfilled. He is to let the locks on his head grow long.
6.6: "During the entire time of his dedication, he is not to come near a dead body.
6.7: He is not to defile himself on account of his father, mother, brother, and sister when they die, because the crown of his consecration to God is on his head.
6.8: "During the entire time of his dedication, he is set apart to God.
6.9: When someone suddenly beside him, so that his consecrated head is defiled, then he is to shave his head on the day of his purification. Seven days later he is to shave it again.
6.10: On the eighth day, he is to bring two turtledoves or two pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
6.11: "Then the priest is to offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering to make atonement for him because of the guilt he incurred on account of his contact with the dead body. Then he is to consecrate his head on that day.
6.12: He is to dedicate to the Lord the days of his consecration by bringing a year old male lamb as his offering. The previous time will have failed because his consecration became defiled.
6.13: "This is the law of the Nazarite： When the days of his consecration are completed, he is to come to the entrance at the tent of meeting.
6.14: He is to bring an offering to the Lord, a year old male lamb, and a year old ewe female lamb, both without blemish, for a sin offering and a ram without blemish for apeace offering,
6.15: a basket of unleavened bread made from choice flour, cakes mixed with oil, a wafer of unleavened bread smeared with oil, along with grain and drink offerings.
6.16: "The priest is to come into the Lord's presence and present his sin and burnt offerings.
6.17: He is to offer the ram, a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord, along with the basket of unleavened bread. Then the priest is to present his grain and drink offerings.
6.18: "The Nazarite is then to shave his head of consecration at the entrance of the tent of meeting. He is to take the lock of his head of consecration and set it over the fire where the peace offering for sacrifice is.
6.19: Then the priest is to take the boiled shoulder of the ram, one cake of unleavened bread from the basket, and one wafer of unleavened bread. He is to place them in the hands of the Nazarite, after he himself has shaved his symbol of consecration.
6.20: The priest is to wave the offerings, that is, the breast and the thigh offering in the Lord's presence. Then the Nazarite may drink wine afterward.
6.21: "This is to be the law of the Nazarite when he commits his offering to the Lord on account of his consecration, over and beyond what he owns alone plus whatever he can provide, based on the vow from his own mouth that he vows to fulfill on account of the law of his consecration."
6.22: Later, the Lord told Moses,
6.23: "Teach Aaron and his sons to bless the Israelites：
6.24: May the Lord bless you and guard you.
6.25: May the Lord's face enlighten you and bestow favor on you.
6.26: May the Lord turn to face you, lavishing peace on you!
6.27: They are to pour out my name to the Israelites while I continue to bless them."

7.1: The same day that Moses finished setting up, anointing, and consecrating the tabernacle and its utensils, he also anointed and consecrated the altar and its utensils.
7.2: Then the presiding leaders of Israel, as heads of the ancestral houses, brought an offering. They were the leaders of the tribes who supervised the census.
7.3: They brought their offering into the Lord's presence, consisting of six covered carts and twelve oxens - one cart each from two leaders and an ox from each one.
7.4: After they presented them in front of the tabernacle, 4the Lord told Moses,
7.5: "Take these gifts from them and use them in service at the tent of meeting. Present them to the Levites, distributing them to each person according to his work."
7.6: So Moses took the carts and the oxen and presented them to the Levites.
7.7: Two carts and four oxen were given to the Gershonites for their work.
7.8: Four carts and eight oxen were given to the Merarites for their work.
7.9: But he gave none of them to the Kohathites, because their responsibility was to carry the holy things on their shoulders.
7.10: The leaders brought the offerings for the dedication of the altar the same day that it was anointed. After the leaders brought their offering to the altar,
7.11: the Lord told Moses, "They are to present their offerings, one leader per day, for the dedication of the altar."

7.15: one young bull, one ram, a one year old male lamb for a burnt offering;
7.16: and one male goat for a sin offering.

7.84: This was what was presented at the dedication of the altar from the leaders of Israel on the same day that it was anointed： twelve silver bowls, twelve silver basins, twelve gold ladles.

7.86: Also, twelve gold ladles filled with incense were presented, each ladle weighing ten shekels (calculated according to the shekel of the sanctuary). All of the gold of the ladles weighed 120 shekels.
