27.1: Now the daughters of Hepher's son Zelophehad, Gilead's grandson, who had been fathered by Machir, who had been fathered by Manasseh, from the tribe of Manasseh the direct son of Joseph, were named Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah. They approached
27.2: and Moses, Eleazar the priest, the elders, and the entire community at the entrance of the tent of meeting, stood before them, and said,
27.3: "Our father died in the wilderness, but he wasn't with the company of those who gathered against the Lord along with the company of Korah. He died in his own sin, and he had no sons.
27.4: Why are you going to eliminate the name of our father from his family, just because he had no son -  Give us a possession from among our father's relatives."
27.5: So Moses brought the family into the Lord's presence,
27.7: "The daughters of Zelophehad are telling the truth. You are certainly, to give to them a possession for an inheritance among their father's relatives. You are to pass on the inheritance of their father to them.
27.8: Tell the Israelite that when a man dies without a son, you are to pass his inheritance to his daughter.
27.9: If he doesn't have a daughter, give his inheritance to his brothers.
27.10: If he doesn't have brothers, give his inheritance to his father's brothers.
27.11: If his father doesn't have brothers, then give his inheritance to a relative who is nearest to him from the family and he'll take possession of it. This is to be a permanent ordinance for the Israelites, just as the Lord commanded Moses."
27.12: Then the Lord said to Moses, "You are climb these Abarim mountains and look over the land that I'm going to give the Israelites.
27.13: After you've seen it, you'll be taken to be with your people just as your brother Aaron was gathered to them,
27.14: because in the wilderness of Zin, when the community rebelled, you rebelled against my command to treat me as holy before their eyes in regards to the Meribah Springs in Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin."
27.16: "May the Lord God of the spirits of all living creatures appoint a man over the community
27.17: who will go in and out before them, and who will lead them out and bring them in so that the Lord's community won't be like a flock without a shepherd."
27.18: "Select Nun's son Joshua. The Spirit is in that man." the Lord answered Moses. "You are to lay your hand on him
27.19: and make him stand in front of Eleazar the priest and the entire community. Then you are to set him in charge right before their eyes,
27.20: turning over your authority to him so that the entire community of Israel knows to obey him.
27.21: "You are to make him stand in the presence of Eleazar the priest, who is to inquire on his behalf using the Urim in the presence of the Lord regarding a decision of judgment, because by his command he and all the Israelites with him will go out or come in."
27.22: So Moses did what the Lord had commanded him. He took Joshua, made him stand in the presence of Eleazar the priest and the entire community,
27.23: laid his hands on him, and charged him, just as the Lord had commanded, using Moses' authority.

28.2: "You are to command the Israelites about my offerings that they are to be sure to bring edible offerings to me, presented by fire and a pleasing aroma to me, at their appointed time.
28.3: "Tell them that this is the offering, presented by fire that you are to offer to the Lord： two one year old lambs, offered daily every day.
28.4: Offer the first lamb in the morning and the second toward the evening,
28.5: accompanied by one tenth of an ephah of fine flour for grain offering, mixed with one fourth of a hin of pure olive oil.
28.6: This burnt offering, which was prescribed at Mount Sinai, is to be offered every day as a pleasing aroma made by fire to the Lord.5
28.7: "The drink offering is to be one fourth of a hin for each lamb. You are to pour out a drink offering of strong wine to the Lord in the holy place.
28.8: You are also to offer the second lamb toward the evening. Just like the morning sacrifice, you are to present the grain offering, accompanied by its corresponding drink offering, as a presentation made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord."
28.9: "Every Sabbath day, you are to offer two one year old lambs without any defects with two tenths of an ephah of fine flour for grain offering, mixed with olive oil, along with their corresponding drink offering.
28.10: This burnt offering is to be presented every Sabbath, apart from the continuous burnt offering, along with its corresponding drink offering."
28.11: "On the first day of each month, you are to offer a burnt offering to the Lord consisting of two young bulls, one ram, and seven one year old lambs, all of them without any defects,
28.12: along with three tenths of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering, mixed with olive oil, for each bull, two tenths of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering, mixed with olive oil, for the one ram,
28.13: one tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with olive oil as grain offering for each lamb. This burnt offering will be a pleasing aroma, incinerated as an offering to the Lord.
28.14: Their drink offerings are to be half a hin of wine for each bull, one third of a hin for the ram, and one fourth of a hin for each lamb. This burnt offering is to be presented each and every month throughout the year.
28.15: One goat is to be offered at regular intervals as a sin offering to the Lord, accompanied by its corresponding drink offering."
28.16: "The Lord's Passover is to take place on the fourteenth day of the first month.
28.17: You are to hold a festival on the fifteenth day of this month for seven days, during which time unleavened bread is to be eaten."
28.18: "On the first day, you are to hold a sacred assembly. No servile work is to be done.
28.19: Bring an offering that is to be incinerated in the Lord's presence, consisting of two young bulls, a ram, and seven one year old lambs, all without any defects,
28.20: along with their grain offering of fine flour mixed with olive oil. Offer three tenths of an ephah for each bull, two-tenths of an ephah for the ram,
28.21: and one tenth of an ephah for each of the seven lambs.
28.22: Then present one goat for a sin offering to make atonement for you,
28.23: apart from the burnt offering in the morning, which you are to continue offering.
28.24: "Do this every day for seven days, as an edible sacrifice to the Lord made by fire, a pleasing aroma. It is to be offered apart from the continuous burnt offering and its corresponding drink offering.
28.25: On the seventh day you are to hold another sacred assembly for your benefit, on which no servile work is to be done."
28.26: "On the first day of your harvest season, you are to hold a sacred assembly when you present your first fruits during the Feast of Weeks. No servile is to be done.
28.27: You are to offer this burnt-offering as a pleasing aroma to the Lord： two young bulls, one ram, and seven one year old lambs,
28.28: along with their corresponding grain offerings of fine flour mixed with olive oil; specifically, three tenths of an ephah for each bull, two tenths of an ephah for the one ram,
28.29: one tenth of an ephah for each of the seven lambs,
28.30: and one goat to make atonement for you.
28.31: Offer them in addition to the regular burnt offering, accompanied by its grain offering and its corresponding drink offerings."

30.1: Later, Moses told the elders of the Israelite tribes, "This is what the Lord has commanded.
30.2: When a man makes a vow to the Lord, or swears an oath - an obligation that is binding to himself -  he is not to break his word. Instead, he is to fulfill whatever promise came out of his mouth."
30.3: "When a young woman makes a vow to the Lord or pledges herself to an obligation while she still lives in her father's house,
30.4: and her father hears her vow and the obligations that she had pledged herself to fulfill, yet her father keeps silent about it, then all her vows and every obligation she pledged herself to are to stand.
30.5: "But if her father disallows her on the same day that he hears what she has said, then all her vows and every obligation she had pledged herself to fulfill are not to stand. The Lord will forgive her, because her father has forbidden her."
30.6: "If she has a husband and she makes a vow that is binding on herself, or if she makes a hasty vow with her mouth that she pledges herself to fulfill,
30.7: and husband hears her vow, yet remains silent on the day that he hears is, then her vows are to stand and the obligation to that she had pledged herself to fulfill is to stand.
30.8: But if, on the same day her husband hears, and he disallows her, then he has revoked her vows that she made for herself, along with any hasty vows that she spoke and to which she pledged herself to fulfill. The Lord will forgive her."30
30.9: "Everything that a widow or a divorced woman pledges herself to fulfill are to be binding on her.
30.10: But if, while she had been living in her late or former husband's house, she makes a vow or a promise that binds her with an oath,
30.11: and her husband hears it but remains silent, not disallowing it, then all her vows are to stand, along with every obligation that she has pledged herself to fulfill.
30.12: "But if her husband disallowed them the very day that he heard her, everything that she spoke relating to her vows and her obligation to herself are not to stand, because her husband had revoked them. The Lord will forgive her.
30.13: "Her husband may confirm or revoke every vow and binding obligation that afflicts her.
30.14: But if her husband remains silent about her from day to day, then he has affirmed all her vows or obligations that she has obligated herself to fulfill. He has affirmed them because he remained silent from the day he heard her vows.
30.15: But if had nullified them after he had heard, then he will be responsible for any resulting iniquity."
30.16: These are the statutes that the Lord commanded Moses concerning a man and his wife and concerning a father and his young daughter while she still lives in her father's house.

31.2: "Be sure to exact vengeance on behalf of the Israelites from the Midianites, after which you'll be taken home to your people."
31.3: SoMoses instructed the people, "Muster your men of war to attack the Midianites and deliver the Lord's vengeance against Midian.
31.4: Send 1,000 men to war from every tribe throughout all of Israel."
31.5: So 1,000 men from every tribe - 12,000 from the thousands of Israel - were mustered and equipped for war.
31.6: Moses sent 1,000 men from every tribe to fight against them, along with Eleazar's son Phinehas, in whose hands were the articles of the sanctuary and trumpets to sound he battle alarms.
31.7: They fought against the Midianites just as the Lord had commanded Moses, killing every man.
31.8: They executed the five kings of Midian, including Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba. They also executed Beor's son Balaam with a sword.
31.9: After this, the Israelites took captive the Midianite women and children and confiscated as spoils of war all their cattle, livestock, and their goods.
31.10: They burned every town where they had lived and incinerated all of their encampments.
31.11: They took all the booty and plunder, including both humans and animals.
31.12: Then they brought the captives, booty, and plunder to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the entire community of Israel at the camp on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan River in Jericho.
31.13: Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the community went out to meet them outside the camp.
31.14: But Moses became livid with anger at the officers of the army, the captains of thousands, and the captains of hundreds who had returned from servicing in the battle.
31.15: "Did you keep all the women alive?" Moses asked them.
31.16: "Look! These women were the same ones who were counseled by Balaam to cause the Israelites to commit a grievous sin against the Lord at Peor. As a result, that plague infected the Lord's community.
31.17: You are to kill every male child and every woman who has had sexual relations with a man.
31.18: You are to allow the young women who haven't yet had sexual relations with a man to live for yourselves."17
31.19: "Now you are to stay outside the camp for seven days, after which anyone who has killed a person or touched someone who was killed may purify yourselves on the third day and you and your captives will be pure on the seventh day.
31.20: Furthermore, you are to purify every garment, that is, everything made of leather, goat's hair, or containing wood."
31.21: Eleazar the priest told soldiers who had gone to battle, "This is the ordinance of the law that the Lord commanded Moses
31.22: concerning anything containing gold, silver, brass, iron, tin, and lead,
31.23: or anything that can survive a refiner's fire： You are to pass it through fire, after which it will be clean. Then it is to be purified with the water of impurity. Everything that cannot survive a refiner's fire is to be washed in water.
31.24: Wash your clothes on the seventh day, after which you will be clean. Then you may enter the camp."23
31.25: Then the Lord said to Moses,
31.26: "Take an inventory of the booty that was taken in the battle, both of humans and of animals. You, Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the fathers of the community,
31.27: are then to divide the booty between the warriors who went to war and the rest of the community.
31.28: "After this, you are to exact a tribute for the Lord from the soldiers who went to war, consisting of the tribute earned by one person out of every 500, whether from people, cattle, asses, or flocks.
31.29: "You are to take half their share and give it to Eleazar the priest as a raised offering to the Lord.
31.30: "Then take half the share of the Israelites, one drawn out of every fifty people, cattle, asses, flocks, and from every animal, then give to the Levites who maintain the service of the Lord's tabernacle."
31.31: So Moses and Eleazar the priest did what the Lord had commanded Moses.
31.32: Now, the goods confiscated, in excess of the war implements that the warriors had gathered was 675,000 sheep,
31.35: 32,000 women who had not had sexual relations with a man.
31.36: Now half of the share of those who went to war numbered 337,500 sheep,
31.37: so the Lord's tribute from the sheep totaled 675.
31.38: The cattle numbered 36,000 so the Lord's tribute totaled 72.
31.39: The donkeys numbered 30,500 so the Lord's tribute totaled 61.
31.40: The people numbered 16,000, and Lord's tribute totaled 32 people.
31.41: Then Moses gave the tribute, a raised offering of the Lord, to Eleazar the priest, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
31.42: From half of the share of the Israelites that Moses had set aside from the soldiers
31.43: there wee 337,500 sheep for the community,
31.47: Moses took a portion drawn from every 50 Israelites, including from both human and animals, and gave them to the Levites who maintained the Lord's tabernacle, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
31.48: Then the officers in charge of thousands of soldiers, the captains of thousands, and the captains of hundreds approached Moses
31.49: and told him, "Your servants took a count of the soldiers who were under our authority. We didn't miss a single man.
31.50: We've brought offerings to the Lord from whatever each man found - jewel-encrusted gold, anklets, bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and necklaces to make atonement for ourselves in the Lord's presence."
31.51: Then Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from them and everything that was fashioned into jewels.
31.52: The gold for the raised offering that they brought to the Lord totaled 16,750 shekels,
31.53: because every soldier had confiscated war booty for his own use.
31.54: Moses and Eleazar took the gold from the captains of thousands and hundreds and brought it to the tent of meeting, to serve as a memorial to the Israelites in the Lord's presence.

32.1: Now, the Reubenites and Gaddites happened to be joint owners of a very large herd of cattle. When they observed that Jazer and Gilead were good grazing lands for cattle,
32.2: the Gaddites and Reubenites approached Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the community and said,
32.3: "Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon -
32.4: the land that the Lord defeated in the sight of the community of Israel - is perfect for cattle and your servants have cattle.
32.5: If we've found favor in your sight, let this land be given to your servants as our possession instead of us crossing the Jordan River.
32.6: "Will your relatives have to go to war while you remain here?" Moses asked the Gaddites and Reubenites in response.
32.7: "Why would you discourage the Israelites from crossing over to the land that the Lord has given them -
32.8: That's what your ancestors did when I sent them from Kadesh Barnea to explore the land.
32.9: When they arrived in the Eshcol Valley and saw the land, they discouraged the Israelites from entering the land that the Lord had given them.8
32.10: "That's why the Lord's anger flared up that day and he promised by an oath that
32.11: 'Not one of the men who went up from Egypt from 20 years old and above will see the land that I promised to give to their ancestors, that is, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, because none of them followed me whole heartedly,
32.12: except Jephunneh's son Caleb, the Kenizzite, and Nun's son Joshua. They've whole heartedly followed the Lord.'
32.13: "The Lord's anger had flared up against Israel so that he made them wander in the wilderness for 40 years until that whole generation, who committed evil in the eyes of the Lord, had died.
32.14: And now, look! You're acting just like your ancestors, like a brood of sinful men, who are incrementally provoking the fierce anger of the Lord against the Israelites.
32.15: If you stop following him, he will once again abandon them in the wilderness. You'll end up destroying this entire people."14
32.16: Then they approached him and said, "Here's where we're going to build corrals for our cattle and cities for our families,
32.17: but we will keep ourselves armed and stay ready to go with the Israelites until we've brought them to their own places. Our families intend to live in fortified cities in the presence of the inhabitants of the land,
32.18: but we won't return to our homes until every Israelite has taken possession of each of their inheritances,
32.19: since our inheritance will not be with them across the Jordan River and beyond. Instead, our inheritance is on this side of the Jordan River, facing eastward."
32.20: "If you do this," Moses replied to them, "that is, if you equip yourselves for war in the Lord's presence
32.21: and every one of your armed soldiers crosses over the Jordan River in the presence of the Lord until he has dispossessed his enemies ahead of him
32.22: and subjugated the land before him, then afterwards when you return, you'll be able to stand blameless before the Lord and before Israel. This land will then be your possession before the Lord.
32.23: "But if you won't do so, look out! You will be sinning against the Lord. Be certain of this, that your sin will catch up to you!
32.24: So after you've built cities for your families and corrals for your cattle, be sure to keep your promises."
32.25: Then the Gaddites and Reubenites spoke, "Your servants will do exactly what our master has commanded.
32.26: Our children, wives, flocks, and all our cattle will be settled in the cities of Gilead,
32.27: but every soldier that we've equipped for battle will cross the Jordan River in the presence of the Lord, as our master has spoken."
32.28: So Moses instructed Eleazar the priest and Nun's son Joshua, and the officers of the ancestral tribes of the Israelites,
32.29: telling them, "If the Gaddites and Reubenites cross over the Jordan River with you, that is, all of their soldiers who've been equipped for battle in the Lord's presence, so that the land is subjudated right before your eyes, then you are to give them the land of Gilead as their possession.
32.30: But if the armed men don't cross over with you, then they won't have any possession in the land of Canaan."
32.31: "We'll do just what the Lord told your servants," the Gaddites and the Reubenites responded.
32.32: "We are to cross over in battle array in the Lord's presence into the land of Canaan, and afterwards the possession of our inheritance will be in this side of the Jordan River."
32.33: So Moses gave to the Gaddites, to the Reubenites, and to the half-tribe of Joseph's son Manasseh the kingdom of Sihon, the king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og, the king of Bashan, the whole land with its cities, and even the territories surrounding it.
32.34: The Gaddites rebuilt Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,
32.36: Beth-Nimrah, and Beth-Haran as fortified cities with corrals for sheep.
32.37: The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,
32.38: Nebo, Baal-Meon, (after having changed their names) and Sibmah. The cities that they rebuilt were renamed.
32.39: The descendants of Manasseh's son Machir attacked Gilead and then captured and dispossessed the Amorites who were there.
32.40: That's why Moses gave Gilead to Manasseh's son Machir, who lived there at the time.
32.41: Manasseh's son Jair captured their towns and renamed them Havvoth-Jair.
32.42: Nobah captured Kenath and its towns and renamed it Nobah after himself.

33.1: Here's the travel itinerary for the Israelites after they left the land of Egypt in groups under the authority of Moses and Aaron.
33.2: Moses recorded their departures in their travels after being commanded to do so by the Lord. Here's a list of their travels based on their departures:
33.3: They departed from Raamses on the first month, the fifteenth day of the first month. The day after the Passover, the Israelites came out confidently, and all the Egyptians watched them leave,
33.4: while they were burying their first born, whom the Lord had killed among them. The Lord also executed justice against their gods.
33.5: Then the Israelites traveled from Raamses and rested in Succoth.
33.6: They traveled from Succoth then rested in Etham, which is at the outskirts of the wilderness.
33.7: They traveled from Etham but turned back to Pihahiroth, which is outside of Baal-Zephon. They rested outside of Migdol.
33.8: They traveled from Hahiroth and passed through the midst of the sea to the wilderness. They were on the road three days in the wilderness of Etham then rested in Marah.
33.9: They traveled from Marah and arrived at Elim. In Elim there were twelve wells of water and 70 palm trees, so they rested there.
33.10: They traveled from Elim, then rested by the Reed Sea.
33.11: They traveled from the Reed Sea, then rested in the Wilderness of Sin.
33.12: They traveled from the Wilderness of Sin, then rested in Dophkah.
33.13: They traveled from Dophkah, then rested in Alush.
33.14: They traveled from Alush, then rested in Rephidim, but there was no water there for the people to drink.
33.15: They traveled from Rephedim, then rested in the Wilderness of Sinai.
33.16: They traveled from the Wilderness of Sinai, then rested in Kibroth-Hattaavah.
33.17: They traveled from Kibroth-Hattaavah, then rested in Hazeroth.
33.18: They traveled from Hazeroth, then rested in Rithmah.
33.19: They traveled from Rithmah, then rested in Rimmon-Perez.
33.20: They traveled from Rimmon-Perez, then rested in Libnah.
33.21: They traveled from Libnah, then rested in Rissah.
33.22: They traveled from Rissah, then rested in Kehelathah.
33.23: They traveled from Kehelathah, then rested at Mount Shepher.
33.24: They traveled from Mount Shepher, then rested in Haradah.
33.25: They traveled from Haradah, then rested in Makheloth.
33.26: They traveled from Makheloth, then rested in Tahath.
33.27: They traveled from Tahath, then rested in Terah.
33.28: They traveled from Terah, then rested in Mithkah.
33.29: They traveled from Mithkah, then rested in Hashmonah.
33.30: They traveled from Hashmonah, then rested in Moseroth.
33.31: They traveled from Moseroth, then rested in Bene-Jaakan.
33.32: They traveled from Bene-Jaakan, then rested in Hor-Haggidgad.
33.33: They traveled from Hor-Haggidgad, then rested in Jotbathah.
33.34: They traveled from Jotbathah, then rested in Abronah.
33.35: They traveled from Abronah, then rested in Ezion-Geber.
33.36: They traveled from Ezion-Geber, then rested in the Wilderness of Zin, which is also known as Kadesh.
33.37: They traveled from Kadesh, then rested in Mount Hor at the outskirts of the land of Edom.
33.38: Then Aaron the priest ascended Mount Hor in obedience to the Lord's command and died there, in the fortieth year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month.
33.39: Aaron was 123 years old when he died on Mount Hor.
33.40: Meanwhile, the Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negev in the land of Canaan, heard of the approach of the Israelites,
33.41: who had traveled from Mount Hor and then rested in Zalmonah.
33.42: They traveled from Zalmonah, then rested in Punon.
33.43: They traveled from Punon, then rested in Oboth.
33.44: They traveled from Oboth, then rested in Iye-abarim at the outskirts of Moab.
33.45: They traveled from Iyim, then rested in Dibon-Gad.
33.46: They traveled from Dibon-Gad, then rested in Almon-Diblathaim.
33.47: They traveled from Almon-Diblathaim, then rested in the mountains of Abarim, facing Nebo.
33.48: They traveled from the mountains of Abarim, then rested in the plains of Moab by the Jordan River, across from Jericho.
33.49: They rested by the Jordan River in the area from Beth-Jeshimoth to Abel-Shittim in the plains of Moab.
33.50: Then the Lord told Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan River across from Jericho,
33.51: "Tell the Israelites that when they have crossed the Jordan River to the land of Canaan,
33.52: they are to drive out all the inhabitants of the land and destroy all their idols and their molten images. You are to demolish all their high places,
33.53: take possession of the land, and live in it, because I've given you the land to inherit.
33.54: "You are to divide the land among yourselves by lot according to your families. The larger the families are in number, the larger their inheritance is to be. The lesser the families are  in number, the lesser their inheritance is to be. To whomever the lot falls ,that inheritance goes to him. Divide  it according to your ancestral tribes.
33.55: "But if you fail to drive out the inhabitants of the land before you, their survivors will become irritants in your eyes and thorns in your sides, to prick your sides and afflict you in the very land in which youll be living.
33.56: Then, what I had planned to do to them, I'll start to do to you."

34.2: "Issue these orders to the Israelites： 'You're about to enter the land of Canaan. This territory has been apportioned to you as your inheritance： the entire land of Canaan, all the way to its borders."
34.3: "'To your south is the Wilderness of Zin, bordering Edom. Your southern border is to extend east toward the far end of the Dead Sea,
34.4: then it is to turn southward to the ascent of Akrabbim, cross Zin, and then run south of Kadesh Barnea and proceed from there to Hazar-Addar and across to Azmon.
34.5: Then the border is to turn from Azmon toward the wadi of Egypt and from there to the Mediterranean Sea."
34.6: "The western border is to be the Mediterranean Sea. This is to be the wetern border."34
34.7: "Your northern border is to extend from the Mediterranean Sea to Mount Hor.
34.8: From Mount Hor, you are to mark out the entrance to Hammath, with the border running through Zedad,
34.9: then through Ziphron, and then to Hazar-Enan. This is to be the northern border."
34.10: "You are to mark the border on the east from Hazar-enan to Shepham.
34.11: The border is then to extend from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain, then to the Sea of Chinnereth on the east.
34.12: The border is to continue along the Jordan River all the way to the Dead Sea. This is to be your land, as measured by its boundaries."
34.13: Moses commanded the Israelites, "You are to inherit this land by lot, just as the Lord commanded to give it to the remaining nine and a half tribes.
34.14: The tribes of Reuben, Gad, and half the tribe of Manasseh, as defined by their ancestoral houses, have received their inheritance.
34.15: These two and a half tribes received their inheritance this side of the Jordan River, east of Jericho, facing the rising sun."
34.17: "These are the names of the men who are to divide the land for your inheritance： Eleazar the priest and Nun's son Joshua.
34.18: You are to appoint a leader from each tribe to divide the land for inheritance.
34.19: "These are the names of the men： Appoint Jephunneh's son Caleb from the tribe of Judah,
34.20: Ammihud's son Shemuel from the tribe of Simeon;
34.21: Chislon's son Elidad from the tribe of Benjamin;
34.22: and Jogli's son Bukki is to be leader of the tribe of Dan.
34.23: "From the tribe of Joseph, you are to appoint Ephod's son Hanniel  to be leader of the half tribe of Manasseh;
34.24: Shiphtan's son Kemuel to be leader of the half tribe of Ephraim.
34.25: Parnach's son Elizaphan to be leader of the tribe of Zebulun;
34.26: Azzan's son Paltiel to be leader of the tribe of Issachar;
34.27: Shelomi's son Ahihud to be leader of the tribe of Asher;
34.28: and Ammihud's son Pedahel to be leader of the tribe of Naphtali."
34.29: These are the ones whom the Lord commanded to divide the inheritance of the Israelites in the land of Canaan.

35.1: The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Moab, beside the Jordan River in Jericho. He told him,
35.2: "Instruct the Israelites to set aside a portion of their inheritance for the Levites to live in, along with grazing land surrounding their towns.
35.3: The towns are to be reserved for their dwelling places and the grazing lands are to be reserved for their cattle, livestock, and all their animals.
35.4: "The grazing lands that you are to reserve for use by Levites are to extend 1,500 feet from the walls of the town.
35.5: You are to measure from outside the wall of the town on the east side 3,000 feet, on the south side 3,000 feet, on the west side 3,000 feet, and on the north side 3,000 feet, with the town placed at the center. This reserved area is to serve as grazing land for their towns.
35.6: You are to set aside six towns of refuge from the towns that you will be giving to the Levites, where someone who kills a human being may run for shelter. In addition, give them 42 other towns.
35.7: "The total number of towns that you are to give to the Levites is to be 48 towns, including grazing lands surrounding these town.
35.8: You are to apportion the towns that you will be giving the Israelites according to the relative size of the tribe. Take a larger portion from those larger in numbers and a lesser portion from those lesser in number. Each is to set aside towns for the Levites proportional to the size of their inheritance that they receive."
35.10: "Tell the Israelites that when they have crossed the Jordan River into the land of Canaan,
35.11: they are to designate some towns of refuge so that anyone who kills someone inadvertently may flee there.
35.12: They are to serve as cities of refuge from a blood avenger in order to keep the inadvertent killer from dying until he has stood trial in the presence of the community.
35.13: You are to set aside six towns of refuge.
35.14: Appoint three towns this side of the Jordan River and three towns in the land of Canaan to serve as the towns of refuge,
35.15: that is, places of refuge for the Israelites, the resident alien, and any travelers among them. Anyone who kills a person inadvertently may flee there."12
35.16: "Whoever uses an iron implement to kill someone is to be adjudged a murderer, and that murderer is certainly to be put to death.
35.17: "Furthermore, whoever uses a stone implement to kill someone is to be adjudged a murderer, and that murderer is certainly to be put to death.
35.18: "Also, whoever uses a wooden implement to kill someone with it is to be adjudged a murderer, and that murderer is certainly to be put to death.
35.19: "The blood avenger himself is to execute the murderer. When he meets him, the murderer is to put him to death.
35.20: If the killer shoved him out of hatred, or hurled something at him while waiting in ambush so that he died,
35.21: or if he struck him with his hand out of hatred so that he dies, then the killed is certainly to be put to death for murder. The avenger of blood is to put him to death when he meets him."
35.22: "But if he pushed him suddenly without hatred, or had he hurled something in his direction without waiting in ambush,
35.23: or if he hit him with a stone carelessly so that he was fatally injured, though he isn't his enemy and he wasn't seeking to commit evil against him,
35.24: then the community is to judge between the inadvertent killer and the blood avenger, following these ordinances.
35.25: The community is to release the inadvertent killer from the blood avenger and return him to the town of refuge where he had fled. He is to live there until the High Priest dies, who will have anointed him with holy oil.
35.26: "But if the inadvertent killer leaves the town of refuge where he had fled
35.27: and the blood avenger finds him outside the town of refuge where he had fled and kills him, the blood avenger is not to be found guilty of murder.
35.28: The inadvertent killer is to live in the town of refuge until the High Priest dies. After the death of the High Priest, the inadvertent killer is to return to the land of his inheritance.
35.29: These are to be the statutes and ordinance for you throughout all your generations, regardless of where you live."
35.30: "Every murderer of a human being is to be executed only according to testimony given by multiple witnesses. A single witness is not to result in a death sentence.
35.31: "You are to receive no ransom for the life of a killer who is guilty of murder; instead, he is to die.
35.32: You are not to receive payment of a ransom for someone who had fled to a town of refuge but then left to live in his homeland before the death of the high priest.
35.33: "You are not to pollute the land where you live, because blood defiles the land, and the land cannot atone for blood that has been spilled on it, except through the blood of the one who spilled it.
35.34: You are not to defile the land where you will be living, because I'm living among you. I am the Lord, who lives in Israel."

36.1: The leaders of the ancestral families of the Gileadites, who were descendants of Machir, and descendants of Manasseh, from Joseph's tribe, approached and spoke to Moses and the leaders of the ancestral houses of the Israelites.
36.2: "The Lord commanded my master to apportion the land as an inheritance by lot to the Israelites," they said. "Now my master was ordered by the Lord to give the inheritance of our brother Zelophehad to his daughters.
36.3: "But when they get married to one of the descendants of the tribes of Israel, their inheritances are to be withdrawn from our father's inheritance and added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they are to belong. Consequently, it is to be withdrawn from the portion of our inheritance.
36.4: "But when the Jubilee Year of the Israelites comes, their inheritance will be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they have come to belong. Their inheritance will thus be taken away from the inheritance of our father's tribe!"
36.5: So Moses issued the Israelites these orders based on what the Lord said： "The tribe of the descendants of Joseph has spoken.
36.6: This is what the Lord is commanding the daughters of Zelophehad. If they decide it's a good idea in their opinion to get married only within the family of their father's tribe, then let them get married
36.7: so that the inheritance of the Israelites won't be turned over from one tribe to another. "Each one has an inheritance from his own father's tribe that the Israelites are to maintain.
36.8: Every daughter who is in possession of an inheritance from the Israelites is to marry someone from the families within her father's tribe so the Israelites can retain possession of their ancestral inheritance.
36.9: That way, their inheritance won't be turned over from one tribe to another, because the Israelites are each to maintain their ancestral inheritances."
36.10: Zelophehad's daughters did just what the Lord had commanded Moses
36.11: for Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah： Zelophehad's daughters married their uncle's sons.
36.12: They married into families of the descendants of Manaseh, that is, Joseph's descendants, so that their inheritance remained within the tribe of their ancestor's family.
36.13: These were the commands and the ordinances that the Lord issued to the Israelites through Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan River in Jericho.

1.1: Deut 1These are the words that Moses spoke to the assembly of Israel east of the Jordan River, in the Arabah desert, opposite Suph between Paran, Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Di-zahab.
1.2: It takes 11 days to travel from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea via Mount Seir.
1.3: On the first day of the eleventh month, in the fortieth year, Moses spoke to the Israelites about everything that the Lord had commanded him concerning them.
1.4: This took place after he defeated Sihon, king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon and Og, king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtaroth at Edrei.
1.5: East of the Jordan River, in the land of Moab, Moses began to expound this Law：
1.6: "The Lord our God spoke to us in Horeb. He said, 'You have been at this mountain long enough.
1.7: Break camp, get going, and proceed to the hill country of the Amorites and all the nearby places in the Arabah desert, the highlands, the foothills, the Negev, the coastal plains, all of the land of the Canaanites, and Lebanon as far as the great river, the Euphrates.
1.8: Look! I've given you the land that lies ahead. Go in and possess the land that I, the Lord, promised to give to your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, as well as to their descendants.'"
1.9: "I also told you at that time that I won't be able to sustain you on my own.
1.10: The Lord your God greatly multiplied your numbers, and today you are like the stars in the sky.
1.11: May the Lord, the God of your ancestors, increase your numbers a thousand times more, and may he bless you, as he promised you.
1.12: How can I bear the burden of you and your bickering all by myself?
1.13: Choose for yourselves wise and discerning men, known to your tribes, and appoint them as your leaders.
1.14: "You answered by saying that this plan is a good thing.
1.15: So I chose leaders from your tribes, wise and respected men, and I appointed them over you - commanders of thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens.
1.16: "I charged your judges at that time, 'When you hold a hearing between brothers, judge fairly between a man and his brother or between foreigners.
1.17: When you hold a hearing, don't be partial in judgment toward the least important or toward the great. Never fear men, because judgment belongs to God. If the matter is difficult for you, bring it to me for a hearing.
1.18: I charged you at that time that you must do all of these things.'"
1.19: "Then we set out from Horeb and walked through that vast and dreadful desert, where you observed the road to the Amorite hill country. Just as the Lord our God ordained for us, we finally arrived at Kadesh Barnea.
1.20: I told you at that time, 'You have reached the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord our God is about to give us.
1.21: Look! The Lord your God has given the land that lies before you. Go and possess it, just as the Lord God of your ancestors commanded you. Don't be afraid or discouraged.'
1.22: "Then all of you approached me and said： 'Let's send out men in advance of us so they can survey the land and bring back a report to us on how we'll go up to their cities.'
1.23: Because this suggestion seemed good to me, I chose twelve men from among you, one from each tribe.
1.24: "Then these men set out, went up to the hill county, reached the Eshcol Valley, and surveyed it.
1.25: They hand-picked some of the fruit of the land, brought it down to us, and gave a report that said, 'The land which the Lord is about to give us is good.'"
1.26: "However, your ancestors didn't go up. Instead, they rebelled against the command of the Lord your God.
1.27: You murmured in your tents, 'The Lord hates us. He brought us out of the land of Egypt in order to deliver us to the Amorites so he could destroy us.
1.28: Where can we go?Our brothers discouraged us when they said that the people are bigger and taller than we are. Their cities are tall and fortified to the sky, and we also saw the Anakim there.'27
1.29: "Then I said to you, 'Don't be terrified or afraid of them.
1.30: The Lord your God is the One who will be going ahead of you. He'll fight for you just as he did in Egypt before your eyes.
1.31: In the desert you saw that the Lord carried you like a man carries his son, on every road you traveled until you reached this place.'
1.32: But despite this, you didn't trust in the Lord your God,
1.33: who walked ahead of you along the way to scout a place for you to pitch camp - by fire at night and cloud by day - to lead you on the way you should go."
1.34: "When the Lord heard your complaints, he became angry and declared,
1.35: 'I swear that not one man of this evil generation will see the good land that I promised to give to your ancestors,
1.36: except Jephunneh's son Caleb. He will see it and I will give to him and to his descendants the land on which he has walked because he wholeheartedly followed the Lord.'
1.37: "The Lord was also furious with me because of you. He said： 'You will not enter the land.
1.38: However, Nun's son Joshua, your assistant, will go there. Encourage him, for he will cause Israel to take possession of it.
1.39: Your little ones - whom you said would be taken captive - and your children who do not yet know right from wrong, will enter the land. I will give it to them and they themselves will possess it.
1.40: But as for you, prepare to set out for the desert on the way to the Reed Sea.'
1.41: "You responded to me and said, 'We have sinned against the Lord. We will now go up and fight according to what the Lord our God commanded.' So each man put on his weapon for battle and recklessly started out for the hill country."
1.42: "Then the Lord told me： 'Tell them not to go up and fight because I will not be in their midst, or else you will be defeated before your enemies.'
1.43: "I spoke to you but you didn't listen. Instead you rebelled against the command of the Lord and went up to the hill country.
1.44: The Amorites who lived in the hill country came out to engage you in battle. They pursued you like bees do and crushed you from Seir to Hormah.
1.45: You returned and cried out in the Lord's presence, but the Lord didn't hear your voice or listen to you.
1.46: You remained in Kadesh for many days. It was a long time, indeed."

2.1: "We turned and set out for the desert on the road to the Reed Sea just as the Lord had directed me. We traveled around Mount Seir for many days.
2.2: "Then the Lord said to me,
2.3: 'You went around this mountain long enough. Turn northward
2.4: and command this people, "You are about to pass through the territory of your relatives, the descendants of Esau who live around Seir. They will be afraid of you so be very careful.
2.5: Don't fight them, because I won't give you any part of their land, not even the size of a footprint. I have given Mount Seir to Esau as their property.
2.6: You may buy food to eat and water to drink from them, paying with cash."'
2.7: Indeed, the Lord your God blessed all the works of your hands. He knows about your travels through this vast desert. The Lord your God was with you this past 40 years, so that you didn't lack anything.
2.8: So we bypassed our relatives, the descendants of Esau who live in Seir. We turned through the Arabah desert from Elath, and from Ezion-geber we traveled the desert road to Moab."6
2.9: "Then the Lord said to me, 'Don't harass Moab or provoke them to war, because I won't give you any part of their land. I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot as their property.
2.10: (The Emites, a people as powerful, numerous, and tall as the Anakim, lived there before.
2.11: Like the Anakim, they were thought of as Rephaim, but the Moabites called them Emites.
2.12: The Horites used to live in Seir before the descendants of Esau dispossessed them, exterminated them, and settled there instead, just as Israel will do in the land of its possession, which the Lord gave them.)
2.13: Now get going and cross the Wadi Zered.' And so we crossed the Wadi Zered.11
2.14: "Now from the time we left Kadesh Barnea until we crossed the Wadi Zered was 38 years. All of that generation, the soldiers in the camp, were destroyed just as the Lord swore they would be.
2.15: Indeed, the hand of the Lord was against them to root them out from the camp until they were utterly destroyed."
2.16: "And so all the soldiers among the people died.
2.17: Then the Lord spoke to me,
2.18: 'Today, you are about to cross the border of Moab at Ar.
2.19: When you come to the Ammonites, don't harass or provoke them to war, for I won't give any part of Ammonite land to you, since I have given it to the descendants of Lot as their property.'
2.20: "'(Indeed, it was considered Rephaim territory, since the Rephaim used to lived there. The Ammonites called them Zamzummites,
2.21: a great people, numerous, and tall as the Anakim. But the Lord destroyed the Rephaim, so that the Ammonites dispossessed them and settled there instead.
2.22: This is what he did for the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, when he destroyed the Horites before them. So they dispossessed them and settled there in their place, where they live to this day.
2.23: It was the same for the Avvites who lived in villages as far as Gaza. The Caphtorites, who came from Crete, destroyed them and settled there in their place.)
2.24: "'Get ready and set out for the Wadi Arnon. Look! I've given into your control Sihon king of Heshbon, the Amorite, along with his land. Prepare to take possession by provoking him to war.
2.25: Starting today I will begin to instill fear and terror of you on the part of every nation under heaven who hears reports about you. They'll tremble in anguish before you.'"
2.26: "I sent messengers from the desert of Kedemoth to Sihon king of Heshbon, with this message of peace：
2.27: 'Let me pass through your territory. I'll stay on the main road. I won't turn to the right or left.
2.28: Sell me food for cash so I can eat and give me water for cash, so I can drink. Just let me pass through on foot
2.29: as the descendants of Esau who live in Seir did for me, as did the Moabites who live in Ar. I'll pass through, until I will have crossed the Jordan into the land that the Lord our God is about to give us.'
2.30: But Sihon king of Heshbon did not allow us to pass through, because the Lord your God had hardened his spirit and made him arrogant, in order to deliver him into your control today.
2.31: "Then the Lord said to me, 'See, I've begun to deliver Sihon and his territory over to you. Prepare to take possession of his land.'
2.32: "Sihon came out to meet us, including his entire his army, at the battle of Jahaz.
2.33: The Lord our God delivered him to us, so we attacked him, his son, and his whole army.
2.34: We captured all his towns at that time. We utterly destroyed every town - the men, the women, and the children, leaving no survivors.
2.35: We only appropriated the livestock for our use, along with plunder from the cities that we captured.
2.36: From Aroer on the edge of Arnon Valley and from the town all the way to Gilead, there was no city that was too strong for us - the Lord our God delivered them all to us.
2.37: You did not encroach onto Ammonite land, the banks of the Wadi Jabbok, the towns in the hill country, and all the other places that were forbidden by the Lord our God."

3.1: "We set out and went up along the road to Bashan. Then Og the king of Bashan came out to meet us - he and his whole army - for a battle at Edrei.
3.2: "Then the Lord said to me, 'Don't fear him, because I've delivered him, his army, and his territory into your control. Do to him just as you have done to Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon.'
3.3: "So the Lord our God also delivered into our control Og king of Bashan, along with his whole army. We attacked him until there were no survivors.
3.4: Then we captured all his cities at that time. There was not a city left that we didn't capture from them - sixty cities in all from the region of Argob, which is part of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
3.5: All of these cities were fortified with high walls, gates, and bars. Furthermore, there were very many unwalled regions.
3.6: We utterly destroyed them, just as we did Sihon king of Heshbon, attacking them in every city - the men, women, and children.
3.7: But we kept for ourselves all of the livestock and plunder from the towns.5
3.8: "So at that time we took control from the two Amorite kings the territory east of the Jordan from Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon.
3.9: (The Sidonians called Hermon Sirion, but the Amorites called it Senir.)
3.10: We took control of all the cities of the plain, all of Gilead and Bashan as far as Salecah and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
3.11: Only Og the king of Bashan remained from the remnants of the Rephaim. In fact, his bed was made of iron. It's in Rabbah of the Ammonites, isn't it?It's about thirteen and a half feet long and six feet wide."
3.12: "Of the land that we captured at that time, I've given its towns to the Reubenites and the Gaddites from Aroer near the Wadi Arnon to half of the hill country of Gilead.
3.13: The remainder of Gilead and Bashan of the kingdom of Og, I've given to the half-tribe of Manasseh. (The whole region of Argob - that is, all of Bashan - is called the land of the Rephaim.)
3.14: Manasseh's son Yair captured all the Argob region as far as the territory of the Geshurites and the Maacathites. Bashan was named after him; that's why it is called Havvoth-jair to this day.
3.15: Furthermore, I've given Gilead to Machir.
3.16: And I've given Gilead to the Reubenites and the Gadites as far as the Arnon Valley, designating the middle of the valley as its boundary, including up to the Jabbok River as a boundary with the Ammonites.
3.17: The Arabah and the Jordan River are also a boundary from Kinnereth to the Sea of the Arabah (that is, the Salt Sea), below the slopes of Pisgah on the east."
3.18: "Then I commanded you at that time, 'The Lord your God gave you this land as a possession. Those equipped for battle - every man a warrior - will cross before your fellow Israelites.
3.19: However, your women, children, and livestock - and I know you have many - may reside in your towns that I gave you
3.20: until the Lord grants rest to your fellow Israelites like you. When they take possession of the territory that the Lord your God is about to give them on the east side of the Jordan River, then each of you may return to the territory that I've allotted for you.'
3.21: "I also charged Joshua at that time, 'You witnessed everything that the Lord your God did to the two kings. Indeed, the Lord will do this to all the kingdoms which you are about to enter.
3.22: You are not to fear them, because the Lord your God will fight for you.'"
3.23: "I pleaded with the Lord at that time,
3.24: 'Lord God, you've begun to show your greatness and your strong power to your servant. For what god in heaven or on earth can equal your works and mighty deeds?
3.25: Let me cross over that I may see the good land on the other side of the Jordan River, the good hill country, as well as Lebanon.'
3.26: "However, the Lord was furious with me because of you. He did not listen to me. Instead, the Lord said, 'You are not to speak to me about this matter again!
3.27: Go up to the top of Pisgah and lift your eyes toward the west, north, south, and east. Look with your own eyes, since you won't be able to cross this Jordan River.
3.28: Therefore charge Joshua to be doubly strong, for he will lead this people and cause them to inherit the land that you'll see.'
3.29: We then encamped in the valley opposite Beth-peor."

4.1: "Now, Israel, listen to the statutes and the ordinances that I'm teaching you to observe so you may live and go in to take possession of the land that the Lord, the God of your ancestors, is about to give you.
4.2: Do not add or subtract a thing to what I'm commanding you. Observe the commands of the Lord your God.
4.3: "You saw with your own eyes what he did in Baal Peor. The Lord your God exterminated from among you every man who followed Baal of Peor.
4.4: But all of you who are clinging to the Lord your God are alive today.
4.5: See! I taught you the statutes and the ordinances, just as the Lord God commanded. Therefore, observe them when you enter the land you are about to possess.
4.6: Observe them carefully, for this will show your wisdom and discernment in the eyes of people who'll listen to all these decrees. Then they'll say： 'Surely this great nation is a wise and discerning people.'
4.7: For what great nation has a god so near like the Lord our God whenever we call on him?
4.8: And what great nation has all the decrees and righteous ordinances like all this teaching that I'm giving you today?
4.9: Only guard yourselves carefully so you won't forget the things that you saw and let them slip from your mind for the rest of your life. Tell them to your children and to your grandchildren.
4.10: The day you stood in the presence of the Lord your God in Horeb, the Lord said to me, 'Gather the people before me so they may hear my words, learn to revere me the whole time that they live in the land, and teach them to their children.'"
4.11: "When you approached and stood at the foot of the mountain - a mountain that was blazing with fire at its core while the sky was covered with thick, dark clouds?
4.12: the Lord your God spoke from the midst of the fire. You heard the sound of words, but you saw no form; there was only a voice.
4.13: He declared to you his covenant, which he commanded you to observe - the Ten Commandments that he wrote on two stone tablets.
4.14: The Lord commanded me at that time to teach you to observe the statutes and ordinances in the land after you cross over to take possession of it.11
4.15: "Therefore, for your own sake, be very careful, since you did not see any form on the day that the Lord your God spoke to you in Horeb from the midst of the fire.
4.16: Be careful! Otherwise, you will be destroyed when you make carved images for yourself - all sorts of images in the form of man, woman,
4.17: any animal on earth, any winged bird that flies in the sky,
4.18: any creeping thing on the ground, or any fish in the sea.
4.19: Do not gaze toward the heavens and observe the sun, the moon, the stars - the entire array of the sky - with the intent to worship and serve what the Lord your God gave every nation.
4.20: For the Lord took you and brought you out of the iron-smelting furnace - out of Egypt - to be the people of his inheritance, as you are today.
4.21: "But the Lord was angry with me because of you. So he swore that I'll never cross the Jordan River to enter the good land that the Lord your God is about to give you as an inheritance.
4.22: For I'm going to die in this land and I won't cross the Jordan River, but you're about to cross over to possess that good land.
4.23: Be careful! Otherwise, you will forget the covenant of the Lord your God, who established that covenant with you. Don't make carved images of any likeness in violation of everything that you were commanded by the Lord your God.
4.24: Indeed, the Lord your God is a consuming fire. He is a jealous God."
4.25: "After you've borne children and grandchildren, have been there for a long time in the land, have become so corrupted that you make images of any form, and have done evil in the eyes of the Lord your God, you will provoke him to anger.
4.26: Heaven and earth will testify against what has occurred today： you'll surely and swiftly be destroyed from the land that you are about to possess by crossing the Jordan River. You won't live long in it, because you'll certainly be exterminated.
4.27: Moreover, the Lord will scatter you among the peoples, and you'll be fewer in number in the nations where the Lord your God will drive you.
4.28: There you'll serve gods made by human hands, serving trees and stones that cannot see, hear, eat, nor smell.
4.29: "If from there you will seek the Lord your God, then you will find him if you seek him with all your heart and soul.
4.30: "In your distress, when all these things happen to you in days to come and you return to the Lord your God, then you will hear his voice.
4.31: For God is compassionate. The Lord your God won't fail you. He won't destroy you or forget the covenant that he confirmed with your ancestors."
4.32: "Indeed, ask from one end of the heavens to the other about days of old, before your time when God created mankind on the earth. Did we ever have anything as great as this, or ever hear of anything like it?
4.33: Has any people heard the voice of God speaking from the middle of a fire just as you did, and survived it?
4.34: Or has any god ever taken for himself one nation out from another nation with testings, signs, wonders, wars, awesome power, and magnificent, terrifying deeds as the Lord your God did in Egypt before your eyes - 33
4.35: "You have been shown this in order to know that 'the Lord is God' and there is no one like him.
4.36: You have been made to hear his voice from heaven so you may be instructed. And he showed you his great fire here on earth, and you heard his voice from the middle of that fire.
4.37: Moreover, he loved your ancestors, chose their descendants after them, and brought you out of Egypt, accompanied by his presence and great power,
4.38: in order to drive out nations that are stronger and more powerful than you, to bring you into this land, and to give you their land as an inheritance, as it is today.
4.39: "May you acknowledge and take to heart this day that the Lord is God in the heavens above and over the earth below - there is no other God.
4.40: May you observe his statutes and keep his commands that I'm giving you today, so that life may go well for you and for your descendants after you. That way, you'll live a long life in the land that the Lord your God is about to give you permanently."
4.41: Then Moses designated three cities on the east side of the Jordan,
4.42: where a person who accidentally killed someone could flee, if he killed his neighbor without having enmity toward him in the past. He may flee to one of these cities and live：
4.43: Bezer in the desert plain for the Reubenites, Ramoth in Gilead for the Gadites, and Golan in Bashan for the Manassites.
4.44: This is the Law that Moses reviewed in the presence of the Israelites.
4.45: These are the instructions, decrees, and ordinances that Moses declared to the Israelites when they came out of Egypt.
4.46: He did this east of the Jordan, in the valley opposite Beth Peor, in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, and whom Moses and the Israelites defeated after leaving Egypt.
4.47: So they took possession of his land, as well as the land of Og king of Bashan. Both Amorite kings lived east of the Jordan -
4.48: from Aroer on the edge of the Wadi Arnon as far as Mount Sirion, which is also called Hermon,
4.49: and all the Arabah east of the Jordan as far as the Dead Sea below the slopes of Pisgah.4

5.1: Moses called all of Israel together and told them： "Listen, Israel! Today I'm going to announce God's laws and regulations so that you will learn them and take care to obey them.
5.2: When the Lord our God made a covenant with us in Horeb,
5.3: it was not with our ancestors that the Lord made this covenant, but with us - we who are here today - all of us who are now living.
5.4: The Lord spoke to you face to face on the mountain from the fire.
5.5: I stood at that time as mediator between the Lord and you to declare his message to you, because you were afraid of the fire and would not go up the mountain. He said:
5.6: "I am the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt - from the house of slavery.
5.7: "You are to have no other gods besides me.
5.8: You are not to make for yourselves a carved image resembling any form in the heavens above, on earth below, or in the waters under the earth.
5.9: You are not to bow down to them in worship or serve them; because I, the Lord your God, am a jealous God, punishing the children for the iniquity of their parents, to the third and fourth generations of those who hate me,
5.10: but showing gracious love to the thousandth generation of those who love me and keep my commandments.
5.11: "You are not to misuse the name of the Lord your God, because the Lord will not leave the one who misuses his name unpunished.
5.12: "Keep the Sabbath day holy, just as the Lord your God commanded.
5.13: Six days you are to labor and do all your work,
5.14: but the seventh day is a Sabbath for the Lord your God. You are not to do any work： neither you, your children, your male and female servants, your oxen and donkeys, all your livestock, as well as the foreigners who live among you, so that your male and female servants may rest as you do. 1
5.15: Remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt, but the Lord your God brought you out from there with great power and a show of force. Therefore the Lord your God has commanded you to observe the Sabbath day.12
5.16: "Honor your father and your mother, just as the Lord your God commanded you, so that your life will be long and things will go well for you in the land that the Lord your God is giving you.
5.17: "You are not to murder.
5.18: "You are not to commit adultery.
5.19: "You are not to steal.
5.20: "You are not to testify falsely against your neighbor.
5.21: "You are not to desire your neighbor's wife, or covet your neighbor's house, fields, his male and female servants, his ox, his donkey, or anything that concerns your neighbor."
5.22: "These commands the Lord declared in a loud voice to your entire assembly on the mountain from out of the fire and dark clouds, and nothing more was added. He inscribed them on two tablets of stones and gave them to me.
5.23: "When you heard the voice from the darkness while the mountain was blazing, all the leaders and elders of your tribes came to me and said：
5.24: 'The Lord our God truly has displayed his glory and power, for we heard him from out of the fire today. We have witnessed how God spoke to human beings, yet they lived.
5.25: Now therefore, why should we die?This great fire will consume us. If we continue to listen to the voice of the Lord our God any longer, we'll die.
5.26: For what mortal man has heard the voice of the living God speaking out of the fire like we did, and lived?
5.27: As for you, go near and listen to everything that the Lord our God will say to you. Then repeat it to us, then we will listen and obey.'5
5.28: "The Lord heard what you said. He said to me： 'I've heard what this people said. Everything they said was good.
5.29: If only they would commit to fear me and keep all my commands, then it will go well with them and their children forever.5
5.30: "'Go and tell them to return to their tents,
5.31: but you stand here with me and I'll speak to you all the commands, decrees, and laws that you must teach them to observe in the land that I'm giving you to possess.
5.32: You must be careful to do what the Lord your God commanded you, turning neither to the left nor to the right.
5.33: Walk in every way that the Lord your God commanded you, so that life may go well for you, and so that you will prolong your days in the land that you will possess.'"

6.1: "Now these are the commands, decrees, and ordinances that the Lord commanded me to teach you. Obey them in the land you are entering to possess,
6.2: so that you, your children, and your grandchildren may fear the Lord your God. Keep all his decrees and commandments that I'm giving you every day of your life, so you may live a long time.
6.3: "Listen, Israel! Be careful to obey, so that life may go well for you and that you may increase greatly. Just as the Lord God of your ancestors told you, you'll have a land flowing with milk and honey.
6.4: "Listen, Israel! The Lord is our God, the Lord alone.
6.5: You are to love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, and all your strength.
6.6: "Let these words that I'm commanding you today be always on your heart.
6.7: Teach them repeatedly to your children. Talk about them while sitting in your house or walking on the road, and as you lie down or get up.
6.8: Tie them as reminders on your forearm, bind them on your forehead,
6.9: and write them on the door frames of your house and on your gates."7
6.10: "When the Lord your God brings you to the land that he promised to your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, he will give you large and beautiful cities that you didn't build,
6.11: houses filled with every good thing that you didn't supply, wells that you didn't dig, and vineyards and olive groves that you didn't plant. When you eat and are satisfied,
6.12: be careful not to forget the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt and slavery.
6.13: Fear the Lord your God, serve him, and make your oaths in his name.
6.14: Do not follow other gods, from the gods of the peoples around you.
6.15: For the Lord your God who is among you is a jealous God. He will turn his anger against you and destroy you from the surface of the land."
6.16: "Don't test the Lord your God like you tried to do in Massah.
6.17: Be sure to observe the commands of the Lord your God, his testimonies and his decrees that he gave you.
6.18: Do what is good and right in the Lord's sight so it may go well with you. Then you'll enter and possess the good land that the Lord your God promised to your ancestors,
6.19: expelling all your enemies before you, as the Lord said."
6.20: "When your son asks you in the future, 'What is the meaning of the instructions, decrees, and ordinances that the Lord our God commanded you?'
6.21: tell him, 'We were slaves to Pharaoh in Egypt, but the Lord brought us out of Egypt with great power.
6.22: Before our very eyes, the Lord did great and terrible signs and wonders in Egypt, to Pharaoh, and to his entire household.
6.23: But as for us, he brought us out from there to bring us into the land and it to us, as he promised our ancestors.
6.24: Then the Lord commanded us to observe all these decrees and to fear the Lord our God for our own good, so that he may keep us alive as we are today.
6.25: It will be credited as righteousness for us, if we're careful to obey the entire law in the presence of the Lord our God, as he commanded.'"

7.1: "When the Lord your God brings you into the land that you are entering to possess, he will drive out many nations before you： the Hittites, Girgashites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites, seven nations who are more numerous and stronger than you.
7.2: So when the Lord your God delivers them to you and you have defeated them, then utterly destroy them. You are not to make any covenant with them nor be gracious to them.
7.3: You are not to intermarry with them. You are not to give your daughters to their sons nor take their daughters for your sons,
7.4: because they will turn your children from me to serve other gods so that the Lord's anger blazes against you and swiftly destroys you by fire.
7.5: This is what you will do to them： tear down their altars, break their pillars, cut down their ritual pillars, and burn their carved idols in fire,
7.6: because you are a holy people to the Lord your God. The Lord your God chose you to be his people, his treasured possession from all the peoples on the face of the earth."
7.7: "It was not because you were more numerous than other people of the earth that the Lord committed himself to you and chose you. In fact, you were the least numerous of all the peoples.
7.8: But the Lord loved you and kept his oath that he made to your ancestors. The Lord brought you out with great power from slavery, from the control of Pharaoh, king of Egypt.
7.9: "Know that the Lord your God is God, the trusted God who faithfully keeps his covenant to the thousandth generation of those who love him and obey his commands.
7.10: But for the one who hates him, he will repay him by destroying him. He will not delay dealing with someone who hates him.
7.11: Therefore keep the commands, decrees, and the ordinances that I am instructing you to obey today."
7.12: "If you pay attention to these laws and obey them, then the Lord your God will continue with you his covenant of gracious love that he promised with an oath to your ancestors.
7.13: He will love you and increase your numbers. He will bless the fruit of your womb, the fruit of your land (the grain, new wine, and oil), the offspring of your herds, and the lambs of your flock, in the land that the Lord promised your ancestors he would give you.
7.14: You will be blessed among all the nations. There will be no infertility among you, not even among your herds.
7.15: The Lord will turn aside every disease from you. He will not inflict on you the terrible diseases you knew in Egypt, but will inflict them instead on all who hate you.
7.16: You are to utterly destroy everyone whom the Lord your God will deliver to you. Do not have pity on them nor serve their gods. Otherwise, they will become a snare for you."13
7.17: "You may say to yourselves, 'These nations are more numerous than we are. How can we dispossess them?'
7.18: But you must not fear them. Be sure to remember what the Lord your God did to Pharaoh and all of Egypt.
7.19: Your eyes saw the great trials, the signs and wonders, the awesome power with which the Lord your God brought you out.
7.20: "The Lord your God will do the same to all the people whom you fear. He'll send plagues against them until the survivors who hide from you have perished.
7.21: Do not tremble before them, for the Lord your God, who is among you, is a great and awesome God.
7.22: He slowly will dislodge these nations before you, but he will not destroy them quickly, so the wild animals won't multiply around you.
7.23: But the Lord your God will deliver them over to you, throwing them into great confusion until they are destroyed.
7.24: He will deliver kings into your control, and you will wipe out the memory of them from under heaven. No one will be able to stand before you. You are to utterly destroy them.
7.25: Burn the images of their gods in the fire. Desire neither the silver nor the gold that adorns them, nor take them for yourselves, so you won't be ensnared by them, because the gold and silver is detestable to the Lord your God.
7.26: Do not bring any detestable thing to your house, because you yourself will be utterly destroyed along with these detestable things. You must absolutely abhor and detest all of it, because it has been devoted to destruction."

8.1: "Be careful to observe every command that I'm instructing you today, in order that you may live, increase, and enter and take possession of the land that the Lord promised by an oath to your ancestors.
8.2: "Remember how the Lord your God led you all the way these 40 years in the desert, to humble and test you in order to make known what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep his commands.
8.3: He humbled you, causing you to be hungry, yet he fed you with manna that neither you nor your ancestors had known, in order to teach you that human beings shall not live by food alone - instead human beings are to live by every word that proceeds from the mouth of the Lord.
8.4: "The clothes you wore did not wear out, nor did your feet blister during these 40 years.
8.5: Be convinced in your heart that as a father disciplines his son, so the Lord your God disciplines you.
8.6: Observe the commands of the Lord your God by walking in his ways and by fearing him,
8.7: because the Lord your God is bringing you to a good land - a land with rivers and deep springs flowing to the valleys and hills.
8.8: It's a land filled with wheat, barley, vines, fig trees, and pomegranates. It's a land filled with olive oil and honey?
8.9: a land without scarcity. You'll eat food in it and lack nothing. It's a land where its rocks are iron and you can dig copper from its mountains."
8.10: "When you have eaten and are satisfied, bless the Lord your God for the good land that he has given you.
8.11: Be careful! Otherwise, you will forget the Lord your God by failing to keep his commands, ordinances, and statutes that I'm commanding you this day.
8.12: Otherwise, when you eat and are satisfied, when you have built beautiful houses and lived in them,
8.13: when your cattle and oxen multiply, when your silver and gold increase,
8.14: then you will become arrogant. You'll neglect the Lord your God,
8.15: who brought you out of the land of Egypt, from the house of slavery, and who led you through the vast and dangerous desert, that parched land without water, with its poisonous snakes and scorpions. He brought water out of solid rock for you,
8.16: and fed you in the desert with manna that neither you nor your ancestors had known, to humble and test you so that things go well with you later.812
8.17: "You may say to yourselves, 'I have become wealthy by my own strength and by my own ability.'
8.18: But remember the Lord your God, because he is the one who gives you the ability to produce wealth, in order to confirm his covenant that he promised by an oath to your ancestors, as is the case today.
8.19: If you neglect the Lord your God, follow other gods, and serve and worship them, I testify to you today that you will certainly be destroyed.
8.20: Just like the nations whom the Lord destroyed before you, so will you be destroyed, because you did not listen to the voice of the Lord your God."

9.1: "Listen, Israel! Today you are about to cross the Jordan to enter and dispossess greater and mightier nations than you, who live in large cities that are fortified to the sky.
9.2: The Anakim are strong and tall, and you know them. You've heard it said, 'Who can stand up against the Anakim?'
9.3: But know today that the Lord your God is going ahead of you as a consuming fire. He will destroy and subdue them before you. He will dispossess and destroy them quickly, just as the Lord told you.
9.4: After the Lord has expelled them before you, you are not to say to yourselves, 'The Lord caused me to enter and possess this land because of my righteousness.'
9.5: On the contrary, it is because of the wickedness of these nations that the Lord is dispossessing them before you to confirm what the Lord promised by an oath to your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
9.6: Know that it is not because of your righteousness that the Lord your God is giving to you this good land to inherit, for you are a stubborn people."
9.7: "Remember - and don't ever forget - how you provoked the Lord your God in the desert. From the day that you came out of the land of Egypt until you came to this place you have been rebelling against the Lord.
9.8: At Horeb you continuously rebelled against the Lord, so that he was angry enough to destroy you.
9.9: "Then I went up to the mountain to receive the two stone tablets of the covenant that the Lord had established with you. I stayed on the mountain for 40 days and nights without eating food or drinking water.
9.10: Then the Lord gave me the two stone tablets on which God inscribed with his own finger all the words that the Lord spoke to you on the mountain from the middle of the fire that day when you were all assembled together.
9.11: At the end of 40 days and nights, the Lord gave to me the two stone tablets of the covenant.
9.12: "Then the Lord said to me, 'Get going! Go down from here at once! Your people whom you brought out of Egypt have become corrupt. They have turned quickly from the way that I commanded them, and have cast an idol for their use.'
9.13: "Then the Lord said to me, 'I have examined this people, and they are stubborn indeed.
9.14: Let me alone! I will destroy them and blot out their name under heaven. Then I'll make you into a nation that will be mighty and more numerous than they are.'
9.15: "So I turned and went down from the mountain while the mountain was on fire. The two tablets of the covenant were in both of my hands.
9.16: Then I saw how you had really sinned against the Lord your God! You had made for yourselves a calf, a cast idol. You had turned aside quickly from the way that the Lord your God had commanded.
9.17: So I grabbed the two tablets and then threw them out of my hands, breaking them before your eyes.
9.18: I fell down in the Lord's presence, just as I had the first 40 days and nights. I did not eat food or drink water because of your sin. You had sinned by committing this evil in the sight of the Lord, thereby provoking him to anger.
9.19: I feared the anger and wrath of the Lord against you, because he was irate enough to destroy you. But the Lord also listened to me at that time.
9.20: It was as had been the case with Aaron, the Lord was very angry and about to destroy him, but I prayed for Aaron at that time.
9.21: Now, when you made the calf that made you sin, I grabbed it, burned it with fire, crushed it, and ground it thoroughly until it was pulverized to powder. Then I threw the powder into the river that was flowing from the mountain."18
9.22: "You provoked the Lord again at Taberah, Massah, and Kibroth-hattaavah.
9.23: When the Lord sent you from Kadesh Barnea and told you, 'Go possess the land that I gave you,' instead you disobeyed what the Lord your God said. You didn't trust him or listen to his voice.
9.24: You have been rebelling against the Lord since the day I knew you.
9.25: I fell down in the Lord's presence for 40 days and nights, because the Lord said he was ready to destroy you.
9.26: So I prayed to the Lord and said, 'Oh Lord my God, don't destroy your people and your inheritance whom you redeemed by your power. You brought them out from Egypt in a powerful way.
9.27: Remember your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Don't pay attention to the stubbornness, wickedness, and sinfulness of this people.
9.28: Otherwise, the people of the land from which you brought us will say, "The Lord wasn't able to bring them out of the land that he had promised them. So he brought them out to kill them in the desert because he hated them."
9.29: But they are your people and inheritance, whom you brought out by your mighty strength and awesome power.'"927

10.1: "At that time, the Lord said to me, 'Chisel two tablets of stone for yourself just like the first ones, and then come up to me on the mountain. Also make for yourself a wooden chest.
10.2: I'll write on the tablets what was on the first tablets that you broke. Then place them in the wooden chest.'
