24.11: King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came up against the city, along with his servants, who besieged it.
24.12: King Jehoiachin of Judah surrendered to the king of Babylon, as did his mother, his servants, his princes, and his officers, during the eighth year of his reign.824
24.13: Nebuchadnezzar carried off from there all of the treasures of the Lord's temple, along with the treasures in the king's palace. He cut into pieces all the gold vessels in the Lord's temple that King Solomon of Israel had made, just as the Lord had said would happen.
24.14: Then Nebuchadnezzar sent away into exile all of Jerusalem - all the captains, all the valiant soldiers, 10,000 captives, and all of the craftsmen and ironworkers. Nobody remained except the poorest people of the land.
24.15: He sent Jehoiachin into exile to Babylon, along with the king's mother, the king's wives, his officials, and the leading men of the land. He took them into exile from Jerusalem to Babylon.
24.16: All 7,000 of the most valiant soldiers, 1,000 of the craftsmen and ironworkers, all physically fit and trained for battle were brought by the king of Babylon into exile in Babylon.
24.17: The king of Babylon installed Jehoiachin's uncle Mattaniah as king in his place and then changed his name to Zedekiah.
24.18: Zedekiah was 21 years old when he became king. He reigned for eleven yeas in Jerusalem. His mother was named Hamutal. She was the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
24.19: Zedekiah practiced what the Lord considered to be evil, just as Jehoiakim had done,
24.20: because through the Lord's anger these things happened to Jerusalem and Judah until he threw them from his presence. Zedekiah then rebelled against the king of Babylon,

25.1: so on the tenth day of the tenth month of the ninth year of Nebuchadnezzar's reign as king of Babylon, he and his entire army approached Jerusalem, attacked it, encamped against it, and built a siege wall that surrounded the city.
25.2: The city remained under siege until the eleventh year of the reign of King Zedekiah.
25.3: By the ninth day of the fourth month, the resulting famine had become so severe in the city that no food remained for the people who lived in the land.
25.4: The city was breached, and the entire army left during the night through the gate that stood between the two walls beside the royal garden, even though the Chaldeans had surrounded the city. They escaped by way of the Arabah,
25.5: but the Chaldean army pursued the king and overtook him in the Jericho plains, where his entire army was scattered.
25.6: The Chaldeans captured the king, brought him to Riblah, where the king of Babylon determined his sentence.
25.7: They executed Zedekiah's sons in his presence, blinded Zedekiah, bound him with bronze chains, and transported him to Babylon.
25.8: On the seventh day of the fifth month, which was during the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar's reign as king of Babylon, captain of the guard Nebuzaradan, a servant of the king of Babylon, arrived in Jerusalem,
25.9: set fire to the Lord's temple, the royal palace, and all the houses of Jerusalem. He even set fire to the lavish homes.
25.10: The Chaldean army that accompanied the captain of the guard demolished the walls that surrounded Jerusalem.
25.11: Nebuzaradan, the captain of the guard, carried the survivors of the people who remained in the city, those who had deserted to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the multitude into exile.
25.12: However, the captain of the guard left some of the poor people of the land to work as vinedressers and farmers.
25.13: The Chaldeans also broke into pieces and carried back to Babylon the bronze pillars that stood in the Lord's temple, along with the stands and the bronze sea that used to be in the Lord's temple.
25.14: They also confiscated the pots, shovels, snuffers, spoons, and the rest of the bronze vessels that were used in ministry.
25.15: The captain of the guard also confiscated the fire pans, basins, and whatever had been crafted of pure gold and pure silver.
25.16: The bronze contained in the two pillars, the one sea, and the stands that Solomon had crafted for the Lord's temple could not be inventoried for weight.
25.17: The height of one of the pillars was 24 feet, and the capital on top of it was four and a half feet high. A latticework carved in the form of pomegranates encircled the capital, crafted completely out of brass. The second pillar was identical to the first.
25.18: The captain of the guard arrested Seraiah the chief priest, Zephaniah the second priest, three temple officials,
25.19: one overseer from the city who supervised the soldiers, five of the king's advisors who had been discovered in the city, the scribe who served the army captain who mustered the army of the land, and 60 men of the land who were discovered in the city.
25.20: Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them to the king of Babylon at Riblah,
25.21: where the king of Babylon executed them in the land of Hamath. And so Judah was transported into exile from the land.19
25.22: Now as for the people who remained in the land of Judah whom King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon had left behind, he appointed Ahikam's son Gedaliah, the grandson of Shaphan, to rule.
25.23: When all the captains of the armies, along with their men, heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah, these men visited Gedaliah at Mizpah： Nethaniah's son Ishmael, Careah's son Johanan, Tanhumeth the Netophathite's son Seraiah, and Jaazanaiah, who was descended from the Maacathites.
25.24: Gedaliah made this promise to them and to their men： "Don't be afraid of the servants of the Chaldeans. Live in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and things will go well with you."
25.25: Nevertheless, seven months later Nethaniah's son Ishmael, the grandson of Elishama from the royal family, came with ten men and attacked Gedaliah. As a result, he died along with the Jews and Chaldeans who were with him at Mizpah.
25.26: Then all the people, including those who were insignificant and those who were important, fled with the captains of the armed forces to Egypt, because they were afraid of the Chaldeans.23
25.27: Later on, after King Jehoiachin of Judah had been in exile for 37 years, on the twenty-seventh day of the twelfth month, during the first year of his reign King Evil-merodach of Babylon released King Jehoiachin of Judah from prison.
25.28: He spoke kindly to him, and elevated his position above the thrones of the kings with him in Babylon.
25.29: Jehoiachin changed out of his prison clothes and had regular meals in the king's presence every day for the rest of his life,
25.30: and a regular stipend was provided to him by the king in accordance with his needs for as long as he lived.

1.1: Adam fathered Seth, who fathered Enosh,
1.2: who fathered Kenan, who fathered Mahalalel, who fathered Jared,
1.3: who fathered Enoch, who fathered Methuselah, who fathered Lamech,
1.4: who fathered Noah, who fathered Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
1.5: Japheth's descendants were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
1.6: Gomer's descendants were Ashkenaz, Diphath,?and Togarmah.
1.7: Javan's descendants were Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim. -  -
1.8: Ham's descendants were Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
1.9: Cush's descendants were Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raama, and Sabteca. Raamah's descendants were Sheba and Dedan.
1.10: Cush fathered Nimrod. He became the first powerful ruler on the earth.
1.11: Mitzraim fathered the Ludim, the Anamim, the Lehabim, the Naphtuhim,
1.12: the Pathrusim, the Casluhim (from whom the Philistines descended), and the Caphtorim.  -
1.13: Canaan fathered Sidon his firstborn, as well as Heth,
1.14: and the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
1.15: the Hivites, the Archites, the Sinites,
1.16: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.
1.17: Shem's descendants were Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, Aram, Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.?
1.18: Arpachshad fathered Shelah and Shelah fathered Eber.
1.19: Eber fathered two sons. The name of the one was Peleg (because the earth was divided during his lifetime) and his brother was named Joktan.
1.20: Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
1.21: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
1.22: Ebal, Abimael, Sheba,
1.23: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab - all of these were Joktan's descendants.
1.24: In summary, Shem fathered Arpachshad, who fathered Shelah,
1.25: who fathered Eber, who fathered Peleg, who fathered Reu,
1.26: who fathered Serug, who fathered Nahor, who fathered Terah,
1.27: who fathered Abram - that is, Abraham.
1.28: Abraham's descendants were Isaac and Ishmael.
1.29: These are their genealogies： the firstborn Ishmael fathered Nebaioth, and then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
1.31: Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah - these are the Ishmaelites.
1.32: The descendants born to Keturah, Abraham's mistress, were Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. The descendants of Jokshan were Sheba and Dedan.
1.33: The descendants of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the descendants of Keturah.
1.34: Abraham fathered Isaac. Isaac's descendants were Esau and Israel.
1.35: Esau's descendants were Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
1.36: Eliphaz's descendants were Teman, Omar, Zephi, Gatam, Kenaz, Timna, and Amalek.
1.37: Reuel's descendants were Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
1.38: Seir's descendants were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.
1.39: Lotan's descendants were Hori and Homam. Lotan's sister was Timna.
1.40: Shobal's descendants were Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam. Zibeon's descendants were Aiah and Anah.
1.41: Anah's descendant was Dishon. Dishon's descendants were Hamran, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
1.42: Ezer's descendants were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Jaakan. Dishan's descendants were Uz and Aran.
1.43: Here's a list of kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the Israelites, beginning with Beor's son Bela (his city was named Dinhabah).
1.44: After Bela died, Zerah's son Jobab from Bozrah succeeded him.
1.45: After Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites succeeded him.
1.46: After Husham died, Bedad's son Hadad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, succeeded him. His city was named Avith.
1.47: After Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah succeeded him.
1.48: After Samlah died, Shaul?from Rehoboth on the Euphrates River succeeded him.
1.49: After Shaul?died, Achbor's son Baal-hanan succeeded him.
1.50: After Baal-hanan died, Hadad succeeded him. His city was named Pai, and his wife's name was Mehetabel. She was the daughter of Matred, who was the daughter of Me-zahab.
1.51: Then Hadad died. The clans?of Edom included the clans of Timna, Aliah, Jetheth,
1.54: Magdiel, and Iram - these are the clans? - of Edom.

2.1: Here's a list of Israel's sons： Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun,
2.2: Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
2.3: Judah's three sons Er, Onan, and Shelah were born to him through Bath-shua, a Canaanite. Er, Judah's firstborn, became wicked in the Lord's sight, so he put him to death.
2.4: Judah's daughter-in-law Tamar also bore him Perez and Zerah, so Judah had five sons in all.
2.5: Perez's sons were Hezron and Hamul.
2.6: Zerah had five sons in all： Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara - .
2.7: Carmi's son was Achar, who became Israel's troublemaker by transgressing the Lord's commandment regarding things that were to be destroyed.
2.8: Ethan's son was Azariah.
2.9: Hezron's sons born to him were Jerahmeel, Ram, and Chelubai.
2.10: Ram fathered Amminadab, and Amminadab fathered Nahshon, who was leader of the descendants of Judah.
2.11: Nahshon fathered Salma, Salma fathered Boaz,
2.12: Boaz fathered Obed, and Obed fathered Jesse.
2.13: Jesse fathered Eliab his firstborn, Abinadab his second born, Shimea his third born,
2.14: Nethanel his fourth born, Raddai his fifth born,
2.15: Ozem his sixth born, David his seventh born;
2.16: along with their sisters Zeruiah and Abigail. Zeruiah's three sons were Abishai, Joab, and Asahel.
2.17: Abigail bore Amasa, whose father was Jether the Ishmaelite.
2.18: Hezron's son Caleb had children by his wife Azubah and by Jerioth. These were her sons： Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
2.19: When Azubah died, Caleb married Ephrath, who bore him Hur.
2.20: Hur fathered Uri, and Uri fathered Bezalel.
2.21: Later, Hezron married the daughter of Machir, who had fathered Gilead. He married her when he was 60 years old, and she bore him Segub.
2.22: Segub fathered Jair, who had
2.23: towns in the land of Gilead. 23But Geshur and Aram took 60 towns from Gilead, including Havavoth-jair and Kenath, along with their villages. All these were descendants of Machir, who fathered Gilead.22
2.24: After Hezron died in Caleb-ephrathah, Abijah wife of Hezron bore him Ashhur, who fathered Tekoa.
2.25: The descendants of Jerahmeel, the firstborn of Hezron, were Ram his firstborn, Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah.
2.26: Jerahmeel also had another wife, whose name was Atarah; she was the mother of Onam.
2.27: The descendants of Ram, firstborn of Jerahmeel were Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.
2.28: Onam's descendants were Shammai and Jada. Shammai's descendants were Nadab and Abishur.
2.29: Abishur's wife was named Abihail. She bore him Ahban and Molid.
2.30: Nadab's descendants were Seled and Appaim. Seled died childless.
2.31: Appaim's son was Ishi. Ishi's son?was Sheshan. Sheshan's son was Ahlai.
2.32: Shammai's brother Jada's descendants were Jether and Jonathan, but Jether died childless.
2.33: Jonathan's descendants were Peleth and Zaza. These were the descendants of Jerahmeel.
2.34: Now Sheshan had no sons, only daughters. However, Sheshan had an Egyptian slave named Jarha.
2.35: So Sheshan gave his daughter in marriage to his slave Jarha, and she bore him Attai.
2.36: Attai fathered Nathan, and Nathan fathered Zabad.
2.37: Zabad fathered Ephlal, Ephlal fathered Obed,
2.38: Obed fathered Jehu, Jehu fathered Azariah,
2.39: Azariah fathered Helez, Helez fathered Eleasah,
2.40: Eleasah fathered Sismai, Sismai fathered Shallum.
2.41: Shallum fathered Jekamiah, and Jekamiah fathered Elishama.
2.42: Jerahmeel's brother Caleb's descendants were his firstborn Mesha, who fathered Ziph. The descendants of Mareshah, who fathered Hebron, were as follows:
2.43: Hebron's descendants were Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.
2.44: Shema fathered Raham, who fathered Jorkeam. Rekem fathered Shammai.
2.45: Shammai's descendants included Maon, who fathered Beth-zur.
2.46: Caleb's mistress Ephah also bore Haran, Moza, and Gazez. Haran fathered Gazez.
2.47: Jahdai's descendants were Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.
2.48: Caleb's mistress Maacah bore Sheber, Tirhanah,
2.49: and Shaaph, who fathered Madmannah. Sheva fathered Machbenah and Gibe. Caleb's daughter was Achsah.
2.50: These were Caleb's descendants. The son?of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah, was Shobal, who fathered Kiriath-jearim,
2.51: Salma, who fathered Bethlehem, and Hareph, who fathered Beth-gader.2
2.52: Shobal, who fathered Kiriath-jearim, had other sons, including Haroeh, half of the Menuhoth.
2.53: The families of Kiriath-jearim included the Ithrites, the Puthites, the Shumathites, and the Mishraites. The Zorathites and the Eshtaolites came from them.
2.54: Salma's descendants were Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atroth-beth-joab, and half of the Manahathites, the Zorites.
2.55: The families of the scribes who lived at Jabez included the Tirathites, the Shimeathites, and the Sucathites. These are the Kenites who came from Hammath, who fathered the house of Rechab.

3.1: These are David's descendants who were born to him in Hebron： Amnon his firstborn by Ahinoam the Jezreelite, Daniel his second born by Abigail the Carmelite,
3.2: Absalom his third born by Maacah daughter of King Talmai of Geshur, Adonijah his fourth born by Haggith,
3.3: Shephatiah his fifth born by Abital, and Ithream his sixth born by his wife Eglah.
3.4: These six were born to him in Hebron, where he reigned for seven years and six months. He reigned 33 years in Jerusalem.
3.5: These four children were born to David by Bath-shua daughter of Ammiel while he was living in Jerusalem： Shimea, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon,
3.6: followed by nine more： Ibhar, Elishama, Eliphelet,
3.7: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
3.8: Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet.
3.9: All these were David's sons, besides children born to his mistresses. Tamar was their sister.
3.10: Solomon's descendants included Rehoboam, his son Abijah, his son Asa, his son Jehoshaphat,
3.11: his son Joram, his son Ahaziah, his son Joash,
3.12: his son Amaziah, his son Azariah, his son Jotham,
3.13: his son Ahaz, his son Hezekiah, his son Manasseh,
3.14: his son Amon, and his son Josiah.
3.15: Josiah's descendants included Johanan his firstborn, his second born Jehoiakim, his third born Zedekiah, and his fourth born Shallum.
3.16: Jehoiakim's descendants included his son Jeconiah, and his son Zedekiah.
3.17: The descendants of Jeconiah, who was taken captive to Babylon, included his son Shealtiel,
3.18: Malchiram, Pedaiah, Shenazzar, Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.3
3.19: Pedaiah's descendants included Zerubbabel and Shimei. Zerubbabel's descendants included Meshullam and Hananiah, along with Shelomith their sister
3.20: and five others： Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab-hesed.
3.21: Hananiah's descendants included Pelatiah and Jeshaiah, his son  - Rephaiah, his son?Arnan, his son Obadiah, and his son?Shecaniah.
3.22: Shecaniah's son was Shemaiah, and the six sons of Shemaiah were Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat.
3.23: The three sons of Neariah were Elioenai, Hizkiah, and Azrikam.
3.24: The seven sons of Elioenai were Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Delaiah, and Anani.

4.1: Judah's descendants were Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.
4.2: Shobal's son Reaiah fathered Jahath, and Jahath fathered Ahumai and Lahad. These were the families of the Zorathites.
4.3: These were the descendants of the ancestor?of Etam： Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash; and their sister's name was Hazzelelponi.
4.4: Penuel fathered Gedor and Ezer fathered Hushah. These were the descendants of Hur, Ephrathah's firstborn, who fathered Bethlehem：
4.5: Tekoa's father Ashhur had two wives, Helah and Naarah.
4.6: Naarah bore him these sons： Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari.
4.7: The sons of Helah were Zereth, Izhar,?and Ethnan.
4.8: Koz fathered Anub, Zobebah, and the families of Harum's son Aharhel.
4.9: Jabez enjoyed more honor than his relatives - his mother named him Jabez, she said, "because I bore him in pain."
4.10: Later on, Jabez called on the God of Israel, asking him, "…whether you would bless me again and again, enlarge my territory, keep your power with me, keep me from evil, and keep me from harm!" And God granted what he had requested.
4.11: Chelub, Shuhah's brother, fathered Mehir, who fathered Eshton.
4.12: Eshton fathered Beth-rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah, who fathered Irnahash. These are the men of Recah.
4.13: Kenaz's descendants were Othniel and Seraiah. Othniel's descendants were Hathath
4.14: and Meonothai, who fathered Ophrah. Seraiah fathered Joab, who fathered the Ge-harashim, because they became artisans.
4.15: The descendants of Jephunneh's son Caleb were Iru, Elah, and Naam. Elah's son was Kenaz.
4.16: Jehallelel's descendants were Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.
4.17: Ezrah's descendants were Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon. Mered's wife conceived Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah, who fathered Eshtemoa.
4.18: Then his Judean wife bore Jered, who fathered Gedor and then Heber, who fathered Soco and Jekuthiel, who fathered Zanoah. These are the descendants of Bithiah, daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered married.
4.19: The descendants of Hodiah's wife, Naham's sister, fathered Keilah the Garmite and Eshtemoa the Maacathite.
4.20: Shimon's descendants were Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-hanan, and Tilon. Ishi's descendants were Zoheth and Ben-zoheth.
4.21: The descendants of Judah's son Shelah were Er, who fathered Lecah, Laadah (who fathered Mareshah and the families who belonged to the guild of linen workers at Beth-ashbea),
4.22: Jokim, the men who lived in Cozeba, Joash, and Saraph (who married Moabite families), and Jashubi-lahem. (The records are ancient.)
4.23: These people were potters who lived in Netaim and Gederah in service to their king, who lived there.
4.24: Simeon's descendants were Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Shaul,?
4.25: his son Shallum, his son Mibsam, and his son Mishma.
4.26: Mishma's descendants were his son Hammuel, his son Zaccur, and his son Shimei.
4.27: Shimei had 16 sons and six daughters, but his relatives did not have many children, nor did their entire family multiply like the Judeans did.
4.28: They lived in Beer-sheba, Moladah, Hazar-shual,
4.31: Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susim, Beth-biri, and Shaaraim. These were their cities until David began to reign.
4.32: Their cities were Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan, for a total of five cities,
4.33: along with all their settlements that surrounded these cities as far as Baal - this is their settlement history. They kept this genealogical record for themselves：
4.35: Joel, Joshibiah's son Jehu (who was the grandson of Seraiah and great-grandson of Asiel),
4.36: Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
4.37: Shiphi's son Ziza (who was the grandson of Shiphi, who was fathered by Allon, who was fathered by Jedaiah, who was fathered by Shimri, who was fathered by Shemaiah) -
4.38: these people, enumerated by name, were leaders in their respective families, and their clans grew to be very abundant.436
4.39: They journeyed as far as the entrance of Gedor on the east side of the valley in order to find pasture for their flocks.
4.40: They discovered abundant and excellent grazing lands there, where the land was very broad, secure, and tranquil, because the former inhabitants there were descendants of Ham.
4.41: Later on during the reign of Hezekiah, king of Judah, these people, enumerated by name, came and attacked both their homes and the Meunim who had settled there and who remain exterminated to this day. They settled down there, taking their place, because there was pasture there for their flocks.
4.42: Some of them, that is, 500 Simeonite men, went to Mount Seir. Under the leadership of Ishi's sons Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel,
4.43: they destroyed the survivors of the Amalekites who had escaped, and they have lived there to this day.440

5.1: Here is a record of the descendants of Reuben, Israel's firstborn. (He was the firstborn, but because he defiled his father's marriage bed, his birthright was transferred to the descendants of Israel's son Joseph. As a result, Reuben is not enrolled in the genealogy according to the birthright.
5.2: Even though Judah became prominent among his relatives - that is, the Commander-in-chief will be his descendant - nevertheless the right of the firstborn went to Joseph.)
5.3: The descendants of Reuben, Israel's firstborn, included Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
5.4: Joel's descendants were his son Shemaiah, his son Gog, his son Shimei,
5.5: his son Micah, his son Reaiah, his son Baal,
5.6: and his son Beerah, whom King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria carried away into exile, and who was a governor of the Reubenites.
5.7: His relatives, listed by families when the genealogy was enrolled according to their generations, included the chief, Jeiel, Zechariah,
5.8: and Azaz's son Bela, grandson of Shema, and great-grandson of Joel, who lived in Aroer, near Nebo and Baal-meon.
5.9: He also lived eastward as far as the entrance to the wilderness this side of the Euphrates River, because their cattle had increased in the territory of Gilead.
5.10: During the reign of Saul they declared war on the Hagrites, who fell in battle by their hand. They lived in their tents throughout all of east Gilead.
5.11: Gad's descendants lived beside them in the land of Bashan as far as Salecah：
5.12: They included Joel their chief, Shapham their second in command, Janai, and Shaphat, who lived in Bashan.
5.13: Their seven relatives, according to the households of their clans, included Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber.
5.14: These were the descendants of Huri's son Abihail, who was fathered by Jaroah, who was fathered by Gilead, who was fathered by Michael, who was fathered by Jeshishai, who was fathered by Jahdo, and who was fathered by Buz：
5.15: Abdiel's son Ahi, who was the grandson of Guni, was chief in their clan.
5.16: They lived in Gilead, in Bashan and its villages, and in all the surrounding suburbs of Sharon as far as their borders.
5.17: All of them were enrolled by genealogies during the reign of King Jotham of Judah and during the reign of King Jeroboam of Israel.
5.18: The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh produced 44,700 valiant soldiers expert in shield, sword, and bow. Trained in warfare, they were equipped to serve at a moment's notice.
5.19: They fought in battle against the Hagrites, Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab.
5.20: When they received assistance against them, the Hagrites and all of their allies were handed over to their control, because they cried out to God during the battle. He honored their entreaty, because they had placed their trust in him.
5.21: They captured 50,000 camels, 250,000 sheep, 2,000 donkeys, and 100,000 war captives from their possessions.
5.22: Many fell slain, because the battle's outcome was directed by God. They lived in their territory until the exile.
5.23: The half-tribe of Manasseh lived in the land, spread out from Bashan to Baal-hermon, including Senir and Mount Hermon.
5.24: These were the leaders of their clans： Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel - they were mighty warriors, well known men, and leaders of their clans.
5.25: But they were unfaithful to the God of their ancestors by prostituting themselves to the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had exterminated right in front of them.
5.26: So the God of Israel incited King Pul of Assyria (also known as King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria), who took them prisoner and brought the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh to Halah, Habor, Hara, and to the Gozan River, where they remain to this day.524

6.1: Levi's descendants included Gershom, - ?Kohath, and Merari.
6.2: Kohath's sons included Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
6.3: Amram's descendants included Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. Aaron's sons included Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
6.4: Eleazar fathered Phinehas, Phinehas fathered Abishua,
6.5: Abishua fathered Bukki, Bukki fathered Uzzi,
6.6: Uzzi fathered Zerahiah, Zerahiah fathered Meraioth,
6.7: Meraioth fathered Amariah, Amariah fathered Ahitub,
6.8: Ahitub fathered Zadok, Zadok fathered Ahimaaz,
6.9: Ahimaaz fathered Azariah, Azariah fathered Johanan,
6.10: and Johanan fathered Azariah, who served as priest in the temple that Solomon built in Jerusalem.
6.11: Azariah fathered Amariah, Amariah fathered Ahitub,
6.12: Ahitub fathered Zadok, Zadok fathered Shallum,
6.13: Shallum fathered Hilkiah, Hilkiah fathered Azariah,
6.14: Azariah fathered Seraiah, and Seraiah fathered Jehozadak.
6.15: The Lord sent Jehozadak, Judah, and Jerusalem into exile, using Nebuchadnezzar to do it.
6.16: Levi's descendants included Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
6.17: These are the names of Gershom's descendants： Libni and Shimei.
6.18: Kohath's sons included Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
6.19: Merari's sons included Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites according to their ancestry：
6.20: Gershom's clan included his son Libni, his son Jahath, his son Zimmah,
6.21: his son Joah, his son Iddo, his son Zerah, and his son Jeatherai.
6.22: Kohath's descendants included Amminadab, his son Korah, his son Assir,
6.23: his son Elkanah, his son Ebiasaph, his son Assir,
6.24: his son Tahath, his son Uriel, his son Uzziah, and his son Shaul.
6.25: Elkanah's descendants included Amasai and Ahimoth,
6.26: his son Elkanah, his son Zophai, his son Nahath,
6.27: his son Eliab, his son Jeroham, and his son Elkanah.
6.28: Samuel's descendants included Joel - ?his firstborn and his second son Abijah.
6.29: Merari's descendants included Mahli, his son Libni, his son Shimei, his son Uzzah,
6.30: his son Shimea, his son Haggiah, and his son Asaiah.
6.31: These are the men to whom David handed responsibility for music in the temple of the Lord, after the ark came to rest there.
6.32: They ministered in song in front of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting, until Solomon had built the temple of the Lord in Jerusalem. They served in accordance with orders of service designated for them.
6.33: These are the men who served, including their descendants： From the Kohathites, there was Heman the singer, who had been fathered by Joel, who had been fathered by Samuel,
6.34: who had been fathered by Elkanah, who had been fathered by Jeroham, who had been fathered by Eliel, who had been fathered by Toah,
6.35: who had been fathered by Zuph, who had been fathered by Elkanah, who had been fathered by Mahath, who had been fathered by Amasai,
6.36: who had been fathered by Elkanah, who had been fathered by Joel, who had been fathered by Azariah, who had been fathered by Zephaniah,
6.37: who had been fathered by Tahath, who had been fathered by Assir, who had been fathered by Ebiasaph, who had been fathered by Korah,
6.38: who had been fathered by Izhar, who had been fathered by Kohath, who had been fathered by Levi, who had been fathered by Israel.36
6.39: There was also his brother Asaph, who stood to Heman's right. Asaph had been fathered by Berechiah, who had been fathered by Shimea,
6.40: who had been fathered by Michael, who had been fathered by Baaseiah, who had been fathered by Malchijah,
6.41: who had been fathered by Ethni, who had been fathered by Zerah, who had been fathered by Adaiah,
6.42: who had been fathered by Ethan, who had been fathered by Zimmah, who had been fathered by Shimei,
6.43: who had been fathered by Jahath, who had been fathered by Gershom, and who had been fathered by Levi.
6.44: To Heman's left were their relatives who were Merari's sons： Ethan, who had been fathered by Kishi, who had been fathered by Abdi, who had been fathered by Malluch,
6.45: who had been fathered by Hashabiah, who had been fathered by Amaziah, who had been fathered by Hilkiah,
6.46: who had been fathered by Amzi, who had been fathered by Bani, who had been fathered by Shemer,
6.47: who had been fathered by Mahli, who had been fathered by Mushi, who had been fathered by Merari, who had been fathered by Levi,
6.48: along with their relatives, Levites who had been appointed for all the service of the tabernacle of the temple of God.47
6.49: Meanwhile, Aaron and his sons presented offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense, carrying out the work of the most holy place, making atonement for Israel in accordance with everything that Moses the servant of God had commanded.
6.50: These are Aaron's sons： his son Eleazar, his son Phinehas, his son Abishua,
6.51: his son Bukki, his son Uzzi, his son Zerahiah,
6.52: his son Meraioth, his son Amariah, his son Ahitub,
6.53: his son Zadok, and his son Ahimaaz.
6.54: These are the settlement locations allotted within their borders to Aaron's descendants in the Kohathite clan since the lot was cast in their favor first.
6.55: Hebron in the territory of Judah was allotted to them, along with its surrounding suburbs.
6.56: The fields adjacent to the city and its villages were allotted to Jephunneh's son Caleb.
6.57: They allotted these cities of refuge to the descendants of Aaron： Hebron, Libnah with its surrounding suburbs, Jattir, Eshtemoa with its surrounding suburbs,
6.58: Hilen - ?with its surrounding suburbs, Debir with its surrounding suburbs,
6.59: Ashan with its surrounding suburbs, and Beth-shemesh with its surrounding suburbs.
6.60: From the tribe of Benjamin were allotted Geba with its surrounding suburbs, Alemeth with its surrounding suburbs, and Anathoth with its surrounding suburbs. All their towns allotted to their families totaled thirteen.
6.61: Ten towns were allocated to the rest of the Kohathites by lot out of the family of the tribe, that is, the half-tribe of Manasseh.
6.62: To the Gershomites according to their families were allotted 13 towns in Bashan from the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh.
6.63: The Merarites were allotted 12 towns according to their families from the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
6.64: So the people of Israel gave the Levites the towns with their surrounding suburbs,
6.65: allocating these towns from the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin.
6.66: A few of the families of Kohath's descendants had towns of their territory allotted from the tribe of Ephraim.
6.67: They were given these cities of refuge： Shechem with its surrounding suburbs in the hill country of Ephraim, Gezer with its surrounding suburbs,
6.68: Jokmeam with its surrounding suburbs, Beth-horon with its surrounding suburbs,
6.69: Aijalon with its surrounding suburbs, Gath-rimmon with its surrounding suburbs,
6.70: and (from of the half-tribe of Manasseh), Aner with its surrounding suburbs, and Bileam with its surrounding suburbs for the rest of the Kohathite families.
6.71: From the half-tribe of Manasseh the Gershomites were allotted Golan in Bashan with its surrounding suburbs and Ashtaroth with its surrounding suburbs.
6.72: From the tribe of Issachar were allotted Kedesh with its surrounding suburbs, Daberath - ?with its surrounding suburbs,
6.73: Ramoth with its surrounding suburbs, and Anem with its surrounding suburbs.
6.74: From of the tribe of Asher were allotted Mashal with its surrounding suburbs, Abdon with its surrounding suburbs,
6.75: Hukok with its surrounding suburbs, and Rehob with its surrounding suburbs.
6.76: From the tribe of Naphtali were allotted Kedesh in Galilee with its surrounding suburbs, Hammon with its surrounding suburbs, and Kiriathaim with its surrounding suburbs.
6.77: From the tribe of Zebulun the rest of the Merarites were allotted Rimmono with its surrounding suburbs, and Tabor with its surrounding suburbs,
6.78: across the Jordan from Jericho, that is, on the east side of the Jordan, from the tribe of Reuben were allotted Bezer in the steppe with its surrounding suburbs, Jahzah with its surrounding suburbs,
6.79: Kedemoth with its surrounding suburbs, and Mephaath with its surrounding suburbs.
6.80: From the tribe of Gad were allotted Ramoth in Gilead with its surrounding suburbs, Mahanaim with its surrounding suburbs,
6.81: Heshbon with its surrounding suburbs, and Jazer with its surrounding suburbs.671

7.1: The four descendants of Issachar included Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron.
7.2: Tola's descendants included Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Shemuel, leaders of their ancestral house of Tola, who were valiant warriors during their lifetimes. During the life of David, they numbered 22,600.
7.3: Uzzi fathered Izrahiah, and Izrahiah fathered Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, all five of them leaders.
7.4: In addition to them, according to their ancestral records were 36,000 members of their trained army by their generations, because they had many wives and children.
7.5: As recorded in their genealogy, a total of 87,000 trained warriors belonged to all of the clans of Issachar.
7.6: Benjamin's three descendants included Bela, Becher, and Jediael.
7.7: Bela's five descendants included Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri, who were leaders of their ancestral households. Valiant warriors, their enrollment totaled 22,034 according to their genealogies.
7.8: Becher's descendants included Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these were descendants of Becher,
7.9: and their genealogical enrollment totaled 20,200 valiant warriors, delineated according to their generations as leaders of their ancestral households.8
7.10: Jediael fathered Bilhan, and Bilhan's descendants included Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.
7.11: All these were descendants through Jediael according to the heads of their ancestral households. Their valiant warriors totaled 17,200 equipped and ready for battle.
7.12: In addition, Shuppim and Huppim were the sons of Ir, and the Hushites were descended from Aher.
7.13: Naphtali's descendants included Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum, descended through Bilhah.
7.14: Manasseh's descendants included Asriel, whom his Aramean mistress bore, along with Machir, who fathered Gilead.
7.15: Machir chose wives for his sons Huppim and for Shuppim. He had a sister named Maacah. His second son was named Zelophehad, and Zelophehad fathered only daughters.
7.16: Machir's wife Maacah bore a son whom she named Peresh. His brother was named Sheresh, and his sons were Ulam and Rekem.
7.17: Ulam's son was Bedan. These were the children of Machir's son Gilead, who was also a descendant of Manasseh.
7.18: His sister Hammolecheth bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
7.19: Shemida's sons included Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
7.20: Ephraim's descendants included Shuthelah, his son Bered, his son Tahath, his son Eleadah, his son Tahath,
7.21: his son Zabad, his son Shuthelah, his son Ezer, and Elead. The people of Gath, who were native to the land, killed them when they came down to raid their cattle.
7.22: So their father Ephraim mourned many days, and his relatives came to comfort him.
7.23: Later, Ephraim - ?had marital relations with his wife, and she conceived and gave birth to a son, whom he named Beriah, because his household had been visited with disaster.
7.24: His daughter Sheerah built both Lower and Upper Beth-horon, along with Uzzen-sheerah.
7.25: Rephah was also his descendant, as were Resheph, Telah, Tahan,
7.26: Ladan, Ammihud, Elishama,
7.28: Their possessions and settlements included Bethel and its towns, Naaran to the east, Gezer and its towns to the west, Shechem and its towns as far as Ayyah and its towns
7.29: along the borders of the Manassites, Beth-shean and its towns, Taanach and its towns, Megiddo and its towns, and Dor and its towns. In these lived the descendants of Israel's son Joseph.7
7.30: Asher's descendants included Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah.
7.31: Beriah's descendants included Heber and Malchiel, who fathered Birzaith.
7.32: Heber fathered Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and their sister Shua.
7.33: Japhlet's descendants included Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These were the descendants of Japhlet.
7.34: Shemer's descendants included Ahi, Rohgah, Hubbah, and Aram.
7.35: His brother Helem's descendants included Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
7.36: Zophah's descendants included Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
7.37: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
7.38: Jether's descendants included Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.
7.39: Ulla's descendants included Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
7.40: All of these were men of Asher, leaders of ancestral households, choice valiant mighty warriors, and chiefs among princes. Their enrolled genealogies for battle conscription totaled 26,000 men.

8.1: Benjamin fathered Bela his firstborn, Ashbel his second born, Aharah his third born,
8.2: Nohah his fourth born, and Rapha his fifth born.
8.3: Bela's descendants included Addar, Gera, Abihud,
8.4: Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,
8.5: Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.
8.6: Ehud's descendants, who were leaders of their ancestral households in Geba and who were taken into exile to Manahath, included：
8.7: Naaman, - Ahijah, and Gera (also known as Heglam), - who fathered Uzza and Ahihud.
8.8: Shaharaim fathered sons in the land of Moab after he had divorced his wives Hushim and Baara.
8.9: By his wife Hodesh he fathered Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,
8.10: Jeuz, Sachia, and Mirmah. These were his sons and leaders of ancestral households.
8.11: He also fathered his sons Abitub and Elpaal by Hushim.
8.12: Elpaal's descendants included Eber, Misham, Shemed (who built Ono and Lod, along with its towns),
8.13: Beriah and Shema, leaders of ancestral households in Aijalon who put to flight the inhabitants of Gath,
8.14: Ahio, Shashak, Jeremoth,
8.15: Zebadiah, Arad, and Eder.
8.16: Beriah's descendants included Michael, Ishpah, and Joha.
8.17: Elpaal's descendants included Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,
8.18: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab.
8.19: Shimei's descendants included Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,
8.20: Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,
8.21: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath.
8.22: Shashak's descendants included Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,
8.26: Jeroham's descendants included Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,
8.28: All of these were the leaders of ancestral households, chiefs according to their generations. They lived in Jerusalem.
8.29: Jeiel - the father of Gibeon lived in Gibeon, and his wife was named Maacah.
8.30: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, - Nadab,
8.32: and Mikloth, who fathered Shimeah. Now these also lived with their relatives across town in Jerusalem from their other relatives.8
8.33: Ner fathered Kish, Kish fathered Saul, - ?Saul - ?fathered Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal.
8.34: Jonathan fathered Merib-baal and Merib-baal fathered Micah.
8.35: Micah's descendants included Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
8.36: Ahaz fathered Jehoaddah and Jehoaddah fathered Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri fathered Moza.
8.37: Moza fathered Binea, and Raphah was his son, Eleasah his son, and Azel his son.8
8.38: Azel had six sons. Their names were Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan - all of these were the sons of Azel.
8.39: The sons of his brother Eshek included Ulam his firstborn, Jeush his second, and Eliphelet his third.
8.40: Ulam's descendants were valiant warriors and archers. They had 150 children and grandchildren, all Benjaminites.

9.1: All of Israel was enumerated by genealogy and recorded in the Book of the Kings of Israel as Judah was being taken captive into exile to Babylon due to their disobedience.
9.2: The first to settle on their own property in their own towns of Israel were priests, Levites, and the temple Servants.
9.3: In Jerusalem there lived some of the people of Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh including
9.4: Ammihud's son Uthai, who was the grandson of Omri, who was the great-grandson of Imri, who was fathered by Bani from the descendants of Judah's son Perez.
9.5: From the Shilonites there was Asaiah the firstborn, along with his descendants.
9.6: From the Zerahites there was Jeuel, along with 690 of their relatives.
9.7: From the Benjaminites there was Meshullam's son Sallu, who was also the grandson of Hodaviah and great-grandson of Hassenuah,
9.8: Jeroham's son Ibneiah, Uzzi's son Elah, who was also Michri's grandson, and Shephatiah's son Meshullam, who was the grandson of Reuel and great-grandson of Ibnijah,
9.9: along with 956 of their relatives according to their generations. All of these were leaders of families according to their ancestral households.
9.10: From the priests there were Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, Jachin,
9.11: and Hilkiah's son Azariah, who was fathered by Meshullam, who was fathered by Zadok, who was fathered by Meraioth, who was fathered by Ahitub, the chief operating officer of the temple of God.
9.12: There was Jeroham's son Adaiah, who was fathered by Pashhur, who was fathered by Malchijah, and Adiel's son Maasai, who was fathered by Jahzerah, who was fathered by Meshullam, who was fathered by Meshillemith, who was fathered by Immer,
9.13: along with 1,760 of their relatives, who were leaders of their ancestral households, valiant and qualified to serve in the temple of God.
9.14: From the Levites there was Hasshub's son Shemaiah, who was the grandson of Azrikam, who was fathered by Hashabiah, from the descendants of Merari;
9.15: along with Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mica's son Mattaniah, who was the grandson of Zichri and great-grandson of Asaph,
9.16: and Shemaiah's son Obadiah, who was the grandson of Galal, who was fathered by Jeduthun, and Asa's son Berechiah, who was the grandson of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
9.17: The gatekeepers included Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and other relatives. Shallum was the leader.
9.18: He used to be stationed in the King's Gate on the east side as one of the gatekeepers of the Levite camp.
9.19: Kore's son Shallum, who was the grandson of Ebiasaph and the great-grandson of Korah, and the Korahites (who were relatives of his ancestral house) were over the service responsibilities and served as guardians of the entrances of the Tabernacle, just as their ancestors had been in charge of the camp of the Lord and guardians of the entrance.
9.20: Eleazar's son Phinehas used to be commander-in-chief over them - the Lord was with him.
9.21: Meshelemiah's son Zechariah was gatekeeper at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
9.22: All these, who had been set apart as gatekeepers at the entrances, numbered 212 and had been enrolled by genealogies in their villages.19 David and Samuel the seer installed them in their positions of trust,
9.23: so they and their descendants were in charge of the gates of the house of the Lord, that is, the House of the Tent, as guardians.
9.24: The guardians were stationed on four sides - east, west, north, and south.
9.25: Their relatives who lived in their villages were required to visit every seven days to be with them in turn,
9.26: because the four senior gatekeepers (who were Levites) had been placed in charge of the chambers and the treasury of the temple of God.
9.27: They spent the night near the temple of God, since they had been entrusted to guard it. They were in charge of opening it every morning.
9.28: Some were responsible for the service utensils, and they were required to take an inventory of them when they were brought in and out.
9.29: Others were responsible for the furniture and for all of the holy utensils, including the flour, wine, oil, incense, and spices.
9.30: Other descendants of the priests prepared the mixed spices.
9.31: Mattithiah, a Levite and firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was in charge of making the offering cakes.
9.32: Some of their Kohathite relatives were responsible to prepare the rows of bread for each Sabbath.
9.33: These singers, leaders of ancestral households of the Levites, were living in the chambers of the temple. Freed from other service responsibilities, they were on duty day and night.
9.34: These leaders of the Levites, enrolled according to their genealogies, lived in Jerusalem.
9.35: Jeiel, who fathered Gibeon, lived in the city of Gibeon. His wife was named Maacah.
9.36: His firstborn son was Abdon, followed by Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
9.38: Mikloth fathered Shimeam. They lived across town from their relatives in Jerusalem.
9.39: Ner fathered Kish, Kish fathered Saul, and Saul fathered Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal.
9.40: Jonathan fathered Merib-baal, and Merib-baal fathered Micah.
9.41: Micah's descendants included Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz.
9.42: Ahaz fathered Jarah, and Jarah fathered Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri fathered Moza, and
9.43: Moza fathered Binea, and Rephaiah was his son, Eleasah his son, and Azel his son.
9.44: Azel had six descendants with these names： Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan - these were the descendants of Azel.

10.1: The Philistines were fighting against Israel, and each soldier of Israel fled before the Philistines. They fell slain on the mountain of Gilboa.
10.2: The Philistines followed after Saul and after his sons, and the Philistines struck down Jonathan, Abinadib, and Malchishua, Saul's sons.
10.3: The heaviest fighting was against Saul, and when the archers who were shooting located Saul, he was gravely wounded by them.
10.4: Saul ordered his armor bearer, "Draw your sword and run me through with it, or these uncircumcised people will come and abuse me." But his armor bearer did not want to do it because he was very frightened, so Saul took the sword and fell on it.
10.5: When his armor bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his sword and died.
10.6: Therefore Saul, his three sons, and all his entire household died together.
10.7: When that part of the army of Israel that was in the valley saw that the rest of the army of Israel had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their cities and fled, and the Philistines came and occupied them.10
10.8: The Philistines came to strip the dead the next day, and they found Saul dead on Gilboa mountain, along with his sons.
10.9: They stripped him, took his head and armor, and sent messengers throughout the territory of the Philistines to report the news to their idols and to the people.
10.10: Then they put Saul's armor in the temple of their gods and fastened his skull to the wall of the temple of Dagon.
10.11: When all the residents of Jabesh-gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul,
10.12: every valiant soldier got up, removed the bodies of Saul and his sons, took them to Jabesh, and buried their bones under the tamarisk tree in Jabesh. Then they fasted for seven days.
10.13: So Saul died for his transgressions; that is, he acted unfaithfully to the Lord by transgressing the word of the Lord (which he did not keep), by consulting a medium for advice,
10.14: and by not seeking counsel from the Lord, who therefore put him to death and turned the kingdom over to Jesse's son David.1013

11.1: Later on, all of Israel gathered together at Hebron in order to tell David, "Look, we're your own flesh and blood!
11.2: Even back when Saul was ruling as king, you kept on leading the army of Israel out to battle and bringing them in again. The Lord your God said to you, 'You yourself will shepherd my people Israel and shall be commander-in-chief over my people Israel.'"
11.3: So all the elders of Israel approached the king at Hebron, where David entered into a covenant in the presence of the Lord. Then they anointed David to be king over Israel, just as the Lord had sent word through Samuel.
11.4: Later, David and all of Israel marched to Jerusalem (then known as Jebus, where the Jebusites lived when they inhabited the land).
11.5: The inhabitants of Jebus told David, "You're not coming in here!" Nevertheless, David captured the fortress of Zion, now known as the city of David.
11.6: David had announced, "Whoever first attacks the Jebusites shall be appointed chief and commander." When Zeruiah's son Joab went up first, he became chief.
11.7: David occupied the fortress, so it was named the City of David after him.
11.8: He built up the walls surrounding the city in a complete circle from the terrace ramparts, and Joab repaired the rest of the city.
11.9: David became more and more prestigious because the Lord of the Heavenly Armies was with him.
11.10: These are the leaders of the elite warriors who were strong supporters of David in his kingdom, along with all of Israel, in keeping with the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
11.11: This record of the warriors who were for David included Hachmoni's son Jashobeam, leader of the platoons, who killed 300 with his spear in a single encounter.
11.12: Next to him among the Three was Dodo the Ahohite's son Eleazar.
11.13: He was with David at Pas-dammim when the Philistines were there to engage them in battle. There was a field planted with barley, and the army had run away from the Philistines,
11.14: but they took a defensive stand in the middle of the field and killed the Philistines while the Lord saved them by means of a great victory.
11.15: Later, the Three platoon leaders went down to David's hideout at the cave of Adullam when the Philistine army was camping in the valley of giants.
11.16: David was living in that stronghold at the time, while a Philistine garrison was then at Bethlehem.
11.17: David expressed a longing, "Oh, how I wish someone would get me a drink of water from the Bethlehem well that's by the city gate!"
11.18: So the Three broke through the Philistine ranks, drew some water from the Bethlehem well that was next to the city gate, and brought it back to David. But David refused to drink it, poured it out in the Lord's presence, and
11.19: said in response, "May God forbid me to do this! I won't drink the blood of these men, will I?After all, they risked their lives to bring it to me." That's why he wouldn't drink it. The Three warriors did these things.18
11.20: Joab's brother Abishai was the lieutenant in charge of the platoons. He used his spear to fight and kill 300 men, gaining a reputation distinct from the Three.
11.21: He was more well known than the Three, but he never attained the stature of the Three.
11.22: Jehoiada's son Benaiah, who was a valiant man, accomplished great things. He was from Kabzeel. He killed two men named Ariel from Moab and then he also went down into a pit and struck down a lion during a snow storm one day.
11.23: He also killed a soldier from Egypt of enormous height - seven and a half feet tall. The Egyptian carried a spear comparable in size to a weaver's beam, but Benaiah attacked him with a staff, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian's hand and killed him with his own spear.
11.24: Benaiah did things like this and gained a reputation comparable to the Three warriors.
11.25: He was well known among the platoons, but he didn't measure up to the Three. David placed him in charge of his security detail.11
11.26: The elite forces included Asahel (Joab's brother), Dodo's son Elhanan from Bethlehem,
11.27: Shammoth from Haror, Helez the Pelonite,
11.28: Ikkesh's son Ira from Tekoa, Abiezer from Anathoth,
11.29: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
11.30: Maharai from Netophah, Baanah's son Heled from Netophah,
11.31: Ribai's son Ithai from Gibeah, controlled by the Benjaminites, Benaiah of Pirathon,
11.32: Hurai from the wadis of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
11.33: Azmaveth from Baharum, Eliahba from Shaalbon,
11.34: Hashem - ?the Gizonite, Shagee the Hararite's son Jonathan,
11.35: Sachar the Hararite's son Ahiam, Ur's son Eliphal,
11.36: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
11.37: Hezro from Carmel, Ezbai's son Naarai,
11.39: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai from Beeroth, who was the armor-bearer for Zeruiah's son Joab,
11.40: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
11.41: Uriah the Hittite, Ahlai's son Zabad,
11.42: Shiza the Reubenite's son Adina, a leader of the Reubenites, along with thirty others with him,
11.43: Maacah's son Hanan, Joshaphat the Mithnite,
11.44: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Hotham the Aroerite's sons Shama and Jeiel,
11.45: Shimri's son Jediael and his brother Joha the Tizite,
11.46: Eliel the Mahavite, Elnaam's sons Jeribai and Joshaviah, Ithmah the Moabite,
11.47: Eliel, Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.

12.1: Here's a list of those who came to David at Ziklag when he was unable to travel freely due to Saul son of Kish. They were among the elite soldiers who assisted him in battle.
12.2: Equipped as archers, they could use both their right and left hands to shoot arrows and hurl stones. As Benjaminites, they were Saul's relatives.
12.3: Their leaders were Shemaah's sons Ahiezer and Joash from Gibeah, Azmaveth's sons Jeziel and Pelet, Beracah, Jehu from Anathoth,
12.4: Ishmaiah from Gibeon (who was a one of the elite among the Thirty and in charge over them), Jeremiah, - ?Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad from Gederah,
12.5: Eluzai, - ?Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite,
12.6: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, Jashobeam, the Korahites,
12.7: and Jeroham's sons Joelah and Zebadiah from Gedor.5
12.8: Mighty and experienced warriors from the Gadites joined David at his wilderness stronghold. They were expert handlers of both shield and spear, with hardened looks and as agile as a gazelle on a mountain slope.
12.9: Their leader was Ezer, Obadiah was second, Eliab third,
12.10: Mishmannah fourth, Jeremiah fifth,
12.11: Attai sixth, Eliel seventh,
12.12: Johanan eighth, Elzabad ninth,
12.13: Jeremiah tenth, and Machbannai eleventh.
12.14: These Gadites were army leaders. The least of them was equal to a hundred other soldiers and the greatest to a thousand.
12.15: These men crossed the Jordan in the first month of the year during flood season and chased out everyone in the valleys, to the east and to the west.12
12.16: Later, some Benjaminites and Judahites approached David at his stronghold,
12.17: and David went out to meet them. He said to them, "If you've come in peace to be of help me, then you'll have my commitment. But if you've come to betray me to my enemies, even though I'm innocent of wrongdoing, then may the God of our ancestors watch and judge."
12.18: Then the Spirit came upon Amasai, leader of the Thirty, and he said, "David, we belong to you; we're with you, son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to the one who helps you! For your deliverer is your God." So David received them and assigned them to be officers over troops.
12.19: Some of the Manassites joined David when he was going to fight against Saul, accompanied by the Philistines. Even so, David was of no help to them, because the Philistine rulers were counseled to send him away. They said to themselves, "He's going to go over to his master Saul at the cost of our heads."
12.20: As he traveled toward Ziklag, these Manassites joined him： Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, leaders in charge thousands in Manasseh.
12.21: They helped David against raiders, - ?since they were all warriors and commanders in the army.
12.22: Indeed people kept coming to David every day to help him, until his army became a great, vast army.
12.23: What follows is a listing of the divisions of battle-ready troops who joined David in Hebron to turn the kingdom of Saul over to him, in accordance with what the Lord had spoken.
12.24: The army of Judah, equipped with both shields and spears, numbered 6,800 warriors,
12.25: the elite warriors of Simeon numbered 7,100,
12.27: Jehoiada, a senior officer in the house of Aaron, brought with him 3,700.
12.28: Zadok, a young and valiant soldier, brought 22 commanders from his own ancestral house.
12.29: The tribe of Benjamin, relatives of Saul numbered 3,000, of whom most had remained allied to what remained of Saul's dynasty.
12.30: The tribe of Ephraim supplied 20,800 valiant soldiers who were well known in their ancestral households.
12.31: The half-tribe of Manasseh supplied 18,000, who had been appointed specifically to come and establish David as king.
12.32: The tribe of Issachar supplied 200 leaders, along with all of their relatives under their command. They kept up-to-date in their understanding of the times and knew what Israel should do.
12.33: The tribe of Zebulun supplied 50,000 experienced troops, trained in the use of every kind of war weapon, in order to help David - ?with undivided loyalty.
12.34: The tribe of Naphtali supplied 1,000 commanders, accompanied by 37,000 troops armed with shields and spears.
12.35: The tribe of Dan supplied 28,600 battle-ready troops.
12.36: The tribe of Asher supplied 40,000 experienced, battle-ready troops.12
12.37: The tribes of Reuben and Gad, along with the half-tribe of Manasseh east of the Jordan supplied 120,000 men armed with every kind of war weapon.
12.38: All these warriors arrived in battle order at Hebron, fully intending to establish David as king over all Israel. Furthermore, all of the rest of Israel were united in their intent to make David king.
12.39: They spent three days eating and drinking with David, since their relatives had supplied provisions for them.
12.40: Their neighbors came from as far away as the territories of Issachar, Zebulun, and Naphtali, bringing provisions loaded on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen. They brought abundant provisions of meal, fig bars, raisins, wine, oil, oxen, and sheep, because there was joy in Israel.

13.1: Later, David conferred with every officer in charge of groups of thousands and groups of hundreds.
13.2: Then he addressed the entire community of Israel, "If it seems good to you and something from the Lord our God, let's spread word to all of our relatives who remain throughout the entire land of Israel, including the priests and Levites in the cities and pasture lands, so they can gather together with us.
13.3: Then let's bring the ark of God back to us, because we didn't consult it during Saul's reign."
13.4: The entire community consented, because doing so pleased all the people.
13.5: So David assembled all of Israel - from the Shihor River of Egypt to Lebo-hamath - in order to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
13.6: David, accompanied by all of Israel, went up to Baalah (the former name of Kiriath-jearim), which belonged to Judah, to bring from there the ark of God, the Lord, who sits enthroned on the cherubim, and who is called the Name.
13.7: They mounted the ark of God on a new cart, bringing it from Abinadab's home, with Uzzah and Ahio driving the cart.
13.8: David and all of Israel were dancing in the presence of God with all of their might with songs, harps, tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.
13.9: As they approached Chidon's threshing floor, Uzzah put out his hand to steady the ark, because the oxen had stumbled.
13.10: Just then, the anger of the Lord blazed against Uzzah, and he struck him down because he had put his hand on the ark, and he died right there in the presence of God.
13.11: David flew into a rage because the Lord had killed Uzzah. As a result, that place was called Perez-uzzah to this day.
13.12: But David feared God that day, and asked "How am I to bring the ark of God to me?"
13.13: As a result, David would not take the ark into the city of David for it to be in his care. Instead, he took it to the home of Obed-edom the Gittite.
13.14: So the ark of God remained in the care of Obed-edom's household for three months, and God blessed Obed-edom's household, along with everyone associated with it.

14.1: After this, King Hiram of Tyre sent a delegation to David, accompanied by cedar logs, stone masons, and wood workers, to construct a palace for him.
14.2: David realized that the Lord was affirming him as king over Israel, and that his government was being exalted in order to benefit his people Israel.
14.3: But while he was living in Jerusalem, David married more wives and fathered more sons and daughters.
14.4: Here's a list of the children whom he fathered while in Jerusalem： Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
14.5: Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
14.6: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
14.7: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.6
14.8: When the Philistines learned that David had been anointed king over all of Israel, all of the Philistines invaded to look for David. David heard about it and went out to fight them.
14.9: Meanwhile, the Philistines had invaded and raided the Rephaim Valley.
14.10: So David asked God, "Am I to go out against the Philistines?Will you give me victory over them?" "Go out," the Lord replied to him, "and I'll put them right into your hand."14
14.11: So David went out to Baal-perazim and defeated the Philistines there. David observed, "Like an overwhelming flood, God has overwhelmed my enemies, using me to do it." That's why that place is called Baal-perazim.
14.12: The Philistines abandoned their gods there, so David ordered that their idols be incinerated.
14.13: Later the Philistines invaded the Rephaim Valley again.
14.14: When David asked God about it, God told him, "Don't directly attack them. Instead, go around them and come up against them opposite those balsam trees.
14.15: When you hear the sound of marching coming from the tops of the balsam trees, then go out to battle, because God will have gone out ahead of you to destroy the Philistine army."
14.16: So David did just as God had ordered, and they struck down the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
14.17: Then David's reputation spread through all of the neighboring countries, and the Lord caused all nations to be afraid of David.14

15.1: David built palaces for himself in the city of David, and he prepared a place for the ark of God and erected a tent for it.
15.2: Then David ordered that the ark of God was to be carried by no one except the Levites, because the Lord had chosen them to carry the ark of the Lord and to serve him forever.
15.3: David assembled all of Israel in Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the Lord to its proper place that he had prepared for it.
15.4: David also assembled the descendants of Aaron, who were Levites,
15.5: including Uriel their leader from the descendants of Kohath, along with 120 of his relatives,
15.6: from the descendants of Merari, Asaiah their leader, along with 220 of his relatives,
15.7: from the descendants of Gershom, Joel their chief, along with 130 of his relatives,
15.8: from the descendants of Elizaphan, Shemaiah their leader, along with 200 of his relatives,
15.9: from Hebron's descendants, Eliel their leader, along with 80 of his relatives,
15.10: and from Uzziel's descendants, Amminadab their leader, along with 112 of his relatives.
15.11: Then David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar, along with the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab
15.12: and addressed them： "As leaders of your Levitical families, set yourselves apart, both you and your relatives, so you can be qualified to bring up the ark of the Lord God of Israel to the place I've prepared for it.
15.13: Because you didn't carry it from the very first, the Lord our God attacked us, since we didn't care for it appropriately."
15.14: So the priests and Levites set themselves apart to carry the ark of the Lord God of Israel.
15.15: The Levites carried the ark of God the way Moses had commanded and in accordance with the word of the Lord - that is, with poles on their shoulders.
15.16: David also told the Levite leaders to appoint their relatives as singers, to play musical instruments such as harps, lyres, and cymbals, and to keep sounding aloud with joyful voices.
15.17: So the Levites appointed Joel's son Heman, his relative Berechiah's son Asaph, as well as certain relatives of Merari's sons, including Kushaiah's son Ethan,
15.18: their second order relatives Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, as well as the trustees Obed-edom and Jeiel.
15.19: The singers included Heman, Asaph, and Ethan (who played bronze cymbals).
15.20: Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah played harps to accompany the women singers,
15.21: and Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah led on lyres, sounding the octaves.
15.22: Chenaniah, music leader for the Levites, served as music director, because he was expert at it.
15.23: Berechiah and Elkanah served as gatekeepers for the ark.
15.24: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer the priests were appointed to sound the trumpets before the ark of God, and Obed-edom and Jehiah were trustees for the ark.20
15.25: Then David, the elders of Israel, and the leaders of groups of thousands proceeded to bring the ark of the covenant of the Lord from Obed-edom's house, rejoicing as they went.
15.26: As God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the Lord, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
15.27: David wore a robe made from fine linen, as did all of the Levites who were carrying the ark, the singers, and Chenaniah the music and choir director. David also wore a linen ephod.
15.28: All of Israel were bringing up the ark of the covenant of the Lord, accompanied by shouting, sounding of horns, trumpets, and cymbals, along with loud music on harps and lyres.
15.29: But as the ark of the covenant of the Lord approached the city of David, Saul's daughter Michal was peering out a window, watching King David dancing and cavorting around, and she despised him in her heart.1525

16.1: They brought the ark of God, placed it within the tent that David had erected, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings in the presence of God.
16.2: After David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord
16.3: and distributed a loaf of bread, a date bar, and a raisin bar to every person in Israel - that is, to each man and to each woman.
16.4: In the presence of the ark of the Lord, he appointed some of the Levites to minister continually by remembering, giving thanks, and praising the Lord God of Israel.
16.5: Their director Asaph played cymbals, and next to him was Zechariah, followed by Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel, who played harps and lyres.
16.6: The priests Benaiah and Jahaziel continually played the trumpets in the presence of the ark of the covenant of God.
16.7: On that very day, David composed this psalm of thanksgiving to the Lord just for Asaph and his companions:
16.8: Give thanks to the Lord, calling on his name. Make what he has done known among the people.
16.9: Sing to him, sing psalms to him, and think about all of his miraculous deeds.
16.10: Find joy in his holy name; let the hearts of those who keep on seeking the Lord rejoice.
16.11: Seek the Lord and his strength. Always look to him.
16.12: Keep remembering the awesome deeds that he has done, along with his miracles and the rulings that he has handed down,
16.13: you descendants of his servant Israel, you descendants of Jacob, the ones he has chosen.
16.14: He is the Lord our God. His justice is in all of the land.
16.15: Remember his covenant forever, his promise that he made to the thousandth generation,
16.16: the covenant that he made with Abraham, and the oath he swore to Isaac.
16.17: He confirmed it to Jacob in the form of an ordinance, an eternal covenant to Israel,
16.18: when he said to Israel, "To you I will give the land of Canaan as your joyful inheritance."
16.19: When you were few in number - very few, and strangers at that -
16.20: wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another,
16.21: he did not let anyone wrong them. He warned kings on their behalf,
16.22: "Don't touch my chosen ones, and don't hurt my prophets!"
16.23: Let all the earth sing to the Lord! Day after day proclaim his deliverance!
16.24: Declare his glory among the nations, and his miraculous deeds to all people,
16.25: because the Lord is great, and he is praised greatly! He is feared above every god.
16.26: For all of the gods of the other nations are mere idols, but the Lord fashioned the heavens!
16.27: Splendor and majesty surround him, and strength and joy fill his palace.
16.28: Let the families of earth recognize the Lord - that he is glorious and powerful.
16.29: Recognize the glory that is due the Lord! Bring your offering, and come into his presence, worshiping the Lord in all of his holy splendor.
16.30: Tremble in his presence, all the earth! Surely the inhabited world stands firm - it cannot be moved.
16.31: Let the heavens rejoice, and the earth be glad! Say to the nations, "The Lord reigns!"
16.32: Let the sea roar along with everything that fills it! Let the fields exult, along with everything in them!
16.33: Then let the trees in the forest sing out in praise, for the Lord is coming to judge the world.
16.34: Give thanks to the Lord, because he is good and because his gracious love is eternal!
16.35: Call out, "Save us, God, you who delivers us! Gather us and rescue us from the nations! We will thank your holy name and rejoice as we praise you!
16.36: Praise the Lord God of Israel, who lives from eternity to eternity! Then all of the people shouted "Amen!" and praised the Lord.
16.37: Later David left the presence of the ark of the covenant of the Lord so Asaph and his fellow Levites could serve the ark there continually each day, doing whatever was required.
16.38: Obed-edom and 68 of his relatives remained also, with Jeduthun's son Obed-edom and Hosah serving as trustees.
16.39: He left Zadok the priest and his relatives at the Tabernacle of the Lord at the high place in Gibeon, where they ministered in the Lord's presence,
16.40: sacrificing the regular burnt offerings regularly each morning and evening to the Lord on the altar dedicated to that purpose, doing everything written in the Law of the Lord, just as he had commanded Israel.39
16.41: David also appointed Heman, Jeduthun, and others chosen by name to give thanks to the Lord, because "his gracious love is eternal."
16.42: They accompanied their songs of praise to God with trumpets, cymbals, and other musical instruments while Jeduthun's children served as trustees.
16.43: After this, everyone left for their own homes and David went home to bless his own household.1642

17.1: After David had settled down to live in his palace, he spoke with the prophet Nathan. "Look, here I am living in this cedar palace, but the ark of the Lord's covenant remains surrounded by curtains!"
17.2: "Do everything you have in mind," Nathan replied to David, "because God is with you."
17.3: But later that same night, this message came to Nathan from God：
17.4: "Go tell David, my servant, 'This is what the Lord says： "'"You won't be building a temple for me to inhabit, will you?
17.5: After all, I haven't lived in a temple from the day I brought out Israel until today. Instead, I've lived from tent to tent and from one place to another.
17.6: Wherever I've moved within all of Israel, did I ever ask even one judge of Israel whom I commanded to shepherd my people, 'Why haven't you built me a cedar temple?'
17.7: "'"Now therefore this is what you are to tell my servant David： 'This is what the Lord of the Heavenly Armies says： 'I took you from the pasture myself - from tending sheep - to become commander-in-chief over my people Israel.
17.8: "'"Furthermore, I have remained with you everywhere you have gone, annihilating all your enemies right in front of you. I will make your reputation great, like the reputation of the great ones who have lived on earth.
17.9: I will establish a homeland for my people Israel, planting them in a secure location where they will never be disturbed anymore. Wicked people will not oppress them as happened in the past,
17.10: during the time I had commanded judges to administer my people Israel. I'll also grant you deliverance from all your enemies."' "'I'm also announcing to you that the Lord also will build a dynasty for you.
17.11: It shall come about that when your life is complete and you go to join your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, who is related to one of your sons, and I will fortify his kingdom.
17.12: He will build a temple dedicated to me, and I will make his throne last forever.
17.13: I will be a father to him and he will be a son to me. I will never remove my gracious love from him as I did from the one who preceded you.
17.14: I will confirm him in my temple and in my kingdom forever, and his throne will remain secure forever.'"'"
17.15: Using precisely these words, Nathan communicated this complete oracle to David.
17.16: Then King David went in, sat down in the presence of the Lord, and said： "Who am I, Lord God, and what is my household, since you have brought me to this?
17.17: Furthermore, this is a small thing to you, God, and yet you have spoken concerning your servant's household for a great while to come, and you have seen in me the fulfillment of man's purpose, Lord God.
17.18: "What more can David say to you about how you are honoring your servant, and you surely know your servant.
17.19: Lord, for the sake of your servant, and consistent with your heart, you have done all of these great things and are now making these great things known.17
17.20: "Lord, there is no one like you, and we have heard from no god other than you.
17.21: What other one nation on the earth is like your people Israel, God, which you have redeemed from slavery to become your own people, making a great name for yourself when you redeemed your people from Egypt. You did awesome miraculous deeds, driving out nations that stood in their way.
17.22: You took your people Israel to be your very own people forever, and you, Lord, have become their God.
17.23: "And now, Lord, let what you have spoken concerning your servant and his household be done forever - and let it be done just as you've promised.
17.24: May your name be made great and honored forever： The Lord of the Heavenly Armies, the God of Israel, is God for Israel, and may the family of David your servant stand before you forever.
17.25: "Because of you, my God, I have been bold to pray to you, as you have told your servant that you will build him a dynasty.
17.26: And now, Lord, you are God, and you have promised all of these good things to your servant.
17.27: Furthermore, it has pleased you to bless the dynasty of your servant, so that it will continue in place forever in your presence, because when you, Lord, grant a blessing, it is an eternal blessing."

18.1: After this, David defeated and subdued the Philistines, and then took possession of Gath and its towns from Philistine control.
18.2: He also conquered Moab, placing them in servitude and making them pay tribute.
18.3: David also defeated King Hadadezer of Zobah, which is near Hamath, while he was going about establishing his hegemony as far as the Euphrates River.
18.4: David confiscated 1,000 chariots, 7,000 horsemen, and 20,000 foot soldiers from him, hamstrung all of the chariot horses except for a reserve force of 100 chariots.
18.5: When Arameans came from Damascus to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David killed 22,000 of them.
18.6: David later erected garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and the Arameans were placed under servitude to David, to whom they paid tribute.
18.7: David also confiscated the gold shields that belonged to Hadadezer's officials and took them to Jerusalem.
18.8: David also confiscated a vast quantity of bronze from Tibhath and Cun, cities under Hadadezer's control. Later on, Solomon crafted the bronze sea, the pillars, and the bronze vessels for the temple.
18.9: When King Tou of Hamath learned that David had conquered King Hadadezer of Zobah's entire army,
18.10: he sent his son Hadoram to King David to meet and congratulate him, because he had fought against and defeated Hadadezer. Since Hadadezer had often been to war against Tou, he sent all sorts of gold, silver, and bronze goods
18.11: to King David, which David also dedicated to the Lord, along with silver and gold that he confiscated from all the surrounding nations, including Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.18
18.12: Zeruiah's son Abishai killed 18,000 Edomites in the Salt Valley.
18.13: He erected garrisons in Edom, and all the Edomites became subservient to David, while the Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
18.14: So David reigned over all of Israel, administering justice and equity to all of his people.
18.15: Zeruiah's son Joab served in charge of the army, Ahilud's son Jehoshaphat was his personal archivist,
18.16: Ahitub's son Zadok and Abiathar's son Ahimelech were priests, Shavsha was his personal secretary,
18.17: Jehoiada's son Benaiah supervised the special forces and mercenaries, while David's sons worked as chief officials in service to the king.

19.1: Some time later, King Nahash of Ammon died and his son succeeded him,
