21.2: "Son of Man, look toward Jerusalem, preach against its sanctuaries, and prophesy against Israel's land.
21.3: Declare to Israel's land, 'This is what the Lord says： "Watch out! I'm against you! I'm going to unsheathe my sword to kill both the righteous and the wicked among you.
21.4: Since I'm going to kill both the righteous and the wicked among you, I'll be unsheathing my sword against everyone from south to north.
21.5: Then everyone will know that I am the Lord, who unsheathed my sword, and who won't have to unsheathe it again."'
21.6: "And now, Son of Man, you are to start groaning until you're sick to your stomach. You are to groan bitterly right in front of them.
21.7: When they'll ask you, 'Why are you groaning? you are to say, 'Because of the news that just arrived. Every heart will melt with fear, every hand will grow limp, every spirit will grow faint, and every knee will glisten with sweat.' Look! It has come and it will be fulfilled," declares the Lord God.
21.8: This message came to me from the Lord：
21.9: "Son of Man, prophesy and say, 'This is what the Lord God says： 'A sword! A sword is being sharpened. It's also being polished.
21.10: It's being sharpened for slaughter, and being polished to gleam like lightning.' "We shouldn't be rejoicing, should we, while my Son's scepter, the sword, is despising every tree -
21.11: "It's intended to be polished so it can be grasped in the hand. The sword is sharpened. It's polished for placement into the hand of the executioner.
21.12: "Cry and wail, you Son of Man! It's headed against my people. It's also against all the princes of Israel, who have been tossed to the sword, along with my people. So it's time to grieve like you mean it,
21.13: because testing is sure to come. "In putting the sword to the test along with the scepter, it won't keep on rejecting, will it? declares the Lord God."
21.14: "Now, Son of Man, prophesy as you strike your hands together. Let the sword that fatally wounds be doubled and tripled. That great, slaughtering sword closes in on them. I've set in place a slaughtering sword at the entrance to all their gates,
21.15: so that their hearts melt and the number of those who stumble increase. I've set in place a slaughtering sword at the entrance to all their gates. Oh, no! It's made like lightning. It's drawn to slaughter. Be sharp! Attack on the right, or parry to your left, wherever you point your sword.
21.17: I will also clap my hands; then my anger will subside. I, the Lord have spoken it."
21.18: This message came to me from the Lord：
21.19: "Now, Son of Man, demarcate two pathways by which the sword of Babylon's king will arrive in the land. Both pathways will lead from a single land. "Make a sign, carving it out and installing it at the junction on the way to the city.
21.20: Set it to point one way for bringing the sword against Rabbah, the descendants of Ammon, and the other way against Judah and fortified Jerusalem.
21.21: "Meanwhile, Babylon's king is standing at the fork of the road, where he can head in either of two directions, and that's where he is practicing divination. Shaking his arrows, he's asking questions of his teraphim while he examines livers.
21.22: On his right hand he is divining against Jerusalem, preparing to set up battering rams, preparing for the slaughter, getting ready to sound the alarm for battle, setting the battering rams in place at the gates, building siege mounds, and erecting a siege wall.
21.23: Iin their view it will seem to be a false prophecy, but because they swore allegiance, he'll make them remember their guilt as he takes them captive."
21.24: "Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'Because you brought your sins to remembrance when your transgressions were uncovered, your sins are visibly evident in everything you've done. And since you brought them to remembrance, you'll be taken captive.'
21.25: "So now, you dishonored and wicked prince of Israel, whose day has come in this time of final punishment,
21.26: This is what the Lord God says： 'Remove your turban! Take off your crown! Things aren't going to remain as they used to be. What is lowly will be exalted, and what is lofty will be brought low.
21.27: A ruin! A ruin! I'm bringing about ruin!' But this also will not happen until he who has authority over it arrives, because I'll give it to him."
21.28: And now Son of Man, prophesy to the Ammonites that this is what the Lord God says to the Ammonites cabout their approaching humiliation： "A sword! A sword is being drawn for slaughter. It's polished to flash like lightning.
21.29: When they see empty visions about you while they're divining lies for you, to lay you on the necks of the wicked, who are fatally wounded, whose days have come, their time for punishment.
21.30: Return it to its scabbard. "At the place where you were formed, in the land of your origin, there is where I'll judge you.
21.31: I'm going to pour out my indignation all over you. I'll blow my fierce wrath against you and deliver you into the control of brutal men who are skilled at destruction.
21.32: You'll be fuel for the conflagration. Your blood will be spilled throughout the land. You won't be remembered anymore, now that I, the Lord, have spoken."

22.1: This message came to me from the Lord：
22.2: "Now, Son of Man, will you truly judge that blood-stained city?Then make her aware of all of her detestable practices.
22.3: "You are to say, 'This is what the Lord God says： "The city keeps on shedding blood within her, bringing about her time of judgment. She crafts idols that defile her.
22.4: "You're guilty because of the blood that you've shed. You were defiled by the idols that you've made. You've caused your time of judgment to draw near and have even come to the end of your years. Therefore, I've made you an object of derision among the nations and to other lands.
22.5: Those who are both near and far away from you will scoff at you, because your reputation will be infamous and full of turmoil.
22.6: "Watch out! Each of the princes of Israel has misused his authority to shed blood.
22.7: They've treated mothers and fathers among you with contempt. They've oppressed the foreigner among you. They've maltreated the orphan and the widow among you.
22.8: "You have despised my sacred things and profaned my Sabbaths.
22.9: Slanderous men live among you, intent on shedding blood. They've eaten at the toop of mountain shrines. They've crafted plans to do evil things among you.
22.10: They've revealed the nakedness of their father in your midst. They've humiliated those among you who were unclean due to her impurity.
22.11: One of you commits detestable practices with his neighbor's wife. Another sexually defiles his daughter-in-law. Another humiliates his sister, his own father's daughter.
22.12: They take bribes among you to shed blood. You've taken usury and exacted interest. You've gained control over your neighbor through extortion. And you've forgotten me," declares the Lord God.
22.13: "Watch out! I'm vehemently angry about the unjust gains that you've earned, and about the blood that has been shed among you.
22.14: Can your heart stand up to this?Can your hands remain strong when I deal with you?I, the Lord, have spoken and will fulfill this.
22.15: I'm going to disperse you among the nations and scatter you to other lands. I'm going to put an end to your uncleanness.
22.16: When you've been defiled in the sight of the nations, then you'll know that I am the Lord."
22.17: This message came to me from the Lord：
22.18: "Son of Man, Israel has become like dross to me. All of them are like remnants of bronze, tin, iron, and lead in a furnace-the dross left over from smelting silver.
22.19: Therefore this is what the Lord God says, 'Because all of you have become dross, watch out! I'm going to gather all of you at the center of Jerusalem,
22.20: just like a smelter gathers all the silver, bronze, lead, and tin to the center of a furnace and inects fire in order to melt it, I'm going to gather you in my anger and rage, make you settle down-and then I'm going to melt you down.
22.21: Indeed, I'm going to gather you together and exale the fire of my fury, and then you'll be melted from the inside out
22.22: like melting silver at the center of a furnace. When you've been melted from the center out, then you'll know that I am the Lord. I'll pour out my anger on you.'"
22.23: This message came to me from the Lord;
22.24: "Son of Man, tell her, 'You're a land that hasn't been purified, one that hasn't been rained on in the day of indignation.
22.25: There's a conspiracy of prophets within her, and like a roaring lion tearing its prey, they've devoured people, and confiscated treasures, and taken precious things. They've added to the population of widows within her.
22.26: Her priests have violated my law and profaned my sacred things. They didn't differentiate between what's sacred and what's common. They didn't instruct others to discern clean from unclean things. They hid refused to keep my Sabbaths. "'I'm constantly being profaned among them.
22.27: Her princes within her are like wolves tearing their prey apart. They shed blood, destroying souls, and make unjust gain.
22.28: "'Her prophets whitewashed all of this things through false visions and divine lies. They kept on saying, "This is what the Lord God says…", when the Lord has not spoken.
22.29: The people of the land were vigorously oppressive and took possession of plunder by violence. They've afflicted the poor and the needy and unjustly treated the foreigner.
22.30: I sought for a man among them to build the wall and stand in the breach in my presence on behalf of the land so that it won't be destroyed, but I found no one,
22.31: so I poured my indignation over them. With my fierce anger, I've consumed them. I brought the consequences of their behavior upon them,' declares the Lord God."

23.1: This message came to me from the Lord：
23.2: "Son of Man, here are two sisters who are daughters from the same mother.
23.3: They committed sexual immorality in Egypt. They did this in their youth. There, their breasts were caressed. Their virgin breasts were fondled.
23.4: The older one was named Oholah and her sister was named Oholibah. They belonged to me and gave birth to sons and daughters. Now as to their real identities, Oholah refers to Samaria and Oholibah to Jerusalem."
23.5: "Oholah committed sexual immorality while she belonged to me. She lusted for Assyria warriors
23.6: who were clothed in blue - including governors and commanders. All of them were desirable young men - horsemen mounted on horses.
23.7: She bestowed her sexual favors on them - all of them, the best of the Assyrians - and with whomever she lusted for. "She defiled herself with all their idols.
23.8: She never abandoned the immorality that she practiced in Egypt during her youth, where they laid down with her and fondled her virgin breasts, pouring out their favors all over her.
23.9: Therefore, I delivered her into the control of her lovers, that is, into the control of the Assyrians for whom she lusted.
23.10: They stripped her naked, took away her sons and daughters, and executed her with a sword. She became an object of ridicule among other nations when they punished her."
23.11: "Her sister Oholibah saw this, but she was more corrupt in her lust and sexual immorality than her sister had been in her own sexual immorality.
23.12: She lusted after the Assyrians-governors, commanders, warriors clothed in gorgeous attire, horsemen mounted on their cavalry - all of them desirable young men.
23.13: I saw that she was defiled, because the two of them both were on the same path.
23.14: "She increased her rate of sexual immorality when she saw the images of the Chaldean men who had been carved in red on their walls.
23.15: Girded with waistbands around their loins, with flowing turbans on their heads, all of them looked like chariot officers, similar to the Chaldean Babylonians, where they had been born.
23.16: "She lusted after them when she saw them, so she sent messengers to summon them from Chaldea.
23.17: The Babylonians came to her love nest and defiled her with their sexual immorality. As a result, she was defiled by them. Even so, she turned away from them in disgust.
23.18: She displayed her immorality publicly and stripped herself naked, so I turned away in disgust from her, just as I had turned away in disgust from her sister.
23.19: "Nevertheless, she increased the rate of her sexual immorality, even reminiscing about when she was young, when she kept on practicing sexual immorality in the land of Egypt.
23.20: She lusted after her paramours, whose genitals are like those of asses, and whose emissions are like those of horses.
23.21: Think about the wickedness that you practiced when you were young, when the Egyptians fondled your breasts, the breasts of your youth."
23.22: "Therefore, Oholibah, this is what the Lord God says： 'Look! I'm about to stir up your lovers against you, the ones from whom you've turned away in disgust. I'm going to bring them against you from every direction -
23.23: the Babylonians, all the Chaldeans, Pekod, Shoa, Koa, and all of the As syrians with them. They're all desirable young men, governors, commanders, chariot officers, and famous men, all of them mounted on horses.
23.24: "'They'll invade you with weapons, chariots, wagons, and a vast army. They'll set themselves in place to attack you from every direction with large shields, small shields, and helmet. I'll turn over judgment to them, and they'll punish you according to their own standards.
23.25: I'll expend my jealousy on you so they'll deal with you in anger. They'll cut off your noses and your ears. Your survivors will die by violence. They'll take your sons and daughters away from you, but your survivors will be incinerated with fire.
23.26: They'll strip off your clothes and confiscate your jewelry.
23.27: That's how I'll put an end to your obscene conduct and sexual immorality that you kept on practicing since the day you left the land of Egypt so that you won't look in their direction or even remember Egypt anymore.'
23.28: "This is what the Lord God says, 'I'm about to deliver you into the control of those you hate, into the control of them from whom you turned away in disgust.
23.29: They'll deal with you with hatred. They'll take away your productivity, leaving you naked and defenseless, so that the nakedness of your sexual immorality will be uncovered-your licentious sexual immorality.
23.30: These things will happen to you because of your sexual immorality that was patterned after after what the nations do. You've been defiled by their idols.
23.31: You took the path of your sister, so I'll place her cup in your hand.'"
23.32: "This is what the Lord God says： 'You'll drink from your sister's cup, which is both large and deep. You'll become a laughing stock and derision, since it's so full!.
23.33: You'll be filled with drunkenness and grief. The cup that belongs to your sister Samaria is a cup filled with horror and devastation,
23.34: but you'll drink from it and drain it completely. As to the vessel, you'll break it to pieces and you'll tear at your breasts, for I've spoken,' declares the Lord God."
23.35: "Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'Because you abandoned me and threw me behind your back, you will bear the consequences of your obscene conduct and sexual immorality."
23.36: Then the Lord spoke to me. "Son of Man, speak out in judgment of both Ohola and Oholiba. Make their detestable practices widely known,
23.37: because they've committed adultery, and blood coveres their hands. They've also committed adultery with their idols, making their sons born to me to pass through the fire as an offering to them.
23.38: "They've also done this to me： They defiled my sanctuary and profaned my Sabbaths, all at the same time!
23.39: When they killed their sons as offerings to their idols, time they brought them to my sanctuary and defile it. Look what they've done with my temple!
23.40: "In addition, they sent messengers for men to come from afar. When they arrived, you bathed yourself for them, painted your eyes, decked yourself with jewelry,
23.41: then sat down on an elegant bed. A table was arranged in front of it, on which you set out my incense and oil.
23.42: The sound of a carefree multitude accompanied her. Men from a multitude of peoples were coming-including Sabeans from the wilderness, adorned with bracelets on their hands and beautiful crowns on their heads.
23.43: "After she had worn herself out by her adulterous behavior, I asked her, 'Will they continue with their sexual immorality and with their prostitution?
23.44: They've gone to her, like men do, to have sex with a prostitute. They went in to Oholah and Oholibah, those licentious women.
23.45: Righteous men will judge them with punishment fit for adulterers and with punishment fit for those who shed blod, because they're adulterers with blood on their hands."
23.46: This is what the Lord God says： "Bring a an army against them and deliver them over to terror and plunder.
23.47: Then the army will stone them with stones and cut them to pieces with their swords. They'll kill their sons and daughters and incinerate their houses.
23.48: I'll cause obscene conduct to stop throughout the land, because all the women will be admonished not to practice their obscene conduct.
23.49: You'll receive the consequences for your obscene conduct and bear the punishment of your sins of idolatry. Then you'll know that I am the Lord God."

24.1: On the ninth year, in the tenth month, and on the tenth day of the month, this message came to me from the Lord：
24.2: "Son of Man, write down the name of this day, this very day. The king of Babylon has laid siege to Jerusalem on this very day.
24.3: So compose a parable to the rebellious house. Tell them, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Prepare your pot for boiling! Set it in place. Fill it up with water, too.
24.4: Gather together the best pieces of meat on it?including the thighs and the shoulders?and fill it with the choicest bones. Take the best bones from the flock, pile wood under the pot for the bones, bring it to a boil, and then cook the bones in it."'"
24.6: "This is what the Lord God says： 'How terrible it is for that blood-filled city, to the pot whose rust remains in it, whose rust won't come off. Empty it one piece at a time. Don't let a lot fall on it.
24.7: Her blood was in it. She poured it out onto bare rock. She didn't pour it out on the ground, intending to cover it with dirt. In order to stir up my anger And in order to take vengeance, I set the blood on a bare rock so that it cannot be covered.'
24.9: Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'How terrible it is for that blood-filled city?I'm also going to add to pile of kindling.
24.10: Pile up the wood! Make the fire burn hot. Boil the meat! Mix the seasonings. Burn those bones to a crisp!
24.11: Make the pot stand empty on the coals until its bronze glows red, its rust can be scoured off, and its dross completely removed.
24.12: The pot wearies me, but its thick rust won't come off, even with fire.
24.13: There is wickedness in your obscene conduct. Even though I've cleansed you, you uncleanness cannot be washed away. You cannot be cleansed again until my rage against you has subsided.'
24.14: I, the Lord have spoken. It will happen, because I'm going to do it. I won't hold back, have compassion, or change my mind. They'll judge you according to your ways and deeds,' declares the Lord God."
24.15: This message came to me from the Lord：
24.16: "Son of Man, pay attention! I'm about to take away your most precious treasure with a single, fatal stroke, but you are not to mourn, weep, nor even let tears well up in your eyes.
24.17: You are to weep in silence, but you are not to mourn the dead. You're to keep your turban on your head and your sandals on your feet. You are not to cover your mouth or eat what your comforters bring to you."
24.18: So I spoke to the people in the morning, and my wife died that evening. The next morning, I did as I had been commanded.
24.19: Then the people said to me, "Explain to us what you're doing should mean to us."
24.20: So I responded, "This message came to me from the Lord：
24.21: 'Tell the house of Israel that this is what the Lord God says： "Look! I'm about to profane my sanctuary, the pride of your strength, the desire of your eyes, and the object of your affection. Your sons and daughters, whom you've left behind, will die by the sword.
24.22: That's why you will soon be doing what I've just done. You are not to cover your mouth or eat what your comforters bring to you.
24.23: Your turbans will be on your heads and your sandals will be on your feet. You won't mourn or weep. Instead, you'll waste away in your sins. Every one of you will groan to his relative.
24.24: That's how Ezekiel will be an example for to you. You'll be doing exactly what he has done. When it happens, then you'll know that I am the Lord God."'2419
24.25: "And now, Son of Man, on the day that I take their strength, joy, and glory from them, those on whom they delight to keep their eyes, the focus of their affection-their sons and daughters -
24.26: at that time, a fugitive will come to you and will bring you the news.
24.27: Your mouth will freely speak to the fugitive. You won't be silent any longer. You'll be a sign to them. Then they'll know that I am the Lord."

25.1: This message came to me from the Lord：
25.2: "Son of Man, turn your attention to the descendants of Ammon and rebuke them.
25.3: Tell the Ammonites： 'Listen to a message from the Lord God! This is what the Lord God says： Because you have said, "Aha!" about my sanctuary when it was desecrated, about the land of Israel when it became desolate, and about the households of Judah when they went into exile,
25.4: therefore you'd better look out! I'm going to give you over to domination by men from the East. You will become their property. They will set up military encampments and permanent places in which to live among you, and then they'll eat your fruit and drink your milk.
25.5: I will turn Rabbah into a pasture for camels, and Ammon will become a resting place for flocks of sheep. That's how they'll learn that I am the Lord.'"
25.6: "This is what the Lord God says： 'Because you've applauded, stamped your feet, and rejoiced with all sorts of malice in your heart against the land of Israel,
25.7: therefore you'd better watch out! I'm raising a clenched fist in your direction! I'm about to feed you to the surrounding nations as war plunder. I'm going to eliminate you as a nation and kill off those of you who survive to live in other countries. I'm going to destroy you, and that's how you'll learn that I am the Lord.'"
25.8: "This is what the Lord God says： 'Because Moab and Seir are claiming, "Judah's citizens are just like every other nation,'
25.9: therefore you'd better watch out! I'm going to tear open Moab's flanks, starting with its frontier cities-the very glory of the nation! - including Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim.
25.10: I'm going to give these cities over to domination by men from the East. You will become their property. As a result, Ammon will be forgotten as a nation.
25.11: I'm also going to punish Moab, and that's how they'll learn that I am the Lord.'"
25.12: "This is what the Lord God says： 'Because Edom has made it their practice to seek extraordinary vengeance against Judah's citizens, and by doing so has incurred extraordinary guilt by taking revenge against them,'
25.13: therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'I'm going to raise my clenched fist in Edom's direction and eliminate every single human being and animal from Edom! I'm going to turn everything into a wasteland, starting with Teman, and Dedan will fall by violence!
25.14: 'I'm going to inundate Edom with my retribution, using my people Israel to carry it out! They'll deliver my anger, acting as an agent of my fury. Edom will come to know my vengeance,' declares the Lord God."
25.15: "This is what the Lord God says： 'Because Philistia has made it their practice to carry out retribution, accompanied by extraordinary malice in their personal vendettas-vendettas that spring from their everlasting hostility -
25.16: this is what the Lord God says： "Look out! I'm raising my clenched fist in Philistia's direction. I'm going to execute the Cherethites and destroy what's left of the coastline of the Mediterranean Sea.
25.17: I'll take vengeance on them, punishing them severely in my anger. They'll know that I am the Lord when I take my vengeance on them."'"

26.1: During the eleventh year, on the first day of the month of our captivity, this message came to me from the Lord：
26.2: "Son of Man, because Tyre has been saying about Jerusalem, "The international gateway is broken down, but it's wide open to me! I shall be replenished, now that it lies in ruins!
26.3: "Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'Watch out! I'm coming to get you, Tyre! I'm about to bring many nations to attack you. They'll come in wave after wave, like the breaking tide,
26.4: and will destroy the city walls of Tyre. After they break down her fortified towers, I'll scrape away the city's debris, right down to the bare bedrock,
26.5: and it will become a place where nets will be spread out right in the middle of the Mediterranean Sea. Because I have declared this to happen,' declares the Lord God, 'Tyre will be treated as the spoils of war by the invading nations.
26.6: Furthermore, her citizens who live on the mainland will be executed with swords. That's how they'll learn that I am the Lord.'"
26.7: "This is what the Lord God says： 'Watch out! I'm about to bring from the north King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon, that king of kings. He'll come with horses, chariots, cavalry, and a vast army.
26.8: He'll execute your citizens with swords who live on the mainland. He'll build siege engines to attack you. Then he'll construct siege ramps against you and build huge shields to protect themselves against you.
26.9: 'He'll direct the blows of his battering rams against your walls and will breach your fortified towers with axes.
26.10: There will be so many horses that the dust raised by them will cover you completely. The walls of your city will tremble from the noise of Nebuchadnezzar's cavalry, wagons, and chariots when they enter through the gates of your city, as men enter a city that has been breached.
26.11: 'Their horses will trample all the public places as he executes your inhabitants with swords. The most fortified of your pillars will be torn to the ground.
26.12: They will plunder your riches and loot your businesses. They'll tear down your walls and demolish your luxurious homes. They'll grab the stones, wood, and rubble from the destruction and dump it all into the Mediterranean Sea.
26.13: 'I'll silence the noise of your songs and the music of your harps won't be heard anymore.
26.14: I'll turn you into bare rock, and your city will become a place to spread nets. You will never be built again, because I the Lord have decreed this,' declares the Lord God."
26.15: "This is what the Lord God says to Tyre： 'When your wounded citizens groan while the slaughter takes place among you, the people who live in the coastlands will tremble in terror as they hear about your fall, will they not -
26.16: That's when all the kings of the seafaring nations will abandon their thrones, strip off their fancy clothes, and collapse trembling on the ground. They'll be so frightened as they observe what has happened to you that they'll be unable to stop trembling. They will be utterly appalled at you!
26.17: They'll sing this mourning song for you： "How lost you are, you inhabited city, that was built in the middle of the sea! How famous you were! How strong on the sea! She and her inhabitants inflicted terror to everyone who lived within her."'
26.18: 'Now the coastland inhabitants will tremble on the day that you fall. The coastland inhabitants, who make their living from the sea, will be terrified when you pass away!'"
26.19: "This is what the Lord God says： 'When I turn your city into a ghost town, when I flood you with deep waters that cover you completely,
26.20: I'll make sure that you go straight to the Pit, into the lowest part of the earth, where you'll be with people who lived in ancient times. You'll keep company there with the dead, who have gone into the Pit. As a result, your city won't be inhabited. Meanwhile, I will set my glory in the land of the living.
26.21: I'm going to send terrifying calamity in your direction, and you won't exist any longer. You might be sought after, but you'll never be found again,' declares the Lord God."

27.1: This message came to me from the Lord：
27.2: "Son of Man, compose a mourning song for Tyre.
27.3: Tell Tyre, who lives at the gateway to the Mediterranean Sea, who serves as the international merchant to many coastal districts： 'This is what the Lord God says： 'Tyre, you've been claiming, "I am beauty perfected."
27.4: 'You've set your national boundary in international waters. Your builders made you downright beautiful!'"
27.5: "'They brought in a ship made with pine planking from Senir, configured with a mast carved from a cedar from Lebanon,
27.6: equipped with oars made from oaks from Bashan, with ivory-inlaid cypress wood decking imported from the coastlands of Cypress,
27.7: with sails made with embroidered Egyption linen, festooned with blue banners, and with your sun shades made with purple cloth from Cypress.
27.8: Your sailors were conscripted from Sidon and Arvad, and your officers served aboard as pilots.
27.9: The wise men and elders from Gebal accompanied you, serving as ship's carpenters. All the martime navies and their seaman also accompanied you to assist you in doing business internationally.'"
27.10: "'Soldiers from Persia, Lud, and Lybia, served in your army. They were your mighty soldiers. Their helmets and shields adorned your barracks walls, and they won battle decorations for you.
27.11: Mercenaries from Arvad and Helech stood guard duty on your walls, while brave men manned your towers. They hung their shields all around your walls?just the right touch to perfect your interior decorating!'"
27.12: "'Tarshish was your business partner because of your phenomenal wealth. They traded silver, iron, tin, and lead for your merchandise.
27.13: Greece, Tubal, and Meshech bartered with you, exchanging slaves and bronze vessels for your wares.
27.14: Beth-togarmah traded horses, war horses, and mules in exchange for what you had to sell.
27.15: Men from the low country south of Edom and many of the coastlands were your markets for ivory tusks and ebony that they brought to trade with you.
27.16: 'Aram was one of your customers because you had so much merchandise. They paid by trading turquoise, purple yarn, embroidered goods, Egyptian linen, coral, and rubies.
27.17: The territories of Judah and Israel were your clients, too. They traded wheat from their distribution centers, baked goods, honey, oil, and ointments for your merchandise.
27.18: 'Because you have so much to sell and are so rich, Damascus has been your trading partner, exchanging wine from Helbon, unbleaced wool,
27.19: and casks of wine from Izal for your wrought iron, cassia wood, and aromatic reeds.
27.20: 'Dedan traded with you, exchanging riding blankets.
27.21: Arabia, including all the princes of Kedar, came to you, shopping for lambs, rams, and goats.
27.22: Traders from Sheba and Raamah paid for the best of what you had to offer with all types of spices, precious stones, and gold.
27.23: 'Haran, Canneh, Eden, merchants from Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad did business with you,
27.24: trading garments made into the finest blue and embroidered mantels, and also multi-colored carpets, ropes, and other merchandize.
27.25: Ocean-going fleets carried your merchandise. How filled you were! How glorious you were, at home in the heart of the sea!
27.26: But your rowers have brought you into dangerous waters. The east wind has broken you in the heart of the ocean!
27.27: Your wealth, your products, your merchandize your sailors, your pilots, your tailors, your salesmen, all your mercenaries with you?your entire company with you?will fall into the midst of the sea on the day when you're overthrown!
27.28: When your ships' captains cry out, the pasturelands along the coast will cry out!
27.29: Everyone who handles the oar will abandon ship, they'll head straight for dry land,
27.30: and they will cry so loud you won't be able to make yourself be heard! How bitterly they'll cry! They'll throw dust on their heads and wallow in ashes.
27.31: They'll shave their heads bald because of you. They'll dress themselves in sackcloth and weep for you with deep bitterness of heart, with the most pitiful of mourning.
27.32: In the depth of their despair they'll compose a lament for you. This is what they'll say： 'Who is like Tyre?Who is so silent in the midst of the sea -
27.33: Your merchandize went out over the oceans to satisfy many nations; with the abundance of your wealth you enriched the kings of the earth.'
27.34: But now it's your time to be wrecked at the bottom of the sea! Your products and your workers have sunk, and so have you!
27.35: Everyone who lives by the sea is appalled at your destruction. Their leaders are terrified?their faces reflect their fears!
27.336: Traders circulate among the people, hissing at you. What a horror you've become! Now you will cease to exist forever and ever!

28.1: This message came to me from the Lord：
28.2: "Son of Man, tell Tyre's commander-in-chief, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Because your heart is arrogant, and because you keep saying, 'I have taken my seat, I am a god, seated in God's seat right in the middle of the sea,' "and because you're a man, and not a god, even though you pretend that you have a god-like heart…
28.3: Look! You're wiser than Daniel, aren't you?No secret is too mysterious for you!
28.4: Your wisdom and understanding brought you phenomenal wealth. You've brought gold and silver into your treasuries.
28.5: By your great wisdom, by your skills in trading you have amassed wealth for yourself and your heart has become arrogant because of your wealth."'"
28.6: 'Therefore this is what the Lord God says： "Because you've made your heart like that of God
28.7: Therefore, look! I'm bringing foreigners in your direction, the most terrifying of nations! They will direct their violence against the grandeur that you've created by your wisdom.
28.8: They'll send you down to the Pit, and you'll die defiled in the depths of the sea.
28.9: Is that when you'll say "I'm God" to the face of those who will be killing you?After all, you're a man, and have never been a god, especially when you're under the control of those who will defile you!
28.10: You will die a death fit for the uncircumcised at the hand of foreigners." 'for I have said it will be so,' declares the Lord."
28.11: Another message came to me from the Lord, and this is what it said：
28.12: "Son of Man, start singing this lamentation for the king of Tyre. Tell him, 'This is what the Lord God says： "You served as my model, my example of complete wisdom and perfect beauty.
28.13: You used to be in Eden?God's paradise! You wore precious stones for clothing： ruby, topaz, diamond, beryl, onyx, jasper, sapphire, turquoise, and carbuncle. Your settings were crafted in gold, along with your engravings. On the day of your creation they had been prepared!
28.14: "You were the anointed cherub; having been set in place on the holy mountain of God, you walked in the midst of fiery stones.
28.15: You were blameless in your behavior from the day you were created until wickedness was discovered in you.
28.16: In the vastness of your trade that filled you with violence from top to bottom you sinned, so I cast you away as defiled from the mountain of God. I destroyed you, you guardian cherub, from the midst of the fiery stones.
28.17: Your heart grew arrogant because of your beauty; you annihilated your own wisdom because of your splendor. Then I threw you to the ground in the presence of kings, giving them a good look at you!
28.18: By all of your iniquity and unrighteous businesses you defiled your sanctuaries, so I'm going to bring out fire from within you and burn you to ashes on the earth before the whole watching world!
28.19: Everyone who knows you throughout all the nations will be appalled at your calamity and you shall no longer exist forever."'"
28.20: Another message came to me from the Lord, who had this to say：
28.21: "Son of Man, turn your attention to Sidon and prophesy against her.
28.22: Tell her： 'Pay attention to me, Sidon! I'm against you, and I'm going to glorify myself right in your midst.' They'll learn that I am the Lord when I bring about these punishments and manifest my holiness in her midst.
28.23: I'm going to send disease into that city and blood into her streets. People will drop dead in her midst from the violence done to her from every side. Then they'll learn that I am the Lord God.'"
28.24: "The house of Israel will never again suffer from painful briers and sharp thornbushes that surround them on every side, and they will learn that I am the Lord.
28.25: This is what the Lord God says： 'When I gather the house of Israel from the nations to which I've scattered them, I will show them my holiness before the watching world, and they will live on the land that I have to my servant Jacob.
28.26: They will live in safety in the land, building houses and planting vineyards. They'll live in safety while I administer judgment on everyone who maligns them among those who surround them. At that time they'll learn that I am the Lord their God.'"

29.1: In the tenth year, on the twelfth day of the tenth month, a message came to me from the Lord, who had this to say：
29.2: "Son of Man, turn your attention to Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and prophesy against him and the entire nation of Egypt.
29.3: Tell him that this is what the Lord God says： 'Watch out! I'm coming to get you, Pharaoah, king of Egypt! You big monster! You lay in wait in the middle of your waterways and say "My waterways belong to me! I made them for myself!"
29.4: 'So I'm going to plant a hook in your jaw and make the fish in your waterways grab hold of your scales. I'll bring you up out of the middle of your waterways, along with all of the fish from your waterways that cling to your scales,
29.5: Then I'll fling you out into the desert, you and all those fish in your waterways. You'll fall out in the open fields; you'll never be reunited. I'm giving you to the wild beasts of the earth and to the birds of the sky, and they will dine on you!
29.6: 'Then everyone living in Egypt will know that I am the Lord, because they have been an unreliable ally to the house of Israel.
29.7: When they reached out to you for support, you tore their hands and dislocated all of their shoulders. When they tried to lean on you. all of their loins became unstable.'
29.8: 'Therefore this is what the Lord God says： "Look out! I'm bringing violent death in your direction! I'm going to kill every person and animal,
29.9: and the land of Egypt will be turned into a desolate ruin. Then you will know that I am the Lord. "Because Egypt said, 'The Nile is mine. I made it!'
29.10: therefore watch out! I'm coming to get you! I'm going to attack your waterways, and then I'm going to make the land of Egypt a total wasteland from the Aswan fortress to the border of Ethiopia!
29.11: Neither man nor beast will walk through that area. It won't even be inhabited for 40 years.
29.12: I'll see to it that Egypt becomes a devastated land in the midst of devastated lands. Her cities deep inside her territories will be laid waste and desolate for 40 years. I will scatter Egypt among the nations and disperse them throughout the land.
29.13: "Because this is what the Lord says： 'At the end of 40 years I'll gather the Egyptians from the people among whom they have been scattered.
29.14: I'll restore the economy of Egypt and return them to the land of Pathros, from which they originated, and there they will remain an insignificant kingdom,
29.15: the least significant of kingdoms. It will never again dominate other nations. I will make them so small that they will never again rule any nation.
29.16: Egypt will never again be a source of confidence to the nation of Israel. Instead, Egypt will serve as a reminder of when they sinned by turning to Egypt for help. Then they'll know that I am the Lord God.'"
29.17: On the first day of the first month of the twenty-seventh year of our captivity, a message from the Lord came to me. It had this to say：
29.18: "Son of Man, Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, made his army work very hard to attack Tyre. They tore their hair out and rubbed their shoulders raw! Despite all of that work trying to capture Tyre, neither he nor his army got paid from Tyre for all that!
29.19: Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'Im going to give the land of Egypt to King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon. He's going to carry off Egypt's wealth, capture her war implements, confiscate her plunder, and use it to pay wages for his army!
29.20: I've given him the land of Egypt as a reward for attacking Tyre for me," declares the Lord God.
29.21: "When that day comes about, I'll strengthen Israel's military might, and I will give you an audience in their midst. Then they will know that I am the Lord.'"

30.1: Another message came to me from the Lord, who had this to say：
30.2: "Son of Man, here's what you are to prophesy and announce, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Wail out loud! Oh no! The day!
30.3: "For comes now the day?comes now the day of the Lord, the day of clouds! The time of the gentiles is fulfilled.
30.4: War shall come into Egypt, and Ethiopia will be in anguish when the slain fall in Egypt, when her wealth is carried off, and her foundations are demolished."
30.5: "Ethiopia, Lybia, descendants of Lud, all those who have mixed themselves, and Lybia - along with everyone in the land of Israel who is in league with them - will die violently.
30.6: "This is what the Lord says： 'Those who are supporting Egypt will fall; her majestic strength will collapse from the Aswan fortress by the sword that invades her,' declares the Lord God.
30.7: "They'll remain desolate among desolate lands, their cities will be named among those that are ruined.
30.8: They will know that I am the Lord when I kindle my fire in Egypt and all who help her are crushed.'
30.9: "When that happens, couriers will go out in ships to terrify Ethiopia in its complacency. Anguish will visit them as it will visit Egypt. Watch out! It's coming!
30.10: "This is what the Lord says： 'I'm putting an end to that gang from Egypt, and I'm going to use Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, to do it!
30.11: He and his ruthless army with him will be brought to destroy the land. They'll draw their swords and attack Egypt, filling the land with the dead!
30.12: I'll dry up their waterways and sell off the land by means of evil men. I'm going to make that land desolate, along with everything that's in it, and I'm going to do that by the hand of foreigners. I, the Lord have spoken!'
30.13: "This is what the Lord God says： 'I will destroy the idols and put an end to the images that come from Memphis. There will no longer be a prince from the land of Egypt, and I will make the land of Egypt to become terrified.
30.14: I'm going to turn Pathros into a desolation set fire to Zoan, and judge Thebes.
30.15: I'll pour out my anger on Sin, Egypt's strong fortress, and I'll eliminate the gangs in Thebes.
30.16: I'll set fire to Egypt, and Syene will write in agony. Thebes will be demolished, and Memphis will face daily distress.
30.17: The young men of On and Pi-beseth will die violently, and their cities will be taken captive.
30.18: It will be a dark day for Tahpanhes when I break the yokes of Egypt. That's when her arrogant power will come to an end. She'll be covered by a cloud, and her citizens will go into captivity.
30.19: I will carry out my judgments on Egypt, and they will learn that I am the Lord.'"
30.20: On the seventh day of the first month of the eleventh year of our captivity, a message came to me from the Lord. It had this to say：
30.21: "Son of Man, I've broken the arm of Pharaoh, king of Egypt. Look! It hasn't been set in a splint for healing or wrapped with a bandage so it could be strong enough to hold a sword!
30.22: Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'I'm coming to attack Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and I'm gong to break both of his arms, the strong one and the wounded one. That will make him drop his sword.
30.23: I'm going to scatter Egypt throughout the surrounding nations and disperse them throughout the world.
30.24: 'I'm going to strengthen the military might of the king of Babylon, put my own sword in his hand, and break Pharaoah's strength. Then Pharaoh will groan like a dying man right in front of the king of Babylon.
30.25: 'When I strengthen the military might of Babylon, the military might of Pharaoah will fail, and then they will learn that I am the Lord when I place my own sword in the hand of the king of Babylon. He will attack the land of Egypt.
30.26: When I scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them throughout the world, they will learn that I am the Lord.'"

31.1: On the first day of the third month of the eleventh year of our captivity, this message came to me from the Lord：
31.2: "Son of Man, tell this to Pharaoh, king of Egypt and his gangs： 'Who do you think you are?What makes you so great -
31.3: Think about Assyria, that cedar of Lebanon, beautiful with its branches, like a shady forest, awesome in its height, its summit touches the clouds.
31.4: Plentiful water made it great, Subterranean rivers made it grow. Rivers surrounded the area where it had been planted, and water channels nourished all the trees in the fields.
31.5: That's why it grew taller than any of the trees in the fields. Its boughs flourished. Its branches grew luxurious because of all the water that made it spread out.
31.6: The birds in the sky made nests in its boughs; all the beasts of the field gave birth under its branches. All the great nations rested in its shade.
31.7: 'Beautiful by virtue of its greatness, with its long branches, it was rooted in many waters.
31.8: The cedars in God's garden could not compare to it; Fir trees could not match its boughs. The plane tree never grew branches like it, and no tree in God's garden compares to its beauty.
31.9: I made it beautiful with all of its branches; all the trees in God's garden of Eden envied it!'"
31.10: "Therefore this is what the Lord God says： 'Because of its towering height, with its summit reaching into the clouds, and because it was haughty in its position,
31.11: I turned it over to domination by the leader of those nations, who dealt with it thoroughly. I have driven it away because of its wickedness.
31.12: 'Foreign dictators have trimmed it down to size and abandoned it. Its branches have fallen off on mountains and in all the valleys. Its boughts have broken off in all the ravines of the land. All the nations of the earth have moved out of its shade and abandoned it.
31.13: All the birds in the sky will live among its ruins, and the wild animals will forage among its branches.
31.14: 'As a result, none of its watered trees will grow tall, their tops will never reach to the clouds, and they'll never grow so high again, because all of them have been appointed to death in the world beneath where human beings go, that is, down to the Pit.'"
31.15: "This is what the Lord God says： 'On the day that it descended into Sheol, I shut down its water supplies, covered over its deep water, and shut down its rivers. As a result its abundant water sources dried up, and I caused Lebanon to mourn for it. All the trees of the field wilted because of it.
31.16: I made the nations tremble when they heard that Assyria was falling, descending into Sheol to join those who go down into the Pit. Then all of the trees of Eden in the world below were comforted, including the choicest and best of Lebanon, all of whom were well-watered.
31.17: They also went down with it into Sheol, to those who had been killed violently and to those who had trusted in its strength by living in its shadow among the nations.'
31.18: 'So tell me now, which of the trees of Eden compares to you in glory or greatness?Nevertheless, you'll be brought down, along with those trees of Eden, to the earth below. You'll lie in the middle of the uncircumcised, with those who have been killed in war. Pharaoh and all his gang will be just like this!'" declares the Lord God.

32.1: On the first day of the twelfth month of the twelfth year of our captivity, a message came to me from the Lord that had this to say：
32.2: "Son of Man, start singing this lamentation about Pharaoh, king of Egypt. Tell him, 'You may have called yourself a lion among nations, but you're a monster at sea. You thrash about in your rivers, muddy the water with your feet, and relieve yourself in the rivers.'
32.3: "This is what the Lord God says： 'I'm coming fishing for you! Right in the sight of many nations they'll haul you up in my dragnet.
32.4: I'll fling you up onto the land; I'll haul you onto the surface of the field, I'll make every carrion eating bird come to dine on you, and I'll make all the scavenging animals gorge themselves on you.
32.5: I'll cover the mountains with your flesh and fill their valleys with your rotting carcass.
32.6: I'll drench the land with your bloodflow right up to the mountains, and the ravines will overflow with blood that comes from you!
32.7: When I put out your lights, I'll cover the heavens and darken their stars. I'll cover the sun with a cloud and the moon won't reflect its light.
32.8: I'll darken the bright lights in the sky above you and bring darkness to your territory,' declares the Lord God."
32.9: "I'll bring distress to the hearts of many nations when I bring about your destruction among nations whose territories you have not known.
32.10: I'll make many nations be appalled at you, and their kings will be terrified because of you when I brandish my sword right in their face. They will all tremble from fear for their own safety on the day that you fall!
32.11: "This is what the Lord God says： 'The army of the king of Babylon will attack you.
32.12: I'm going to make your gangs die using the weapons of valiant warriors, all of whom are ruthless people. 'They will devastate the majesty of Egypt, destroying all of its hordes.
32.13: I'm going to destroy all of its livestock along its many riverbanks. Human feet won't muddy the rivers anymore, nor will the hooves of livestock churn the water.
32.14: That's when I'll make their waterways flow smoothly, and their rivers flow like olive oil,' declares the Lord God."
32.15: 'When I turn the land of Egypt into a desolation, and the land is emptied of everything that used to fill it, when I strike everything who lives there, they will learn that I am the Lord.'"
32.16: "This has been a lamentation. They will chant it, and the citizens of the nations will chant it, too. They'll chant it about Egypt and about all of its hordes."
32.17: In the twelfth year, on the fifteen day of the first month of our captivity, a message from the Lord came to me, and this is what it said:
32.18: "Son of Man, mourn about the hordes of Egypt. Bring them down-that is, her and the citizens of those majestic nations-whose destiny is the deep part of the Pit.
32.19: "So who's more beautiful than you?You'll be buried with the uncircumcised.
32.20: "They'll die along with others who are killed violently. Egypt has been given over to violence, which will carry off both it and its hordes."
32.21: "Mighty leaders will address them and those who assist them right out of the midst of Sheol： 'They've come down and will lie still, these uncircumcised people who have died violently.'
32.22: "Assyria will be there, along with all of those who keep company with her, all of them killed violently.
32.23: Her grave will be set in the remotest part of the Pit, surrounded by those who accompanied her. All of them will have been killed, executed by violence, who spread terror throughout the land of the living.
32.24: "Elam will be there. Its hordes will surround its grave. All of them have been killed. They fell violently, and they have descended uncircumcised into the world below after having spread terror throughout the land of the living. They will bear the shame of those who descend to the Pit.
32.25: "They have prepared a bed for her and for her hordes that surround her graves. All of them are uncircumcised, having been killed by violence, because they had spread terror throughout the land of the living. They will bear the shame of those who descend to the Pit and will take their place among the dead.
32.26: "Meshech and Tubal will be there, along with all of the hordes that surround her grave. Every one of them is uncircumcised, killed by violence, because they spread terror throughout the land of the living.
32.27: "They won't be buried with dead warriors from ancient times, who went straight to Sheol, buried with their war weapons, with their swords placed under their heads and their shields laid on top of their bones, since they spread terror throughout the land of the living.
32.28: You'll be broken, and you'll lie down with the uncircumcised who died by violence.
32.29: "Edom will be there, along with its kings and princes who despite all their power have been killed violently. They, too, lie with the uncircumcised; that is, with those who descend into the Pit.
32.30: "All of the princes from the North are there, along with the Sidonians, who have gone down in shame to join those who have been killed because of all the terror they caused by their military might. They lie uncircumcised with those who have been killed violently. They will bear their shame, along with those who descend into the Pit.
32.31: "When Pharaoh sees them, he will take comfort in his hordes. Pharaoah and all his army will die by violence," says the Lord God,
32.32: "because he spread terror throughout the land of the living. Therefore he'll be laid to rest among the uncircumcised, who have been killed violently; that is, Pharaoh and all of his hordes," declares the Lord God.

33.1: This message came to me from the Lord：
33.2: "Son of Man, speak to your nation's children and tell them： "'If I bring war to a land, and the people of that land appoint one of their conscripted men to serve as a sentinel,
33.3: and if he notices that violence is approaching and sounds an alarm to warn the people,
33.4: then if anyone who hears the sound of the alarm does not heed the warning, when the violence arrives and destroys him, his shed blood will remain his own responsibility.
33.5: After all, he heard the alarm sounding, but did not heed the warning, so his shed blood will remain his own responsibility. If he had heeded the warning, he would have saved himself.
33.6: If that sentinel notices that violence is approaching, but does not sound an alarm, then because the nation does not take warning and the violence arrives and destroys their lives because of their guilt, I'll seek retribution for their shed blood from the one who was acting as sentinel.'"
33.7: "'Now as for you, Son of Man, I've established you as a sentinel for the house of Israel. So whenever you hear a message from me, you are to warn them from me.
33.8: If I should say to a certain wicked person, "You wicked man, you're certainly about to die," but you don't warn him to turn from his wicked behavior, he'll die in his guilt, but I'll seek retribution for his bloodshed from you.
33.9: However, if you warn the wicked to turn from his behavior and he does not do so, he will die in his guilt, and you will have saved yourself.'"
33.10: "'Now, Son of Man, tell this to the house of Israel： "'You keep saying, "Our crimes and sins burden us so much that we're rotting away, so how can we keep on living?
33.11: "'Tell them, "As certainly as I'm alive and living," declares the Lord God, "I receive no pleasure in the death of the wicked. Instead, my pleasure is that the wicked repent from their behavior and live. Turn back! Turn back, all of you, from your wicked behavior! Why do you have to die, you house of Israel -
33.12: "'And now, Son of Man, say this to your people： "The righteousness of the righteous won't save them when they keep on committing crimes against me, the wickedness of the wicked won't keep them from remaining away when they're turning from their wickeness, and no righteous person shall keep living by their righteousness when they sin.
33.13: "If I tell the righteous that they will certainly live, if he trusts in his own righteousness and commits evil, none of his righteousness will be remembered, and he will die because of the wrong that he commits.
33.14: "If I tell the wicked that he will certainly die, if he turns from his sin and acts with justice and righteousness,
33.15: returning what has been placed as collateral for a loan, paying back what he has taken, following the regulations that demonstrate life, and committing no iniquity, he will certainly live, and not die.
33.16: None of the sins that he has committed will be remembered against him. In having done what is just and right, he will certainly live.
33.17: "Nevertheless, your people's children keep saying, 'The Lord's way of behaving isn't right,' when all the while it is their way of behaving that isn't right.
33.18: When a righteous man forsakes his own righteousness and commits evil acts, he will die because of those acts,
33.19: and when the wicked turn away from their wickedness and do what is just and right, he will certainly live because of that.
33.20: "And yet you keep saying, 'The Lord's way of behaving isn't right.' But I will judge every one of you according to his behavior, you house of Israel!"'"
33.21: On the fifth day of the tenth month of the twelfth year of our captivity, a fugitive who had escaped from Jerusalem came and informed me, "The city has been destroyed."
33.22: Now the hand of the Lord had been touching me the evening before that fugitive arrived, so the Lord had given me something to say by the time the messenger arrived the next morning. He opened my mouth and I no longer had nothing to say to him.
33.23: As a result, this message came to me from the Lord：
33.24: "Son of Man, those who are living among these ruins of the land of Israel keep saying, 'Abraham was only one man, but he was able to possess the land! As for us, we're a multitude, and the land has been given to us as an inheritance.'
33.25: "So tell them, 'This is what the Lord God says： "You keep eating flesh along with its blood, you keep looking to your idols, and you keep shedding blood, and you're going to take possession of the land -
33.26: You keep trusting in your weapons, you continue to commit loathsome things, men keep defiling their neighbors' wives, and you're going to take possession of the land -
33.27: "Tell them this： 'This is what the Lord God says： "As certainly as I'm alive and living, those who live in the wastelands are certain to die violently, I'll give those who die in the open fields to the wild animals for food, and whoever takes refuge in caves and fortified places will die of diseases.
33.28: Then I'll turn the land into a desolate ruin and her arrogant strength will come to an abrupt end. The mountains of Israel will become so desolate than no one will be able to travel over them.
33.29: Then they'll learn that I am the Lord, when I've turned their land into a desolate wasteland because of all of the loathsome things that they've done."'
33.30: "Now as for you, Son of Man, your nation's children keep gathering together to talk about you beside the walls and at the doorway to their houses. Everyone tells one another, 'Please come! Let's go hear what the Lord has to say!'
33.31: Then they come to you as a group, sit down right in front of you as if they were my people, hear your words-and then they don't do what you say?because they taste only their own desires, they pursue ill-gotten profits, and they keep following their own self-interests.
33.32: As far as they are concerned, you sing romantic songs with a beautiful voice and play a musical instrument well. They'll listen to what you have to say, but they won't put it into practice!
33.33: When all of this comes about-and you can be sure that it will! - they'll learn that a prophet has been in their midst."

34.1: A message came from the Lord for me, and it had this to say：
34.2: "Son of Man, prophesy against Israel's shepherds. Tell those shepherds, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Woe to you shepherds of Israel who have been feeding yourselves and not the sheep. Shouldn't shepherds feed the sheep -
34.3: You're eating the best parts, clothing yourselves with the weel, and slaughtering the home-grown sheep without having fed the sheep!
34.4: You haven't strengthened the weak, treated the sick, set broken bones, regathered the scattered, or looked for the lost. Instead, you've dominated them with brutal force and ruthlessness.
34.5: "Since they have no shepherd, they have been scattered around and have become prey for all sorts of wild animals. How scattered they are!
34.6: My sheep have gone wandering on all of the mountains, on all of the hills, and throughout every high place on the surface of the whole world, with no one to look for them or go out in search of them.
34.7: "Therefore listen to what the Lord says, you shepherds：
34.8: 'As certainly as I'm alive and living, my sheep have truly become victims, food for all of the wild animals because there are no shepherds. My shepherds did not go searching for my flock. Instead, the shepherds fed themselves, and my flock they would not feed!'
34.9: "Therefore, you shepherds, listen to what the Lord says： 'Watch out, I'm coming after you shepherds! I'm going to demand my sheep back from them and fire them as shepherds. The shepherds won't be shepherds anymore when I snatch my flock right out their mouths so they can't be eaten by them anymore.'"
34.11: "This is what the Lord says： 'Watch me! I'm going to search for my flock. I'll watch over them myself.
34.12: Just as a shepherd looks after his flock during the day time while he is with them, so also I'm going to watch over my sheep, delivering them from every place where they've been scattered during the times of gloom and doom.
34.13: I'm going to bring them out from foreign nations and from foreign lands. Then I'll bring them to their own land and feed them in Israel - on the mountains, in their valleys, and in all of their settlements throughout the land.
34.14: "'I'll feed them in excellent pastures, and even the very heights of Israel's mountains will serve as verdant pastures for them in which they'll rest and feed - yes, even on the fertile mountains of Israel!
34.15: I will feed my sheep and make them be at rest,' declares the Lord God.
34.16: "'I'm going to seek both the lost as well as the scattered, and bring them both back so their broken bones can be set and the sick can be healed. But I'll exterminate the far and the stiff-necked in righteousness.
34.17: "Now as for you, my flock, this is what the Lord God says： 'Watch out! I'm going to judge between one sheep and another, and between the rams and the goats.
34.18: Is it such an insignificant thing to you that you're feeding in good pastures but trampling down the other pastures with your feet?Or that you as you're drinking from the clear strems you're muddying the rest with your feet -
34.19: My flock is grazing on what you've been treading down with your feet and they're drinking what you've been making muddy with your feet!'
34.20: "Therefore this is what the Lord God says to them： 'Watch me! I'm going to judge between the fat sheep and the lean sheep,
34.21: since you've been bumping aside all the weaker sheep with your backsides and shoulders, butting them with your horns until they're scattered around outside.
34.22: That's how I'll save my sheep so they won't be plundered any longer. I'm going to judge between one sheep and another.'"
34.23: "'Then I'll install one shepherd for them-my servant David-and he will feed them, will be there for them, and will serve as their shepherd.
34.24: I, the Lord, will be their God, and my servant David will rule among them as Prince.' I, the Lord, have spoken this.
34.25: "I'm going to enter into a covenant with them, one of peace, and I'll eliminate wild beasts from the land so they can live securely in the wilderness and sleep in the forests.
34.26: I'm going to make them and everything that surrounds my hill a blessing. I'll send down the rain! At the appropriate time there will be a rainstorm of blessing!
34.27: "I'll bring fruit to the trees in the fields, the land will yield its produce, they will live securely on their land, and they will learn that I am the Lord when I break the bar that has been their yoke and deliver them from the control of those who have enslaved them.
34.28: They will no longer be plundered by the nations, and wild animals that live on the earth will no longer devour them. They will settle down in confidence with nothing to frighten them.
34.29: I'm going to prepare for them the best of gardening spots. They will no longer live as victims in a land of starvation, nor will they have to bear the insults of the international community.
34.30: That's when they'll learn that I, the Lord their God, am with them, and that they, the house of Israel, are my people,' declares the Lord God.
34.31: "And as for you, my sheep, the flock that I'm pasturing, you are mankind, and I am your God," declares the Lord God.

35.1: A message came to me from the Lord and it went like this：
35.2: "Son of Man, turn your attention toward Mount Seir and begin to prophesy against it.
35.3: Tell them, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Watch out! I'm coming to get you, Mount Seir! I'm stretching out my hand to strike you, and I'm going to turn you into a desolate wasteland.
35.4: I'm going to turn your cities into ghost towns, and you will become a ruin. Then you will learn that I am the Lord.
35.5: Because of your undying hatred, you kept on delivering the Israelites to violent abuse during the time of their calamity, even when they were in their final stages of punishment,
35.6: therefore as I'm alive and living," declares the Lord God, I'm turning you over to bloodshed, and bloodshed will certainly overtake you, since you never have hated shedding blood. That's why bloodshed will certainly pursue you.
35.7: "I'm turning Mount Seir over to ruin and desolation. I'm going to eliminate everyone who comes and goes,
35.8: and I'll fill his mountain with the dead. Those who die by violence will cover your hills, and fill your valleys and all your ravines!
35.9: I will turn you into an everlasting wasteland, and your cities will never be inhabited. Then you'll learn that I am the Lord!
35.10: "Because you have claimed, 'These two nations and these two lands are going to belong to me, and we will take possession of them, even though the Lord is there,'
35.11: therefore as I'm alive and living" declares the Lord God, "I'm going to deal with you as your anger deserves. When I judge you, I'll treat you like you did them-that is, with the same kind of envy that motivated your constant hatred against them.
35.12: "That's how you'll know that I, the Lord, have heard every loathsome, reviling thing that you've had to say against the mountains of Israel, such as 'They're desolate, and we'll eat them for dinner!'
35.13: Not only that, you've arrogantly reviled me many times over, and I've heard every word!
35.14: "So this is what the Lord God says： 'Just as the earth rejoices, I'm going to turn you into a desolate wasteland.
35.15: Just as you rejoiced when Israel's inheritance became desolate, I'm going to do the same thing to you. Mount Seir, you and Edom-all of you-will become a desolate wasteland.' Then they will learn that I am the Lord."'"

36.1: "And now as for you, Son of Man, prophesy to Israel's mountains and tell them, 'Listen to this message from the Lord, you mountains of Israel:
36.2: "This is what the Lord God says： 'The enemy has been saying about you, "Good! The ancient heights are back in our possession!"
36.3: Therefore this is what you are to prophesy： "Here's what the Lord God says, 'You've been turned into a desolate wasteland and crushed by everyone who surrounds you for a very, very good reason. You've become the property of all the other nations and you've become the object of gossip and whispering campaigns of the nations.
36.4: "Therefore listen to what the Lord God has to say, you mountains of Israel： This is what the Lord God says to the mountains, to the hills, to the waterways, to the valleys, to the desolate wastelands, and to the abandoned cities that have become a target of derision to the mountains that surround you：
36.5: "'Because this is what the Lord God says： "Motivated by my burning zealousness, I have spoken againt the rest of the surrounding nations, including Edom, who confiscated my land, taking possession of it with joyful enthusiasm and with animal-like malice, in order to plunder Israel's pastures.
36.6: "Therefore prophesy concerning the land of Israel and speak to its mountains, hills, ravines, and valleys. Tell them 'This is what the Lord God says： "Pay attention! In my zealous anger I'm speaking because you've had to endure the mockery of the world's nations."
36.7: 'Therefore this is what the Lord God says： "I hereby raise my hand to swear this oath： the nations that surround you will have their own mockery to endure!
36.8: But you mountains of Israel are going to sprout branches and bear fruit for my people Israel, because they'll be coming soon.
36.9: "Watch me! I'm on your side! I'll be turning in your direction, and you mountains will be plowed and planted.
36.10: I'm going to make the entire house of Israel grow-every single member-and their cities will be inhabited with all the ruins rebuilt.
36.11: I'll make both the population and the livestock increase throughout your territories. They'll increase and be fruitful. I'll make your territories to be settled like you were in the past, and you will be treated better than you ever were before. At that time you will know that I am the Lord.
36.12: "I'll lead my people, my nation of Israel, across you mountains, and they will take possession of you again, and you'll be their inheritance once more. Never again will you leave them robbed of children.
36.13: "This is what the Lord God says： 'Because some have been saying to you, "You mountains have been devouring human beings and leaving people childless,"
36.14: 'therefore you will no longer devour human beings or leave their nation childless,' declares the Lord God.
36.15: 'I won't let you hear other people mock you, and no nation will ever make you childless again,' declares the Lord God.
36.16: This message came to me from the Lord：
36.17: "Son of Man, when the house of Israel was living on their own land, they defiled it with their lifestyles and behavior; they were as disqualified to be with me as a menstruating woman is to you.
36.18: So I poured out my anger on them because of all the bloodshed throughout the land and because they had defiled it with their idolatry.
36.19: Then I scattered them among the nations, dispersing them to other lands, just as their lifestyles and behavior deserved. That's how I judged them.
36.20: Nevertheless, when they arrived in those nations, they continued to profane my holy name. It was said about them, 'These are the Lord's people, even though they've left his land.'
36.21: I've been concerned about my holy reputation, which the house of Israel has been defiling throughout all of the nations where they've gone.
36.22: "Therefore tell the house of Israel, 'This is what the Lord God says： "I'm not about to act for your sake, you house of Israel, but for the sake of my holy reputation, which you have been defiling throughout all of the nations where you've gone.
36.23: I'm going to affirm my great reputation that has been defiled among the nations (that is, that you have defiled in their midst), and those people will learn that I am the Lord," declares the Lord God, 'when I affirm my holiness in front of their very eyes.
36.24: I'm going to remove you from the nations, gather you from all of the territories, and bring you all back to your own land.
36.25: I'll sprinkle pure water on you all, and you'll be cleansed from your impurity and from all of your idols.
36.26: "I'm going to give you a new heart, and I'm going to give you a new spirit within all of your deepest parts. I'll remove that rock-hard heart of yours and replace it with one that's sensitive to me.
36.27: I'll place my spirit within you, empowering you to live according to my regulations and to keep my just decrees.
36.28: You'll live in the land that I gave to your ancestors, you'll be my people, and I will be your God.
36.29: In addition, I'll deliver you from everything that makes you unclean. I'll call out to the grain you plant, ordering it to produce abundant yields, and I will never bring famine in your direction.
36.30: I'll increase the yields of your fruit trees and crops so that you'll never again experience the disgrace of famine that occurs in other nations.
36.31: "Then you'll remember your lifestyles and practicies that were not good. You will hate yourelves as you look at your own iniquities and loathsome practices.
36.32: I won't be doing any of this for your sake," declares the Lord God, "so keep that in mind. Be ashamed and frustrated because of your behavior, you house of Israel!"
36.33: 'This is what the Lord God says： "At the same time that I cleanse you from all of your guilt, I'll make your cities become inhabited again and the desolate wastelands be rebuilt.
36.34: The desolate fields will be cultivated, replacing the former wasteland that everyone who passed by in times past had noticed.
36.35: They will say, 'This wasteland has become like the garden of Eden, and what used to be desolate ruins are now fully built and inhabited.
36.36: "Then the surviving people that live around you will learn that I, the Lord, have rebuilt these ruins and have cultivated these pastures that used to be desolate wastelands. I, the Lord, have spoken this, and I'm going to bring it about!"
36.37: "This is what the Lord God has to say： 'I'm going to allow the house of Israel to ask anything they want from me, including this： I'm going to increase their population as a shepherd increases his flock.
36.38: The desolate cities will be filled with flocks of human being, just like Jerusalem used to be filed with flocks of sheep during the times of the appointed feasts. Then they will know that I am the Lord.'"

37.1: The Lord laid his hand on me and brought me out by the Spirit of the Lord to the middle of a valley that was filled with bones.
37.2: He led me up and down throughout the valley, and I was amazed to see that the surface of the entire valley was covered with myriads of very dry bones!
37.3: The Lord asked me, "Son of Man, will these bones ever live? "Lord God," I replied, "you know the answer to that!"
37.4: Then the Lord told me, "Prophesy to these bones. Tell them： 'You dry bones, listen to the message from the Lord：
37.5: This is what the Lord God says to you dry bones! 'Pay attention! I'm bringing my Spirit into you right now, and you're going to live!
37.6: I'm going to grow tendons on you, regenerate your flesh, cover you with skin, and make you breathe again so that you can come alive and learn that I am the Lord.'"
37.7: So I prophesied, just as I had been ordered to do so. Immediately there was a noise and a rattling-and then all of a sudden the bones came together by themselves! Each bone came together, one to another!
37.8: As I continued to watch, I saw tendons growing on the bones, and muscles growing up to cover them, and then skin covered the flesh from above. But the bodies weren't breathing.
37.9: Then he ordered me, "Prophesy to the Spirit, Son of Man. Tell the Spirit, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Come from the four winds, you Spirit, and breathe into these people who have been killed, so they will live."'"
37.10: So I prophesied as I had been ordred, and breath entered into them, and they began to live. They stood on their own feet as a vast, united army.
37.11: "These bones represent the entire house of Israel," the Lord explained to me. "Look how they keep saying, 'Our bones are dried up, and our future is lost. We've been completely eliminated!'
37.12: "Therefore prophesy to them, and tell them, 'Watch me! I'm going to open your graves, lift you out of those graves, and bring my people back into the land of Israel.
37.13: Then you'll learn that I am the Lord, when I've opened your graves and caused you to come up out of them, my people.
37.14: I'm going to place my spirit in you all, and you will live. I'll place you all into your land, and you'll learn that I, the Lord, have been speaking and doing this,' declares the Lord."
37.15: A message came to me from the Lord, and it had this to say：
37.16: "Now as for you, Son of Man, grab a stick of wood for yourself and write on it these words： For Judah and the Israelites, his associates Then grab another stick and write on it： For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim, and all the house of Israel, his associates
37.17: Then join them together end-to-end for yourself so that they become a single baton in your hand.
37.18: "When the descendants of your people ask you 'Would you please explain to us what you mean by this?
37.19: you are to tell them, 'This is what the Lord says： "Watch me! I'm taking the stick that represents Joseph, which Ephraim is holding in his hand, along with his companions the tribes of Israel, and I'm going to join them with the stick that is Judah. I'm making them a single stick, that is, a complete baton in my hand."'
37.20: "The sticks on which you engrave your writing are to remain right in front of them in your hand.
37.21: Then tell them, 'This is what the Lord God says： "Watch me take the Israelites out of the nations where they've gone and return them from every direction. I'm going to bring them back into their own land.
37.22: I'm going to make them a united people in the land, on the mountains of Israel, and I'll set a single king to rule over them. They'll never again be two separate people. They'll never again be divided into two kingdoms.
37.23: "'"They will never again defile themselves with their idols, with other loathsome things, or with any of their sins. Instead, I will deliver them from all of the places where they have sinned, and then I'll cleanse them. They will be my people and I will be their God."'"
37.24: "'"My servant King David will be there for them, and one shepherd will be appointed for them. They will live in my decrees, keep my regulations, and practice them.
37.25: They will live in the land that I gave to my servant Jacob and on which your ancestors lived. They will live in that land, along with their children and grandchildren, forever. David my servant will be their everlasting leader.
